Absolution | Description | Absolution is a skill that damages enemies in an area , applying a debuff for a short duration. If a non- unique enemy dies while affected by the debuff, the enemy's corpse will be consumed to summon an allied Sentinel of Absolution minion for a longer secondary duration, or to refresh the duration and life of an existing one instead if you have the maximum number of them.
Absolution | Sentinel of Absolution | A Sentinel of Absolution , according to the datamined material, is a reskinned Temple Adjudicator (which itself has another variant Heretical Adjudicator ). [1]

Absolution of Inspiring | Description | Absolution of Inspiring is a skill that damages enemies in an area , applying a debuff for a short duration. If a non- unique enemy dies while affected by the debuff, the enemy's corpse will be consumed to summon a friendly Sentinel of Absolution minion for a longer secondary duration or to refresh the duration and life of an existing one instead if you have the maximum number of them.
Absolution of Inspiring | Description | It is a transfigured version of Absolution.
Absolution of Inspiring | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version has a lower maximum minion count, but allows the non-minion portion of the skill to be scaled by minion damage with a stronger version of Spiritual Aid.
Absolution of Inspiring | Item acquisition | Absolution of Inspiring has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

Alchemist's Mark | Description | Alchemist's Mark is a spell that marks a single target with a curse. Hits against the marked target grant flask charges and when you ignite or poison the marked enemy, it leaves behind burning ground or caustic ground under them, respectively.
Alchemist's Mark | Skill Functions and Interactions | Elemental Proliferation : Ignites that are proliferated from a nearby enemy or corpse to the marked target will not trigger the burning ground.
Alchemist's Mark | Skill Functions and Interactions | Flask charges : Gain 1 Flask Charge when you Hit a Cursed Enemy grants charges to all flasks. [1]
Alchemist's Mark | Skill Functions and Interactions | Proxies : hits from proxies , such as totems or traps , are unable to create this curse's ground effects.
Alchemist's Mark | Skill Functions and Interactions | The duration of the burning and caustic ground are independent of the duration of the ignite or poison that applied it, making it work well with builds that use high-damage, low-duration ignite/poisons.

Ancestral Call Support | Description | Ancestral Call is a support gem. Strike skills that are linked to Ancestral Call will target two additional nearby enemies at the same time, automatically selecting valid targets within range for the attacks.
Ancestral Call Support | Skill functions and interactions | The effect that targets additional nearby enemies can only target enemies outside the strike range of the attacker's own animation. It has a minimum and a maximum range; an enemy cannot be too close or too far from the attacker to be targeted. [1] The base maximum appears to be approximately 3.5 metres, and can be increased by this support's gem quality and the anoint -only Sione's Ambition. The targeting range is unrelated to weapon range or melee strike range.
Ancestral Call Support | Skill functions and interactions | The 'Ancestors' summoned at targeted enemies are client-side visual effects, not separate entities. Effects like damage reflection and leech will apply to the player normally. [2] [3]
Ancestral Call Support | Skill functions and interactions | When an additional nearby enemy is targeted, the strike against them can also hit other enemies (typically behind the target), depending on the skill's weapon range. While strike range is normally small, it can be raised through modifiers, which some skills may have by default (e.g. Frost Blades of Katabasis ).
Ancestral Call Support | Skill functions and interactions | Because Strike skills perform one critical strike roll per attack, hits against additional enemies require the same chance to crit as the player's own strike.

Animate Guardian | Infobox | Spell , Minion Level: (1-20) Cost: (11-20) Mana Cast Time: 0.60 sec. Requires Level 28. Animates a melee weapon or armour, attaching it to an invisible Guardian that fights by your side. Animating multiple items attaches them to the same Guardian. You cannot animate unidentified items. Cannot be used by Traps or Mines. If your Guardian dies you cannot summon an Animated Guardian again in that area. Minions have (0-38)% more Maximum Life Can use Items requiring up to Level (33-100) Minion deals (0-38)% more Damage Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: (2-40)% increased Minion Movement Speed. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 28
Animate Guardian | Description | Animate Guardian is a spell , animating weapons and armour on the ground to form an allied minion.
Animate Guardian | Skill functions and interactions | Using this skill on an identified item on the ground creates an Animated Guardian that uses a default melee strike skill based on the Guardian's equipped weapon or unarmed. Similar to Spectres and unlike other minions , a Guardian is persistent even after logging out on a character. Unequipping the Animate Guardian gem will cause the Guardian to disappear until an Animate Guardian gem is equipped again and the spell is cast (animating an item is not required to resummon a previously existing Guardian). As the Animate Guardian's equipment is specific to a character , the gem itself can be freely replaced without any detrimental effects. Socketing the gem in a weapon or shield slot allows players to quickly unsummon a Guardian at will by switching weapon sets (default hotkey: X ). You can only have one Guardian at a time.
Animate Guardian | Skill functions and interactions | Animated Guardian can be equipped with:
Animate Guardian | Skill functions and interactions | Ranged weapons cannot be animated, similar to default Animate Weapon. Items can be of any rarity , including unique , but must be identified and on the ground, and of an appropriate level requirement to be equipped. Once a weapon is consumed by the spell and equipped to the Animated Guardian, the item is permanently destroyed and cannot be reobtained. Caution is advised to avoid accidental animation or overwriting of valuable items. Items containing socketed gems cannot be animated, and the Animated Guardian cannot use or trigger skills from gems or items.
Animate Guardian | Skill functions and interactions | The Guardian uses weapons and shields in the same way as player characters, and can dual wield one-handed weapons. If you animate an item type that the Guardian is already using (such as a helmet), the new item will overwrite the old one. Equipping one-handed weapons will alternate equipping weapons between the left and right hand on subsequent casts. Equipping an armour equipment item restores 25% of the minion's maximum life.
Animate Guardian | Skill functions and interactions | Generally speaking an item cannot be "unequiped", leaving an empty slot, once it has been filled, the exception being you can empty one of your AGs hands by cycling between a Two Handed Melee Weapon and a One Handed Melee Weapon or a Shield depending on which hand you need to be free. For example if you need to empty your AG Main Hand to allow for Facebreaker override a Two Handed Weapon with a Shield, or empty your Off Hand to use White Wind bonuses. The Goddess Bound also deletes the Off Hand slot equipment.
Animate Guardian | Skill functions and interactions | Most modifiers on the items themselves apply to the Animated Guardian the same way they apply to player characters, including enchantments , corrupted implicit modifiers , Scourge modifiers , and Crucible passive trees. Modifiers which allocate notable passive skills do not apply as minions do not have access to a passive tree; modifiers that grant keystone effects do function however. Eldritch implicit modifiers that require unique enemies or pinnacle atlas bosses to be in your presence to activate are always active on Animated Guardians.
Animate Guardian | Skill functions and interactions | Items equipped by the Guardian are not deleted when it dies and will be retained when resummoned. However, it cannot be resummoned in the same area it died in (unless that area is a hideout ). Note that with Catarina Bloodline 's Prolonged Servitude , it is possible to unsocket the Animate Guardian gem during the 6 second grace period and resocket it to be able to cast the skill again, resummoning it without any penalty.
Animate Guardian | Skill functions and interactions | Like other minions, the Guardian does not share your curse limit , and has a default curse limit of 1 which can be increased through equipped items. They can apply curses through Curse Enemies with <curse> on Hit modifiers.
Animate Guardian | Skill functions and interactions | Character-bound : The Guardian's items are tied to the character that summoned it, rather than the gem itself. The gem can freely be changed around without risking loss of the Animate Guardian's equipment. This also means that trading or moving an Animate Guardian gem to another character will not transfer the Animate Guardian's equipment between players/characters.
Animate Guardian | Skill functions and interactions | Gem level : Note that the skill's gem level determines the maximum level requirement of items the Animated Guardian can equip; if the gem's level is too low for an equipped item, it will disable the item temporarily until it is high enough gem level, at which point it will function normally. This does not delete the equipped item.
Animate Guardian | Skill functions and interactions | Item skills : Animated Guardians cannot use skills granted by items. Equipping items like Death's Oath , The Rippling Thoughts , or Bubonic Trail will not allow the Guardian to use their associated triggered skills. This also includes Fire Burst granted by Essence of Hysteria , even though it does not contain "trigger" or "granted" in its modifier description.
Animate Guardian | Skill functions and interactions | Ally effects : Like other minions, the Guardian is considered an ally but not a party member. For example, the Guardian cannot benefit from a character with Your nearby party members maximum Endurance Charges is equal to yours from Ambu's Charge , or vice versa. However, other effects that apply to nearby allies or enemies, like Leer Cast or Dying Breath , will apply as normal.
Animate Guardian | Skill functions and interactions | Necromantic Aegis : While using Necromantic Aegis your Guardian benefits from its own shield (if applicable) as well as the one you have equipped, allowing it to gain the benefit of two shields at once. Some unique shields can have powerful interactions when doubled up this way. [1]
Animate Guardian | Skill functions and interactions | Abyssal Sockets : Although it is possible to Animate equipment with Abyss jewels socketed into Abyssal sockets , Animated Guardians do not benefit from abyss jewel modifiers or modifiers that count abyss jewels. For example, equipping a Tombfist or Bubonic Trail with abyss jewel(s) socketed will not grant their conditional modifiers to a Guardian.
Animate Guardian | Skill functions and interactions | Player versus Player : Guardians have poor survivability in PvP areas due to modified damage scaling for skills/minions. Because of this, they are not generally recommended to be used in Hall of Grandmasters and other PvP areas, though after the change in version 3.26.0 where they no longer have their equipped items deleted on death, this is less of an issue.

Animate Guardian of Smiting | Infobox | Spell , Minion , Lightning Level: (1-20) Cost: (11-20) Mana Cast Time: 0.60 sec. Requires Level 28. Animates a melee weapon or armour, attaching it to an invisible Guardian that fights by your side using Smite of Divine Judgement, ignoring weapon restrictions. Animating multiple items attaches them to the same Guardian. You cannot animate unidentified items. Cannot be used by Traps or Mines. If your Guardian dies you cannot summon an Animated Guardian again in that area. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop restricted Drop level: 28 This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.
Animate Guardian of Smiting | Description | Animate Guardian of Smiting is a spell , animating weapons and armour on the ground to form an allied minion that uses a transfigured version of Smite.
Animate Guardian of Smiting | Description | It is a transfigured version of Animate Guardian.
Animate Guardian of Smiting | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version does not give the Animated Guardian increased life , but is able to use Smite of Divine Judgement , a transfigured version of Smite , which lacks the aura buff component. Animate Guardian ignores the skill's weapon restrictions, allowing it to use the skill with any melee weapon or while unarmed.
Animate Guardian of Smiting | Skill functions and interactions | The equipment of all Animate Guardian skills is tied to the summoner, not a specific gem or skill type. Animate Guardian and Animate Guardian of Smiting effectively share the same set of items, which is unaffected by changing from one gem to another (assuming all gems are high enough level).
Animate Guardian of Smiting | Item acquisition | Animate Guardian of Smiting has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

Animate Weapon | Infobox | Duration , Minion , Spell , Physical Level: (1-20) Cost: (4-16) Mana Cast Time: 0.60 sec. Requires Level 4. Animates a Melee Weapon Item or Lingering Blade to fight by your side. You cannot animate unidentified Weapons. Will not animate weapons with 6 sockets. Cannot be used by Traps or Mines. Base duration is 40.00 seconds Maximum (5-14) Animated Weapons Can use Items requiring up to Level (9-100) Minions' Attacks deal (5-126) to (8-184) additional Physical Damage Animated Lingering Blades deal (1-17) to (2-80) Added Attack Physical Damage Minions deal (0-38)% more Damage Minions have (0-38)% more Attack Speed Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: +(0.1-2) to Maximum Animated Weapons. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 5
Animate Weapon | Description | Animate Weapon is a spell that animates a weapon on the ground as a flying allied minion.
Animate Weapon | Skill functions | Animate Weapon can be used on identified melee weapons lying on the ground. Bows and Wands cannot be animated unless the threshold jewel Spirit Guards is being used. Animated weapons are permanently consumed and cannot be recovered. If the "Key Pickup" option is enabled in the UI tab of the Options menu, the "Highlight items and objects" key may be held in order to animate specific weapons. Otherwise, the skill will attempt to animate a random valid weapon within a small area around the player's cursor. Attempting to animate a weapon with a level requirement higher than the maximum listed on the gem will cost mana and perform the cast animation, but will fail to create a minion.
Animate Weapon | Skill functions | Animate Weapon can also be used on Lingering Blades left behind by Bladefall and Ethereal Knives of Lingering Blades.
Animate Weapon | Skill functions | Animated Weapons are minions that are considered to be using the original weapon animated. They are therefore affected by modifiers to minions, as well as support gems for weapons, such as Elemental Damage with Attacks Support , but not affected by any of the player's passive tree for specific weapons.
Animate Weapon | Skill functions | All modifiers on the weapon item itself apply to the minions once animated, including enchantments , corrupted implicit modifiers , Scourge modifiers , and Crucible passive trees (except modifiers which allocate player notable passive skills , as minions do not have access to your passive tree).
Animate Weapon | Calculating damage | There are different calculations for weapons summoned from items on the ground and lingering blades from spells.
Animate Weapon | Casting on a weapon | Weapons inherit the hit damage, attack speed and critical strike chance of the weapon they are summoned from. This includes any direct modifiers to base stats, such as 108% increased Physical Damage , which are factored into the base damage of the minion.
Animate Weapon | Casting on a weapon | The minion then gains additional Physical damage based on gem level, which is not scaled by weapon local modifiers. The additional damage for Lingering Blades does not apply to animated itemized weapons.
Animate Weapon | Casting on a weapon | Other weapon-specific stats found on the weapon (such as Attacks with this Weapon Penetrate 12% Elemental Resistances or 25% increased Elemental Damage with Attacks are additive with similar sources of stats for minions.
Animate Weapon | Casting on a weapon | Note that modifiers to Minions on animated weapons will have no effect on the Animate Weapon minion itself, as Animated Weapon is considered using the weapon, and has no minions of its own. The only exception to this would be when animating The Scourge.
Animate Weapon | Casting on a Lingering Blade | Animated weapons created from Lingering Blades have similar base stats to those of a Glass Shank. [1]
Animate Weapon | Casting on a Lingering Blade | The same formula is used as when it is cast on a weapon item, except that it also gains additional flat damage on top of the minion's inherent flat damage modifier. This counts as added damage.

Animate Weapon of Ranged Arms | Description | It is a transfigured version of Animate Weapon.
Animate Weapon of Ranged Arms | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version can only animate bows or wands (similar to Spirit Guards ), or Lingering Blades. Animated weapons created from Lingering Blades have similar base stats to those of a Crude Bow instead of a Glass Shank. The skill has a slower cast time and higher cost.

Animate Weapon of Self Reflection | Infobox | Duration , Minion , Spell Level: (1-20) Cost: (4-16) Mana Cast Time: 0.60 sec. Requires Level 4. Animates a copy of your main hand Melee Weapon to fight by your side. Cannot be used by Totems, Traps or Mines. Base duration is 15.00 seconds Maximum 6 Animated Weapons Can use Items requiring up to Level (9-100) Minions deal (0-38)% more Damage Minions have (0-38)% more Attack Speed Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: +(0.05-1) to Maximum Animated Weapons. Animate Weapon of Self Reflection | Description | Animate Weapon of Self Reflection is a spell that animates melee weapons based on your own equipped weapon.
Animate Weapon of Self Reflection | Description | It is a transfigured version of Animate Weapon.
Animate Weapon of Self Reflection | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version can only animate a copy of your main hand melee weapon and cannot animate items on the ground or Lingering Blades. It cannot be used by a totem. It also has lower duration, no added physical damage, and does not gain additional maximum animated weapons at higher levels.
Animate Weapon of Self Reflection | Skill functions and interactions | Animated Weapons can proc The Scourge 's legacy summon wolf on kill mod. Animated Weapons with Energy Blade active have base damage based on time of cast. If your main weapon becomes a non-melee weapon via toggling off Energy Blade or switching weapon set, the summons will die. Animated Weapons will snapshot your melee weapon if you weapon swap to another melee weapon. For example, you can animate Alternating Sceptre and weapon swap to Rakiata's Dance in order to apply alternate ailments with 1 weapon and animate the rest as Rakiata's Dance; however, if you weapon swap to a ranged weapon, your weapons will disappear. Using Ephemeral Edge will give minions added maximum damage based on the player's Energy Shield, when The Grey Wind will give minions added damage based on the player's Life.
Animate Weapon of Self Reflection | Item acquisition | Animate Weapon of Self Reflection has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

Arcane Cloak | Description | Arcane Cloak is a guard skill that consumes a large amount of mana to apply a buff that absorbs incoming damage for a short period of time. The buff absorbs more damage and grants added lightning damage the more mana was spent.
Arcane Cloak | Skill functions and interactions | Guard skill: Guard skills share cooldown with other guard skills. They do not go on cooldown until the buff they provide expires.
Arcane Cloak | Skill functions and interactions | Mana spent: The mana consumed by Arcane Cloak is considered spent mana, which means it can interact with effects based on mana spent, such as Arcane Surge Support and Indigon. This skill always spends mana , therefore Eldritch Battery cannot deduct ES from this mana spent and it cannot have its cost type converted with sources like Lifetap Support and Whispers of Infinity.
Arcane Cloak | Skill functions and interactions | Mana cost: Arcane Cloak technically has a mana cost of 0. The mana spent by the skill is unaffected by cost modifiers.
Arcane Cloak | Skill functions and interactions | Arcane: Arcane Cloak cannot be supported by Archmage Support.
Arcane Cloak | All Guard skills | Below are the 5 skill gems with the Guard gem tag :

Archmage Support | Infobox | Lightning , Arcane , Support , Spell Icon: ^ Level: (1-20). Requires Level 31. Supports spell skills that deal damage with hits and have no reservation. Cannot support arcane skills, Brand skills, Orb skills, Vaal skills, or skills used by totems, traps or mines. Cannot modify the skills of minions. This is a Support Gem. It does not grant a bonus to your character, but to skills in sockets connected to it. Place into an item socket connected to a socket containing the Skill Gem you wish to augment. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 31
Archmage Support | Description | Archmage Support is a support gem that adds lightning damage to the supported skill , based on the caster's unreserved maximum mana , with the downside of also adding an additional mana cost.
Archmage Support | Skill functions and interactions | Triggered spell skills : Archmage will support triggered spells normally. This includes spells with no original mana cost and spells that cannot be cast directly. As long as the spell is NOT casted by things other than you it will receive increased manacost and added damage.
Archmage Support | Item acquisition | Archmage Support can drop anywhere.

Armageddon Brand | Infobox | Spell , AoE , Fire , Duration , Brand Level: (1-20) Cost: (15-27) Mana Cast Time: 0.40 sec Critical Strike Chance: 5.00% Effectiveness of Added Damage: (180-210)%. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 28
Armageddon Brand | Description | Armageddon Brand is a spell that creates a brand at the target position. While attached, the brands creates fiery meteors that land on the target's location and deal fire damage in an area.
Armageddon Brand | Skill functions and interactions | Maximum Active Brands : All brands share a limit of three (3) active brand limit. This can be split between the two brand skills (2-1) or all of one type. Passive skills can increase the number of active brands that are allowed to be active at a time.
Armageddon Brand | Skill functions and interactions | Maximum Brand Attachment : By default, you may only have one brand of any kind attached to a single target at any time. Taking the Keystone Runebinder allows you to place a second brand on a single target. Like the Active Brand limit these brands may either be split (1-1) or both the same type of brand.
Armageddon Brand | Skill functions and interactions | Spell Echo : Armageddon Brand currently functions with Spell Echo Support so far as repeating the initial cast of the brand. The more casting speed multiplier on spell echo does NOT however affect the activation speed of the brand once attached to a target.
Armageddon Brand | Skill functions and interactions | Meteor : When Armageddon Brand activates, it casts a meteor falling down on the attached enemy. However, this meteor has a slow travel time, so if the enemy is fast-moving, the meteor can miss the enemy entirely. This also affects the "smoothness" of the skill, as this meteor drop delay time cannot be overcome. The meteor deals damage in an area on impact, this area is affected by modifiers to AoE.
Armageddon Brand | Skill functions and interactions | Ignite : When scaling Ignite, it is not recommended to take Runebinder unless player tries to maximize ignite uptime while using Sadism Support , since having multiple brands attached to an enemy does not increase the Ignite damage taken by the enemy (since there can only be one active Ignite instance on the enemy). The only benefit from the keystone would be higher hit frequency on the enemy which would potentially cause a bigger Ignite (from a bigger hit) to be applied.

Arrogance Support | Description | Arrogance Support is a support gem. It grants supported skills increased aura effect, and causes them to reserve life instead of mana , at the expense of a very high reservation multiplier ( 220%-201% for level 1-20 gems). This gem is typically used on low life builds that use energy shield as effective health.
Arrogance Support | Skill functions and interactions | Arrogance causes supported skills to reserve life instead of mana, similar to Blood Magic. This is not a conversion mechanic; the new reservation will no longer be affected by mana reservation modifiers, only by #% increased (Life) Reservation Efficiency of Skills.
Arrogance Support | Skill functions and interactions | Note that while Arrogance can support any skill with a reservation, its Supported Skills have #% increased Aura effect only affects auras , not non-aura buffs or other skill effects such as heralds , aspects , or Blood and Sand.

Autoexertion | Infobox | Warcry , Trigger , Spell Level: (1-20) Reservation: 15% Mana Cooldown Time: 0.60 sec Can Store 1 Use(s) Cast Time: Instant. Requires Level 24. Reserves mana based on the supported warcries to cause those warcries to be repeatedly triggered. Supported Warcries will Trigger when their Cooldown is over This Skill's Mana Reservation is the total of the Mana Reservations of Supported Skills, and cannot be further modified Supported Skills have (0-19)% increased Cooldown Recovery Rate Supported Warcries are Triggered when their Cooldowns are over Supported Skills' Base Mana Cost is 10 Supported Warcries do not grant Buffs or Charges to You or Allies Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Supported Skills have (0.5-10)% increased Cooldown Recovery Rate. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 24
Autoexertion | Description | Autoexertion is a skill gem that reserves mana for each supported warcry skill to have them trigger automatically once off cooldown. Supported warcries no longer grant buffs or charges , but can still exert attacks and debuff enemies.
Autoexertion | Skill functions and interactions | When linked to Autoexertion, all supported warcries each have a base reservation of 15%. This can be modified by modifiers to reservation efficiency. These modified reservations are then added together, and the sum is used as a single reservation for Autoexertion. This value cannot be further modified, as reservation efficiency is already applied. However, Prism Guardian and Blood Magic can be used to reserve life instead of mana.
Autoexertion | Skill functions and interactions | Autoexertion does not cause warcries to share a cooldown; each warcry will automatically trigger at the end of their cooldowns.
Autoexertion | Skill functions and interactions | Triggering a warcry does not count as 'using' a warcry for when you use a Warcry , but it does count as warcrying for when you Warcry effects (e.g. Lead By Example , gain 5 Valour when you Warcry, if able Banner Mastery ), and also as having Warcried Recently (e.g. Admonisher or Deidbellow ). [1]
Autoexertion | Skill functions and interactions | When warcries are triggered through Autoexertion, each warcry's base mana cost is overwritten to 10 instead of the value on the warcry gem.
Autoexertion | Skill functions and interactions | Autoexertion does not trigger skills in hideouts.
Autoexertion | Item acquisition | Autoexertion can drop anywhere.
Autoexertion | Recipes | Autoexertion can be created from the following recipes:
Autoexertion | Vendor reward | This item can be bought at the listed NPC vendors , after completing the following quests:
Autoexertion | Related unique items | The following unique items are related to Autoexertion: No results found

Automation | Description | Automation is a hybrid gem. While the Automation skill is active, skills supported by Automation Support trigger immediately and automatically once off cooldown. It supports instant spells without a reservation. It cannot support skills used by totems , traps , or mines.
Automation | Skill functions and interactions | Automation can support multiple instant spells at once. Each skill will be used off cooldown independently. Note that most guard skills and instant movement skills share cooldowns, preventing subsequent linked skills of that category from triggering.
Automation | Skill functions and interactions | For example, it can be used to automate Steelskin , Withering Step , and Detonate Mines all at once.
Automation | Skill functions and interactions | It is not possible to automate auras supported by Divine Blessing Support , as Divine Blessing adds a cast time to the aura.
Automation | Skill functions and interactions | Automation does not trigger spells in hideouts.

Awakened Elemental Focus Support | Description | Awakened Elemental Focus Support is the awakened variant of Elemental Focus Support obtainable in maps.
Awakened Elemental Focus Support | Skill functions and interactions | Besides having greater damage magnitude, it also grants +1 to Level of Supported Elemental Skill Gems at level 5 and above.
Awakened Elemental Focus Support | Skill functions and interactions | This support gem has a drawback of disabling the application of any elemental ailments with supported gems, even if it has stats that grant the ability to always inflict ailments. This drawback makes it more difficult to trigger certain effects requiring ailments, forcing the player to lose some of their stats or require the application of ailments through other means, such as Summon Skitterbots or a different skill. Some notable support gems that are affected negatively when linked together with Elemental Focus include:
Awakened Elemental Focus Support | Skill functions and interactions | Elemental Focus does not prevent Vortex 's ground effect, Chilled Ground , and Shocked Ground from applying ailments, but does prevent other secondary ailment applications like Wintertide Brand or the chilling areas from Creeping Frost.
Awakened Elemental Focus Support | Item acquisition | Awakened Elemental Focus Support has restrictions on where or how it can drop. Can drop from either version of the Maven , from Maven's Invitations , or from Maven-witnessed map bosses.

Awakened Unleash Support | Infobox | Spell , Support Icon: q Level: (1-5) Cost & Reservation Multiplier: 140%. Requires Level 72. Supports spell skills, making their effect reoccur when cast. Cannot modify the skills of minions. Supported Spells gain a Seal every (0.70-0.66) seconds, to a maximum of (3-4) Seals Supported Spells are Unsealed when cast, and their effects Reoccur for each Seal lost Supported Skills deal (35-33)% less Damage when Reoccurring Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Supported Skills deal (0.5-10)% increased Spell Damage. Awakened Unleash Support | Description | Awakened Unleash Support is the awakened variant of Unleash Support obtainable in maps.
Awakened Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | The gem's advanced description specifies (Spell cast time is 10% longer for each time the Spell reoccurs).
Awakened Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | While the spell "reoccurs", your character will stand in place while playing the casting animation. Note that the reoccurring spells do not count as being cast by you, and will therefore not trigger any on-cast abilities such as Intensify Support stacks.
Awakened Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | Due to how this support gem functions, to achieve maximum DPS potential, casting intervals need to be timed to allow for generation for maximum Seals between casts. As increase cast speed is generally also a DPS increase, using Unleash may be detrimental in some scenarios, and the player should ensure these factors have been compared.
Awakened Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | When used in conjunction with Spell Echo Support , the first cast uses all Seals and repeated casts will not benefit from Seals unless your cast speed is slow enough to generate an additional Seal between the repeats.
Awakened Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | The damage multiplier of reoccur portion of the cast, depends on how often the skill is cast and can be estimated (for a lvl 5 gem) using the following equation:
Awakened Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | 100 % − 38 % 0.66 second \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}{\dfrac {100\%-38\%}{0.66{\text{second}}}}
Awakened Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | or approximately: 6.06 % less DPS \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}{6.06\%{\text{ less DPS}}}
Awakened Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | This assume the player have matching cast time that equal to 0.66 second : 1.51 cast per second to achieve this dps for reoccur portion of the cast.
Awakened Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | This does not take into account of gem quality Supported Skills deal 0.5% increased Spell Damage (per 1% quality) which is multiplicative with Supported Skills deal (40-38)% less Damage when Reoccurring.
Awakened Unleash Support | Item acquisition | Awakened Unleash Support has restrictions on where or how it can drop. Can drop from either version of the Maven or from Maven-witnessed map bosses.
Awakened Unleash Support | Monster restrictions | This item can be acquired from the following monsters:
Awakened Unleash Support | Recipes | Awakened Unleash Support can be created from the following recipes:

Bane of Condemnation | Description | It is a transfigured version of Bane.
Bane of Condemnation | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version has higher base chaos damage over time and gains more damage and increased duration per curse socketed, but the base radius and duration is lowered and has a slower cast time. Gem quality increases base duration instead.
Bane of Condemnation | Changes from default version | Despite the removal of regular Bane's Applied Curses have 25% less Effect from its gem text, Bane of Condemnation still applies a 25% less effect penalty to curses it supports. [1] This will also show up on the tooltip of supported hex skills.
Bane of Condemnation | Changes from default version | Despite using a different hidden support (id: SupportDarkRitualAltX ) than the original Bane (id: SupportDarkRitual ), their effects are currently identical.
Bane of Condemnation | Changes from default version | Note: The large damage multipliers are only if you are willing to wait the full duration, and only considers a single cast.

Barrage Support | Description | Barrage Support is a support gem which modifies bow and wand attack skills to fire additional projectiles in succession, much like the Barrage skill.
Barrage Support | Skill functions and interactions | This support causes the bow and wand skills to projectiles in sequence similarly to Barrage , but unlike the skill itself, each additional projectile adds additional attack time as a penalty. Support gems that provide additional projectiles become gradually worse as more sources of additional projectiles are obtained, with Lesser Multiple Projectiles Support beating out Awakened Greater Multiple Projectiles Support at 6 projectiles.

Blade Blast | Description | Blade Blast is a spell that creates an area of effect explosion that deals physical damage. The explosion chains to any Lingering Blades left on the ground by Bladefall.
Blade Blast | Skill functions and interactions | The damage and level of the skill that leaves lingering blades on the ground is not calculated in the blade blast damage. Lower level skills can create more blades in certain situations. For example:
Blade Blast | Skill functions and interactions | Lingering blades from Ethereal Knives and Bladefall will persist after unequipping them. Therefore using said skills on a weapon swap and then swapping back to blade blast will save gem slots.
Blade Blast | Skill functions and interactions | Partying : Blade Blast is able to detonate Lingering Blades created by other allies.
Blade Blast | Skill functions and interactions | Detonation chain: A lingering blade is marked for detonation when it is hit by a Blade Blast explosion. This explosion can originate from either the original cast of Blade Blast or another detonated lingering blade. A queue of blades marked for detonation is kept and newly marked blades are added to the end of this list. The queue is emptied at a rate of one blade per server tick or 30 blade detonations per second, see Cooldown#Server_frames.

Blade Blast of Dagger Detonation | Description | Blade Blast of Dagger Detonation is a spell that creates an area of effect explosion that deals physical damage around the user. This skill gains additional spell damage equal to a percentage of damage from equipped daggers.
Blade Blast of Dagger Detonation | Description | It is a transfigured version of Blade Blast.
Blade Blast of Dagger Detonation | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version does not interact with Lingering Blades. Instead, it has higher damage effectiveness, longer cast time, is centred around the user as a nova , and gains added damage from your equipped daggers similar to a stronger version of Spellblade Support.

Blade Blast of Unloading | Description | Blade Blast of Unloading is a spell that creates an area of effect explosion that deals physical damage around the user. This explosion also detonates blades from Blade Vortex. If used with Blade Vortex, all stacks of it will be consumed immediately, creating an expanding ring around the player.
Blade Blast of Unloading | Description | It is a transfigured version of Blade Blast.
Blade Blast of Unloading | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version does not interact with Lingering Blades. Instead, it has higher damage effectiveness, longer cast time, is centred around the user as a nova , and detonates each blade stack from Blade Vortex , dealing damage for each blade in an expanding area around the player until no blades remain, or 10 blades have been detonated by that detonation chain.
Blade Blast of Unloading | Skill functions and interactions | Blade Vortex : A maximum of 10 Blade Vortex blades can be detonated per use of Blade Blast. Re-casting Blade Blast while Blade Vortexes are detonating will cause simultaneous detonations, increasing the rate of detonation at the cost of maximum area of effect. Blade Blast cannot detonate Vaal Blade Vortex or other players' Blade Vortex blades.
Blade Blast of Unloading | Item acquisition | Blade Blast of Unloading has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 12
Blade Vortex | Description | Blade Vortex (often referred to as BV ) is a spell that creates an ethereal blade that spins around the caster that deals physical damage , each blade lasting for the minimum duration of 4 seconds.
Blade Vortex | Skill functions and interactions | Blade Blast of Unloading : When targeted by Blade Blast of Unloading , the blades will detonate in sequence, increasing in AoE with subsequent detonations. The damage from these detonations scale solely from the Blade Blast skill.
Blade Vortex | Skill functions and interactions | Maximum blades : Up to 10 blade stacks can be active at a time. Each stack has its own duration.
Blade Vortex | Skill functions and interactions | Hit interval : When the blade is first spawned, the skill will deal a hit after the hit interval duration (0.6 seconds with one blade).
Blade Vortex | Skill functions and interactions | Multiple copies: You can only have one Blade Vortex effect at any one time. Casting Blade Vortex with another copy of the gem will remove all active stacks and start a new Blade Vortex effect.
Blade Vortex | Skill functions and interactions | Multiple casts: Using Blade Vortex while you have no Blade Vortex effect will create a new Blade Vortex effect with all the "spell cast" modifiers baked into that Blade Vortex effect. Any subsequent uses of Blade Vortex will only add a stack to the currently active effect. This means that the less damage from Unleash Support or more damage from Greater Spell Echo Support are normally not applied, since the casts which have those damage modifiers are typically ones which are only adding another stack. However, removing the active Blade Vortex effect during the cast time will allow you to snapshot the effects of a later cast in a series of repeats (e.g. use Blade Blast of Unloading and Blastchain Mine Support ). This allows you to snapshot the more damage and increased AoE from Greater Spell Echo Support for as long as that Blade Vortex effect lasts. However, at least from Dec 2022, there is a bug when using this strategy with Plume of Pursuit. Instead of causing the whole Blade Vortex effect to either always critically strike or never critically strike, it will merely allow the Blade Vortex effect to critically strike as if Plume of Pursuit were not equipped. Note also that Intensity updates on each cast [1] , and stats that apply per hit instead of per cast do not snapshot.
Blade Vortex | Skill functions and interactions | Visual effect: Blade vortex's visual effect does not scale with Area of effect modifiers. Instead, to give a rough indication of radius, there are several versions of the knife effect, each rotating at a different radius. The skill will always try to have one knife using the largest-radius effect that's not larger than the actual radius of the skill, but that skill results in discrete visual changes where you need to reach a certain threshold before the next wider effect can be used. The full radius is always used mechanically. [2]
Blade Vortex | Calculating DPS | Blade Vortex's in-game tooltip DPS gives the average damage of one hit from one Blade Vortex instance, multiplied by casts per second. This value is misleading primarily because Blade Vortex can hit more than once over its duration, thus cast speed must not be used, but hits per second (rounds per second, spin rate), over the duration of the stacks.
Blade Vortex | Math and tables | Base spin rate of Blade Vortex is 600 ms. Blade Vortex hit more frequently with more stacks it has. As gem says: 35% increased Hit Rate for each blade. So spin time is calculated as S p i n T i m e = B a s e S p i n T i m e ( 1 + S p i n T i m e R e d u c t i o n × S t a c k s C o u n t ) \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}SpinTime={BaseSpinTime \over (1+SpinTimeReduction\times StacksCount)}
Blade Vortex | Math and tables | R o u n d s P e r S e c o n d = 1 S p i n T i m e = ( 1 + S p i n T i m e R e d u c t i o n × S t a c k s C o u n t ) B a s e S p i n T i m e \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}RoundsPerSecond={1 \over SpinTime}={(1+SpinTimeReduction\times StacksCount) \over BaseSpinTime}
Blade Vortex | Math and tables | Assuming spin rate increases for 35% for each stack, we have the following data:
Blade Vortex | Math and tables | Each stack of Blade Vortex increases hit damage with the following formula: H i t D a m a g e = B a s e H i t D a m a g e × ( 1 + F × S t a c k s C o u n t ) \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}HitDamage={BaseHitDamage\times {(1+F\times StacksCount)}}
Blade Vortex | Math and tables | Where F {\textstyle \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}F} is per-stack damage increase factor.
Blade Vortex | Math and tables | Then we need just multiply hits per second with hit damage. D a m a g e P e r S e c o n d M u l t i p l i e r = H i t D a m a g e × R o u n d s P e r S e c o n d \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}DamagePerSecondMultiplier={HitDamage\times RoundsPerSecond}
Blade Vortex | Math and tables | Assuming 35% more damage per stack, then using base hit damage as 1, we would expect the following per-hit damage increases:
Blade Vortex | Math and tables | Finally, to obtain sustained DPS from tooltip use the following formula: S u s t a i n e d D P S = D a m a g e P e r S e c o n d M u l t i p l i e r × T o o l t i p D P S C a s t s P e r S e c o n d \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}SustainedDPS={{DamagePerSecondMultiplier\times TooltipDPS} \over CastsPerSecond}
Blade Vortex | Math and tables | or in expanded form S u s t a i n e d D P S = ( 1 + P e r S t a c k D a m a g e I n c r e a s e F a c t o r × S t a c k s C o u n t ) × ( 1 + S p i n T i m e R e d u c t i o n × S t a c k s C o u n t ) × T o o l t i p D P S ( C a s t s P e r S e c o n d × B a s e S p i n T i m e ) \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}SustainedDPS={{{(1+{PerStackDamageIncreaseFactor\times StacksCount})\times (1+{SpinTimeReduction\times StacksCount})}\times TooltipDPS} \over ({CastsPerSecond\times BaseSpinTime})}

Blade Vortex of the Scythe | Description | Blade Vortex of the Scythe is a spell that creates an ethereal blade that spins around the caster that deals physical damage.
Blade Vortex of the Scythe | Description | It is a transfigured version of Blade Vortex.
Blade Vortex of the Scythe | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version has more damage and damage effectiveness, a larger radius, higher mana cost, slower cast time, and it hits directly after being cast. The duration of each blade is much shorter and the hit rate does not increase with more active blades. It has a single maximum active blade limit. Gem quality grants more physical damage.
Blade Vortex of the Scythe | Skill functions and interactions | Interaction w/ Blade Blast of Unloading : When targeted by Blade Blast of Unloading , the blades can be detonated. The damage from the detonation scale solely from the Blade Blast skill.
Blade Vortex of the Scythe | Skill functions and interactions | Maximum blades : Only one blade can be active at a time. Once the limit is reached, creating a new blade will simply remove the existing active blade.
Blade Vortex of the Scythe | Skill functions and interactions | Hit interval : Unlike regular Blade Vortex, Blade Vortex of the Scythe will deal a hit immediately when the blades are created. Recasting Blade Vortex of the Scythe while there's already an active blade will not cause the skill to deal a hit on cast. To optimize the damage, your cast speed should be slightly longer than the blade duration and have the blade duration be slightly higher than 0.8 seconds.
Blade Vortex of the Scythe | Item acquisition | Blade Vortex of the Scythe has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

Bladefall | Description | Bladefall is a physical spell that causes ethereal blades to rain from the sky, dealing damage to enemies in a sequence of volleys. Enemies can be hit multiple times where these overlap. The blade volleys leave behind Lingering Blades that interact with other skills.

Bladefall of Impaling | Description | Bladefall of Impaling is a physical spell that causes ethereal blades to rain from the sky, dealing damage and applying Impale on critical strike to enemies in a sequence of volleys. Enemies can be hit multiple times where these overlap.
Bladefall of Impaling | Description | It is a transfigured version of Bladefall.
Bladefall of Impaling | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version does not leave Lingering Blades. Instead, it has a higher critical strike chance and inflicts Impale on hit, but prevents critical strikes from doing extra damage, rendering critical strike multiplier useless for hits.
Bladefall of Impaling | Item acquisition | Bladefall of Impaling has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

Bladefall of Trarthus | Description | Bladefall of Trarthus is a physical spell that once activated continuously spends mana to fire ethereal blades raining down from the sky at nearby enemies. The blade volleys leave behind Lingering Blades that interact with other skills.
Bladefall of Trarthus | Description | It is a Trarthan version of Bladefall.
Bladefall of Trarthus | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this Trarthan version is an instant skill that costs a continuous percentage of your maximum mana while active, during which it will automatically fire a volley of blades at a nearby enemy at a rate based on your maximum mana. It has lower damage and damage effectiveness per hit, and enemies cannot be hit multiple times from the same volley. The skill cannot be triggered or supported by proxies.

Bladestorm | Description | Bladestorm is a melee attack skill that creates a bladestorm that continuously deals damage in an area for a short duration. Its properties change depending on whether the user is in Blood Stance or Sand Stance.
Bladestorm | Skill functions and interactions | Bladestorm has two components: an initial melee attack that deals damage around the player, and a lingering blood or sand storm that will repeatedly (based on attack speed) deal 50% of melee damage to enemies within its area.
Bladestorm | Skill functions and interactions | While dual wielding , one weapon is used per skill use (including repeats ), and created storms will hit with that weapon for their entire duration. [1]
Bladestorm | Skill functions and interactions | This skill interacts with Blood and Sand Stance :
Bladestorm | Skill functions and interactions | In either stance, Bladestorm will have more attack speed while inside a blood bladestorm.
Bladestorm | Skill functions and interactions | While the storms have 100% chance to inflict blind or bleeding, the initial hit does not. [2]
Bladestorm | Skill functions and interactions | The storms generated by the initial melee attack will wait for the duration of the attack time to start dealing damage, e.g. if you have an attack time of 0.5 seconds, the storm will start dealing damage after 0.5 seconds.
Bladestorm | Skill functions and interactions | Because hits do not snapshot , existing storms will account for any changes to the skill's stats, including attack rate. [3]
Bladestorm | Skill functions and interactions | When used with General's Cry , each Mirage Warrior will have their own independent bladestorm limit.
Bladestorm | Item acquisition | Bladestorm can drop anywhere.

Blazing Salvo | Description | Blazing Salvo is a spell that fires multiple flaming projectiles that deal fire damage in an area of effect upon landing. The further you target with the spell, the wider area the projectiles can land.
Blazing Salvo | Skill functions and interactions | Returning Projectiles: When supported with Returning Projectiles Support , the projectiles you cast will land at the targeted location. Upon impact, they will deal area fire damage, after which the projectiles will then travel back to the location from where they were cast, exploding again. The projectiles' grouping will also spread further apart during their return, as per their normal behavior.
Blazing Salvo | Skill functions and interactions | Traps and Mines: When fired from a trap or mine , the skill will target the nearest target.

Blind Support | Description | Blind is a support gem that provides supported skills with a chance to inflict blind , which greatly reduces an enemy's chance to hit for a short duration.
Blind Support | Skill functions and interactions | Spells do not have a chance to hit (spell always hit and does not take evasion and accuracy into calculation) , the ability to hit with a spell is unaffected by being blinded. Thus, using this support against monster that use spell did not have any effect. Some unique items still provide additional damage against blinded monsters.
Blind Support | Skill functions and interactions | An attacker's chance to hit with attack skill, is the direct opposite of the defender's chance to evade. Since player characters with Unwavering Stance cannot evade, attacks made against them always hit. As a result, even blinded monster will always have 100% chance to hit a player character with Unwavering Stance (if assuming zero dodge), since the Unwavering Stance character always has 0% chance to evade. Similarly, attacks made by characters with Resolute Technique cannot be evaded, so blind has no effect beyond the sight radius reduction. This also applies to attack that have the mod Hits can't be Evaded during PVP.
Blind Support | Item acquisition | Blind Support can drop anywhere.

Blind Support | Vendor reward | This item can be bought at the listed NPC vendors , after completing the following quests:
Blind Support | Mods | No results found
Blink Arrow | Infobox | Attack , Minion , Duration , Movement , Travel , Bow Level: (1-20) Cost: (14-20) Mana Cooldown Time: 3.00 sec Can Store 1 Use(s). Requires Level 10. Blink Arrow | Description | Blink Arrow is an attack that fires an arrow, upon reaching the destination you are teleported there and a clone is summoned at your previous location. The clone is a minion that uses your bow and quiver.
Blink Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | The skill happens in four stages:
Blink Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | The length of the first stage is affected by anything that affects regular attacks - weapon base speed, attack speed modifiers etc. The length of the second stage is affected both by bonuses to projectile speed and by global bonuses to attack speed, but not by the weapon's base speed. [2] [3] The delay is unaffected by distance travelled.
Blink Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | The delay of the first two stages can be reduced by linking Faster Attacks Support and Faster Projectiles Support ; for situational movement uses, it can be socketed into a Quill Rain on swap. Due to the delay before the teleport, Blink Arrow may not be the best skill for quick repositioning to escape a mob of monsters or an incoming skill; however, it can be combined with a blink skill (particularly Frostblink ), which is instant). Its main advantage is its range, which can reach maximum bow range which is roughly twice the range of Flame Dash. [1]
Blink Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | The minion generated by this skill will spawn with the current % of total life as the player; if the player is on Low Life (e.g. via Petrified Blood ), the minion will also spawn at Low Life, causing it to instantly explode with Minion Instability. The minion uses a bow Default Attack supported by all applicable support gems linked to Blink Arrow. It is unable to use other skills or move around.
Blink Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | This skill can be used multiple times in a row by getting additional uses via Second Wind Support.
Blink Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | Clones copy the visuals of all buffs , debuffs, and similar effects on the summoner at the time they are created. This is intended to obscure whether the player used Blink Arrow or Mirror Arrow in PvP and has no effect on minions' actual stats. [4]

Bloodthirst Support | Description | Bloodthirst Support is a support gem that grants more damage to attacks made with the supported skill. It also grants added flat physical damage with weapons based on your maximum life pool to the skill while on low life.
Bloodthirst Support | Skill Functions and Interactions | Bloodthirst's more damage modifier is active unconditionally, whether the character is on low life or not.
Bloodthirst Support | Skill Functions and Interactions | The flat added physical damage is a conditional modifier, because it is only active while on low life and using a weapon.
Bloodthirst Support | Skill Functions and Interactions | Damage Calculations
Bloodthirst Support | Skill Functions and Interactions | The total damage depends on the characters maximum life and total flat damage from other sources.
Bloodthirst Support | Skill Functions and Interactions | These calculations count both the flat physical damage and the more damage. Therefore they assume the character is on low life and using a weapon.
Bloodthirst Support | Skill Functions and Interactions | Add up all flat sources of damage not from the gem = Q
Bloodthirst Support | Skill Functions and Interactions | (Total life X.02) = Y
Bloodthirst Support | Skill Functions and Interactions | ( Q + Y ) = K
Bloodthirst Support | Skill Functions and Interactions | ( K X More damage) = V
Bloodthirst Support | Skill Functions and Interactions | (V / Q) = final answer.
Bloodthirst Support | Skill Functions and Interactions | Colours indicate scenarios where Level 20 Melee Physical Damage Support or Level 5 Awakened Melee Physical Damage Support would be more beneficial.

Bonechill Support | Description | Bonechill Support is a support gem that grants the supported skill more cold damage and increased chill effect. Chills from supported skills also cause enemies to take increased cold damage (from all sources) equal to the strongest chill affecting them.
Bonechill Support | Mechanics | Bonechill Support adds the increase Cold Damage taken by Chill Effect stat to chill debuffs from supported skills, as well as to chilling areas created by them. This is a boolean property, and accordingly does not stack when enemies are both chilled by a skill and inside that skill's chilling area. [1]
Bonechill Support | Mechanics | Chill Effect refers to the enemy's strongest #% reduced Action Speed from any chill. This does not need to be applied by the same chill debuff: one chill can apply Bonechill, and another chill can apply the strongest action speed reduction. [2] This means the player can choose to support a secondary skill with Bonechill (e.g. Frostblink ) to make enemies take increased cold damage from a primary skill with different support gems.
Bonechill Support | Mechanics | Because Bonechill's increased cold damage taken is applied by chill and based on chill effect, it is normally capped to 30%, has no effect on enemies Unaffected by Chill , [3] and will affect the player when their chill is reflected (e.g. by Non-Damaging Ailments you inflict are reflected back to you ).
Bonechill Support | Mechanics | This effect does not stack with Ahuana's Bite 's Enemies Chilled by your Hits have Damage taken increased by Chill Effect ; chill effect can only increase cold damage taken once.
Bonechill Support | Mechanics | Freeze is unrelated to Bonechill, though hits that freeze typically also have enough cold damage to chill. [4]

Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 28
Boneshatter | Description | Boneshatter is a melee attack skill that strikes enemies forcefully and also hurts you. Successive uses will raise the damage dealt both to enemies and you through Trauma stacks. Stunning an enemy with the strike releases a damaging area of effect pulse.
Boneshatter | Description | Boneshatter can be used with a Mace , Sceptre , Axe or Staff.
Boneshatter | Skill functions and interactions | Boneshatter cannot be supported by Trauma Support. Skills that gain bonuses based on your Trauma stacks do not care about the source of that trauma; for example, if you use Boneshatter to generate trauma, skills supported by Trauma Support will still be granted added Physical Damage even if they themselves did not generate the trauma.
Boneshatter | Gaining Trauma | Hitting an enemy with Boneshatter or strike skill (other than Boneshatter) supported by Trauma Support will grant you a Trauma stack, giving more Damage or added Physical Damage respectively. However, you will also take Physical Damage per Trauma stack upon gaining a new one. Only one stack can be obtained per attack, regardless of the number of enemies hit. Repeated attacks from Multistrike Support will also not grant additional Trauma stacks and only inflicts self-damage every 3rd hit. This can be used to reduce the gain and impact of Trauma for builds incapable of tanking high stacks of Trauma.
Boneshatter | Gaining Trauma | Each Trauma stack counts as an individual buff , with each Trauma stack having its own duration. The (now drop-disabled ) Mantra of Flames Jewel therefore grants a damage bonus for each stack of Trauma. Linking Boneshatter or a Trauma skill with More Duration Support / Efficacy Support , cursing yourself/being cursed with Temporal Chains , or gaining skill effect duration elsewhere will increase the duration of each Trauma buff stack, reducing the need for high attack speed to maintain high Trauma.
Boneshatter | Damage taken from Trauma | With a level 20 gem and N current Trauma stacks, you will take (194 ( Boneshatter ) /497 ( Boneshatter of Complex Trauma ) 146 ( Trauma Support ) ) *(N+1) physical damage (before damage mitigation ) upon your next attack.
Boneshatter | Damage taken from Trauma | The damage taken per Trauma stack hits , enabling when-hit effects and Cast when Damage Taken Support. It also allows your armour and/or physical damage reduction to prevent some of the physical damage taken. However, the damage cannot be blocked or evaded via evasion rating/ dodge. "Avoid damage from hits" effects (e.g. Elusive ) do work, however.
Boneshatter | Damage taken from Trauma | Damage conversion modifiers do not affect the damage from Trauma. However, damage taken as modifiers such as #% of Physical Damage from Hits taken as Cold Damage will shift the damage you take, allowing mitigation via elemental resistances. Additionally, it is possible to chill , freeze , or shock yourself this way; it is not possible to self-inflict damaging ailments unless you have a source of All Damage from Hits can Ignite/Poison you such as Mokou's Embrace.
Boneshatter | Damage taken from Trauma | Recoup is a valuable stat to have due to the escalating damage quickly exceeding and overpowering other forms of recovery, whereas Recoup will always recover the same percentage of damage received.
Boneshatter | Item acquisition | Boneshatter can drop anywhere.

Brand Recall | Description | Brand Recall is a spell that recalls all brands to the caster and causes them to activate.
Brand Recall | Skill functions and interactions | Brand Recall can only reach level 6 through regular experience gain, but the required experience to reach level 6 is comparable to that of regular gems. Its level cap can be exceeded the same way as other gems.
Brand Recall | Skill functions and interactions | Activations caused by Brand Recall cannot benefit from against Branded Enemy stats, even if the brand immediately attaches to an enemy.
Brand Recall | Skill functions and interactions | Spends 20% of Brand's Cost is a cost for effects that interact with costs, like Eldritch Battery or Tawhanuku's Timing or stats that count total resource spent. It is not directly affected by cost modifiers (as the modifiers would already apply to the brand's cost, to prevent double-dipping). If the cost of the recalled brands changes, the cost spent by Brand Recall updates to match it. Insufficient mana or life does not prevent the brands from being recalled.
Brand Recall | Skill functions and interactions | Because Brand Recall's natural cooldown is 4 seconds, socketing Brand Recall in a Trigger a Socketed Spell when you Use a Skill, with a 8 second Cooldown weapon replaces its natural cooldown with the longer veiled cooldown. This allows it to trigger independently from a second copy of Brand Recall (which can be given a different trigger, such as Automation ). [1]
Brand Recall | Skill functions and interactions | When triggering Brand Recall and using Summon Triggerbots to cause an additional trigger, the recalled brand will activate twice. This can be combined with Arcanist Brand : the two triggers of Brand Recall will cause two Arcanist Brand activations, triggering the supported skill twice, both of which will be duplicated by Triggerbots. The result is a total of four skill effects per brand. [2]

Cast On Critical Strike Support | Infobox | Critical , Support , Spell , Trigger Icon: & Level: (1-20) Cost & Reservation Multiplier: 120% Cooldown Time: 0.15 sec Can Store 1 Use(s). Cast On Critical Strike Support | Description | Cast on Critical Strike (commonly referred to as CoC ) is a support gem that causes linked spells to trigger when the player critically strikes with a linked attack skill.
Cast On Critical Strike Support | Supporting multiple spells | If more than one spell is supported by Cast on Critical Support, the order of the triggered spells will follow the trigger order. (Successive triggers cycle through all valid supported spells in the same order.) Each supported skill has its own cooldown, except multiple copies of the same spell, which share their cooldown.
Cast On Critical Strike Support | Effective cooldown & cooldown thresholds | Due to how server frames are calculated, there are breakpoints of cooldown recovery rates required to achieve a higher effective trigger rate. [1]. Additional cooldown reduction that does not reach the next threshold is wasted.
Cast On Critical Strike Support | Effective cooldown & cooldown thresholds | The attack rates below are maximums; exceeding the attack rates even slightly can significantly reduce proc rate due to skipping procs while the skill is still on cooldown. It is better to be slightly below the attack rate than above it. Note that multiples of the attack rate also work (e.g. 12.12 APS will proc at the same rate as 6.06 APS).
Cast On Critical Strike Support | Item acquisition | Cast On Critical Strike Support can drop anywhere.
Cast On Critical Strike Support | Recipes | Cast On Critical Strike Support can be created from the following recipes:
Cast On Critical Strike Support | Vendor reward | This item can be bought at the listed NPC vendors , after completing the following quests:
Cast On Critical Strike Support | Strategy | Fundamentally, a Cast on Critical setup requires an attack , a spell , and the Cast on Crit Support itself. Each of these pieces can be from gems, or one of them can be replaced by modifiers or skills granted by items.
Cast On Critical Strike Support | Strategy | Certain skills that can deal multiple hits throughout its animation such as Spectral Throw and Lancing Steel can proc spells multiple times with a single attack.
Cast On Critical Strike Support | Weapon choice | The choice of weapon on a character will affect both the variety of attacks that can proc Cast on Crit, as well as the investment into critical strike chance required. Generally, a weapon with high critical strike chance and attack speed is preferred.
Cast On Critical Strike Support | Weapon choice | Melee skills such as Cyclone , Static Strike , etc. will prefer weapons such as Jewelled Foil , Karui Sceptre , Imperial Skean , or Imperial Claw / Gemini Claw.
Cast On Critical Strike Support | Weapon choice | Bow skills such as Tornado Shot or Blast Rain typically prefer a Spine Bow.
Cast On Critical Strike Support | Weapon choice | Wand skills such as Kinetic Blast typically prefer an Imbued Wand.
Cast On Critical Strike Support | Weapon choice | Some two-handed weapons such as Spine Bow and Exquisite Blade can roll a modifier that grants Socketed Gems are supported by Level 18 Cast On Critical Strike if it has Shaper influence.
Cast On Critical Strike Support | Weapon choice | Various unique weapons are often used with CoC, such as Cospri's Malice. Some granted skills from weapons can replace the attack or spell of a CoC setup, such as Martyr of Innocence 's Vengeance , Ngamahu's Flame 's Molten Burst , or The Whispering Ice 's Icestorm. Note that triggered attacks can trigger a CoC spell, but attempting to support a spell with a trigger condition with Cast on Crit will disable it due to exceeding the trigger limit.
Cast On Critical Strike Support | Support gem synergy | While the attack skill is generally not scaled as a primary damage source, support gems modifying the spell can have synergistic effects with the attack. For example, a setup using Chain Support or Greater Multiple Projectiles Support with Fireball may prefer using projectiles like Tornado Shot or Kinetic Blast. Alternatively, a setup using Increased Area of Effect Support with Ice Nova may prefer using Cyclone.
Cast On Critical Strike Support | Related modifiers | The following modifiers are related to Cast On Critical Strike Support:
Cast On Critical Strike Support | Legacy mods | The list below displays Cast On Critical Strike Support related modifiers where all spawn weights have been set to zero.
Cast On Critical Strike Support | Related unique items | The following unique items are related to Cast On Critical Strike Support:

Cast on Melee Kill Support | Skill functions and interactions | Melee Kill : Spells can only be triggered by kills with melee attack damage. Enemies killed by damage over time (e.g. Bleeding applied by a melee hit) will not trigger supported spells. [1]

This Gem can only Support Skill Gems requiring Level (38-70) or lower Supported Skills deal (65-27)% less Damage Trigger Supported Spells when you take (528-3272) total Damage from Hits Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Supported Skills require (0.5-10)% reduced amount of Damage taken to Trigger. This is a Support Gem. It does not grant a bonus to your character, but to skills in sockets connected to it. Place into an item socket connected to a socket containing the Skill Gem you wish to augment. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 38
Cast when Damage Taken Support | Description | Cast when Damage Taken is a support gem. It causes a linked spell to trigger when the player takes a certain amount of damage from hits.
Cast when Damage Taken Support | Skill functions and interactions | Trigger gem : Cast when Damage Taken supports spells. Multiple different spells can be supported, and each will have their own damage taken counter and cooldown. Multiple copies of Cast when Damage Taken will also function independently, as long as they don't support copies of the same active skill, which would cause a shared cooldown. Because spells will only be triggered after the damage is taken, Cast when Damage Taken linked to a guard skill will never save the player from a killing blow; the character will die before the spell is triggered.
Cast when Damage Taken Support | Skill functions and interactions | Damage counter and trigger cooldown : The Cast when Damage Taken gem itself does not have a counter or cooldown, only spells it supports do. [1] Each supported skill has an internal damage counter that tracks the total accumulated damage taken from hits. Damage over time and non-damage loss do not contribute to this counter, i.e. you cannot use Righteous Fire to trigger Cast when Damage Taken. [2] All damage counters will be reset to 0 upon entering a new area. A skill does not track damage taken while it is on cooldown. If the damage threshold is met but the skill cannot trigger (e.g. due to insufficient mana), the damage counter resets.
Cast when Damage Taken Support | Skill functions and interactions | Skill cooldowns : Because skills can only have one cooldown, Cast when Damage Taken does not add its 0.25s cooldown to skills that already have a cooldown that's equal or higher.
Cast when Damage Taken Support | Skill functions and interactions | Maximum level of supported skills : Cast when Damage Taken cannot support skills with a higher level requirement than that of Cast when Damage Taken itself. This restriction only applies to skill gems; support gems are not affected.
Cast when Damage Taken Support | Skill functions and interactions | Trigger order : If multiple triggers proc simultaneously, skills triggered follow a set order.
Cast when Damage Taken Support | Skill functions and interactions | Targeting : Triggered spells will target the location of the entity that dealt the hit that caused the trigger. Reflected damage is considered to come from the enemy which the damage was reflected from. If triggered by self-damage , it will target a random enemy, or if there are no enemies, it will target the player's location or a random direction, depending on the triggered skill. [3] [4]

Cast when Damage Taken Support | Alternate skill effects | Cast when Damage Taken Support has the following alternate skill effects :
Cast when Damage Taken Support | Cats when Damage Taken | Cats when Damage Taken was a trigger gem available only on April 1, 2015 as an April Fool's joke. [5] Like Cast when Damage Taken , it caused a linked spell to activate when the player accumulated damage taken, but besides casting the spell, it also summoned black cats. Other properties were identical to the Cast when Damage Taken skill.

Cast when Stunned Support | Description | Cast when Stunned is a support gem that grants supported spells a chance to trigger when the player is stunned or blocks a stunning hit.
Cast when Stunned Support | Skill functions and interactions | Different spells supported by Cast when Stunned are independent: when the character is stunned or blocks a stunning hit, each supported spell rolls its own chance to trigger, and each spell that succeeds its chance check will trigger and go on its own cooldown. Because multiple copies of the same spell (or a transfigured variant ) with the same trigger share a cooldown, supporting duplicate spells with Cast when Stunned will only result in two chance rolls, never two triggers.
Cast when Stunned Support | Skill functions and interactions | Triggered spells will target the location of the enemy that stunned the player. Skills that don't target a location like Ice Nova will still occur at the player.
Cast when Stunned Support | Skill functions and interactions | Spells that trigger when stunned create their effects before resources are lost to the stunning hit. This means skills like Steelskin or Frost Shield can absorb damage from the hit that triggers them. [1] (Unlike Cast when Damage Taken Support.)
Cast when Stunned Support | Skill functions and interactions | Supported spells will not trigger when stun is avoided or ignored, whether that's by stun avoidance , ignoring interruption (e.g. Unwaveringly Evil ), or the 50% chance to ignore stun by having non-zero current energy shield.
Cast when Stunned Support | Skill functions and interactions | Cast when Stunned cannot cause spells to trigger from self-damage , as this cannot stun.
Cast when Stunned Support | Skill functions and interactions | Compared to Cast when Damage Taken Support , Cast when Stunned compares favorably in several regards: it has no damage penalty, no cost multiplier, no gem level limitations, and a lower cooldown. However, whether its condition is easier or harder to fulfill depends on the player's life, mitigation, and stun modifiers like Valyrium.
Cast when Stunned Support | Cold critical strikes | Note that critical strikes that cause you to take cold damage cannot inherently stun. For a full breakdown of this interaction, see: Stun § Interaction with cold damage crits.
Cast when Stunned Support | Cold critical strikes | Builds that want to be reliably stunned would ideally want at least one of the following:
