Chain Hook of Trarthus | Description | Chain Hook of Trarthus is a melee attack skill that deals a hit to enemies in a cone in front of the user and attaches a chain to them. Hitting an enemy attached to the chain grants rage. When the chain breaks, it deals damage in an area around the target. Requires a one handed axe , mace , sceptre , or sword.
Chain Hook of Trarthus | Description | It is a Trarthan version of Chain Hook.
Chain Hook of Trarthus | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this Trarthan version is not a movement skill. The skill can hit multiple targets and it attaches a chain to hit enemies. Instead of granting rage on attack hit, chained enemies grant rage to the user when hit. The chain can break when the enemy moves far enough from the user, dealing area attack damage around them.
Chain Hook of Trarthus | Skill functions and interactions | Attaching a chain does not count as a hit or deal damage (for example The Writhing Jar worms are not destroyed when attaching the hook). Because of this, The Writhing Jar , Squirming Terror , and The Hateful Accuser can be used to spawn additional targets to chain onto.
Chain Hook of Trarthus | Skill functions and interactions | Already chained targets cannot be targeted again by chains, with the exception of Repeat or having multiple chains applied nearly at the same time. Chains cannot be broken by repeatedly using the skill. However, it is possible to break chains without moving out of range by attempting to chain more targets than the maximum number of chains.
Chain Hook of Trarthus | Skill functions and interactions | Repeats : Each additional Repeat (e.g. Multistrike Support , Echoing Shrine , Fatal Flourish ) allows you to apply an additional chain to the same target in a single use of an attack. [1]
Chain Hook of Trarthus | Skill functions and interactions | Line of Sight : If the chain is broken with no direct line of sight between you and the target, the attack will deal no damage (e.g. behind a wall or a very thick boulder). [2]
Chain Hook of Trarthus | Skill functions and interactions | General's Cry : After a mirage throws a chain, they'll disappear. The chain will linger in the air for about 5 seconds and then break. Summoning another set of mirages causes the previous chains to break instantly.
Chain Hook of Trarthus | Skill functions and interactions | Strategy : To have multiple mirage chains to attach to one target, they need to attack nearly at the same time which can be achieved by having a slow attack speed. [3]
Chain Hook of Trarthus | Skill functions and interactions | Two Different Attack Animations : This skill retains the two different attack animations based on the distance between you and an enemy from the original gem. 120% of base attack speed from gem description is applied only towards to the melee animation.

Chain Support | Description | Chain Support is a support gem that causes projectiles created by support skills to chain.
Chain Support | Mechanics | Chain Support also works with non-projectile skills that can chain, which are currently Orb of Storms , Dark Pact , Tempest Shield , Wild Strike , Arc , Exsanguinate , and Vaal Arc.
Chain Support | Mechanics | Chain Support causes any projectiles created by the linked skills to chain when they hit an enemy. When this happens, the projectile targets another enemy in range and changes direction to fly towards second enemy. It will continue to chain until all the chains are used up. This gem adds 2 to the number of times a projectile can chain.

Chance to Flee Support | Infobox | Support Icon: z Level: (1-20). Requires Level 8. Supports any skill that hits enemies. Supported Skills have (25-44)% chance to Cause Monsters to Flee Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Supported Skills have (1-20)% chance to Cause Monsters to Flee. This is a Support Gem. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 8
Chance to Flee Support | Description | Chance to Flee Support is a support gem that grants supported skills a chance to cause monsters to flee when they are hit by those skills.
Chance to Flee Support | Mechanics | Enemies hit by the supported skill will have a Chance to Flee; they will attempt to run to a random point somewhere away from the character until they reach a certain point, are interrupted, or are unable to reach their selected point and give up. If a fleeing enemy becomes stunned , their fleeing behaviour will be interrupted.
Chance to Flee Support | Mechanics | Magic monsters have a 50% chance to avoid fleeing for any reason, and rare monsters are immune to this gem's effects.
Chance to Flee Support | Mechanics | Some monsters naturally flee when they are burning. This includes apes , humanoids and sea witches.
Chance to Flee Support | Mechanics | This support gem has limited usage, as groups of normal and magic monsters are usually not dangerous enough to threaten a character. Causing a monster to flee often increases the amount of effort required to kill them, as packs can scatter in different directions lowering the efficiency of attacks and spells that target an area. The gem may benefit characters using the far Shot keyword, or characters who cause bleeding and want to ensure weak targets still take the increased damage rate caused by movement. Characters seeking increased bleeding damage might be better served by Chance to Aggravate Bleeding.
Chance to Flee Support | Item acquisition | Chance to Flee Support can drop anywhere.

Chance to Flee Support | Vendor reward | This item can be bought at the listed NPC vendors , after completing the following quests:

Clarity | Description | Clarity is an aura that grants mana regeneration to the caster and their allies.
Clarity | Skill functions and interactions | At level 20, Clarity's mana regeneration is equivalent to the natural mana regeneration of 1886 mana.
Clarity | Skill functions and interactions | When the player has a large amount of life but little mana and needs both the regeneration and other mana-reserving skills, linking this with Arrogance Support might be a consideration. Beware the high reservation multiplier, however.
Clarity | Skill functions and interactions | For more information about auras in general, see aura.
Clarity | Skill functions and interactions | As Clarity reserves a flat amount of mana (whereas most other auras reserve a percentage), it can often be used at a low level without overly compromising the mana available for active skills.

Cleave of Rage | Description | Cleave of Rage is a melee area attack that deals damage in a frontal 180-degree arc, granting rage on hit. This skill requires dual-wielding both an axe and a sword and simultaneously deals damage with both weapons.
Cleave of Rage | Description | It is a transfigured version of Cleave.
Cleave of Rage | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version does not have a radius bonus scaling with level. Instead, it grants Rage on hit and scales area of effect with Rage. It also requires dual wielding a sword and an axe. Quality bonus grants additional Rage gain on hit instead.
Cleave of Rage | Skill functions and interactions | Rigwald's Command and Rigwald's Savagery are a set of items that pairs thematically with this skill. Rigwald's Savagery or a Psychotic Axe and The Saviour is also another synergistic combination.

Close Combat Support | Description | Close Combat Support is a support gem which grants the supported attack more melee damage based on how close the user is to the enemy. It also causes the skill to grant Combat Rush on hit, a buff which grants the next travel skill used within 2 seconds more attack speed. This support gem restricts the skill to swords and axes.
Close Combat Support | Skill functions and interactions | The "up to x% more damage" stat on Close Combat support (and the matching stat on the Slayer's Impact notable) applies the full damage bonus to targets up to a distance of 1.5 metres from you, then linearly scales down the bonus to 0 at a distance of 4 metres. [1]
Close Combat Support | Skill functions and interactions | Close Combat appears to be a buff, scaling with Ichimonji , but lacks the typical green-bordered icon.

Cluster Traps Support | Infobox | Trap , Support Icon: L Level: (1-20) Cost & Reservation Multiplier: 140%. Requires Level 38. Supports traps skills, making them throw extra traps randomly around the targeted location. Supported Skills throw up to 2 additional Traps Supported Skills throw Traps randomly around targeted location Supported Skills can have up to 5 additional Traps placed at a time Supported Skills deal (61-55)% less Damage Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Supported Skills have (1-20)% increased Trap Trigger Area of Effect. This is a Support Gem. It does not grant a bonus to your character, but to skills in sockets connected to it. Place into an item socket connected to a socket containing the Skill Gem you wish to augment. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 38
Cluster Traps Support | Description | Cluster Traps is a support gem that changes the laying behaviour of any supported traps. Instead of throwing a single trap, three traps are thrown at once, but these traps will be placed randomly in a radius around the targeted location and do less damage than normal. The maximum number of traps of the linked skill is also increased by five.
Cluster Traps Support | Skill Functions and Interactions | Multiple traps - When a trap skill is supported by Multiple Traps Support and Cluster Traps, the resulting trap spread will throw a total of 5 traps in the same random spread as Cluster Trap alone.
Cluster Traps Support | Skill Functions and Interactions | Throwing function - Cluster Trap skills can be thrown to locations of different heights, across gaps, and, unlike Multiple Traps Support, do not have a minimum throwing range. When paired with Multiple Traps, the rules for trap placement follow Cluster Trap's throwing patterns.
Cluster Traps Support | Skill Functions and Interactions | Cluster radius - Traps thrown with Cluster Trap will always land within 20 units of the targeted enemy or location. Notably, this means with enough % increased Trap Trigger Area of Effect it is possible to increase your traps' trigger radius enough that they will always trigger against a stationary target. For standard sized enemies (radius of 2 units), this is achieved with a trap trigger radius of 18, a total of 224% increased Trap Trigger Area of Effect. The unique jewel Hair Trigger may be useful or even necessary to achieve this much trigger area.
Cluster Traps Support | Item acquisition | Cluster Traps Support can drop anywhere.

Deals (34-1717) to (51-2575) Cold Damage Base duration is 5.00 seconds Deals (28.6-2259.5) Base Cold Damage per second Modifiers to Spell Damage apply to this Skill's Damage Over Time effect Burst Base radius is 1.5 metres Chilled Ground Base initial radius is 1.5 metres Chilled Ground Base final radius is 3 metres 25% chance to gain a Frenzy Charge when an Enemy Dies while in this Skill's Area Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: (1-20)% more Effect of Chill. Cold Snap | Description | Cold Snap is a spell that deals cold damage to enemies in an area, leaving chilled ground that expands and deals cold damage over time. It has a cooldown that can be bypassed with Frenzy Charges. It also has a 25% chance to grant a frenzy charge when you kill an enemy within its area of effect.
Cold Snap | Skill functions and interactions | Modifiers to Spell Damage apply to this Skill's Damage Over Time effect : this modifier allows spell damage modifiers to modify the skill's natural cold damage over time. It will not apply to other types of damage over time, such as Ignite.
Cold Snap | Skill functions and interactions | Chilled Ground : Cold Snap creates an expanding chilled ground that causes enemies to be chilled. While its base effect will scale with the player or skill's modifiers to cold and non-damaging ailments, it is not considered a chill inflicted by the player, limiting the use of certain stats. (See chilling area.)
Cold Snap | Skill functions and interactions | Trigger : Even if the appropriate charges are available, Cold Snap's cooldown cannot be bypassed when triggered. [1]

Combustion Support | Infobox | Fire , Support Icon: i Level: (1-20) Cost & Reservation Multiplier: 130%. Requires Level 8. Supports any skill that hits enemies. Supported Skills deal (10-19)% more Fire Damage Supported Skills have 25% chance to Ignite Enemies Ignited by Supported Skills have -10% to Fire Resistance Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Supported Skills deal (0.5-10)% increased Fire Damage. Combustion Support | Description | Combustion is a support gem that grants supported skills that grants more fire damage and chance to ignite the enemy. Enemies ignited by the supported skill have reduced fire resistance for the duration of the ignite applying Combustion.
Combustion Support | Skill functions and interactions | Note that even though this skill 'Supports any skill that hits enemies', by default only fire damage can ignite. Therefore it by default only helps skills dealing fire damage, see more on ignite , including uniques that enable other elements to ignite. The fire damage could be supplied by gear or for minions for instance a jewel with 'Minions deal additional Fire Damage'.
Combustion Support | Skill functions and interactions | The Chance to Ignite an enemy given by this gem is independent of the chance to ignite an enemy as a result of a critical strike. The overall probability that an enemy will be ignited is:
Combustion Support | Skill functions and interactions | ChanceToIgnite = 1 − ( 1 − SkillCritChance ) ∗ ( 1 − SkillIgniteChance ) \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}{\text{ChanceToIgnite}}=1-(1-{\text{SkillCritChance}})*(1-{\text{SkillIgniteChance}})
Combustion Support | Skill functions and interactions | It is worth noting that as opposed to chance to critical strike, which is checked once for each cast of the skill, the chance to ignite is checked separately for each individual hit. For example, a hit from Consecrated Path linked with Combustion that hits two enemies at once could potentially ignite one enemy, but not the other.
Combustion Support | Skill functions and interactions | The chance to Ignite from this gem will stack additively with chance to Ignite modifiers from other sources, such as from the Ash, Frost and Storm notable passive skill, or the stat of Firestorm.
Combustion Support | Skill functions and interactions | The normal rules for burning apply, linking this gem to a skill that deals no fire damage is pointless; unless the user is igniting enemies via cold damage skills with The Three Dragons or Hrimburn , or via lightning damage using Stormfire.

Concentrated Effect Support | Description | Concentrated Effect Support is a support gem that increases the area damage dealt by supported skills, but reduces their area of effect.
Concentrated Effect Support | Skill functions and interactions | The Less Area of Effect modifier will affect any area of effect produced by the linked skill, even if they do not deal area damage. The reduction will therefore apply to curses , auras , Elemental Proliferation Support , and so forth.
Concentrated Effect Support | Skill functions and interactions | The effect of Less Area of Effect to the actual radius of a skill results in a reduction of only 16.3% (rounded off) versus the full 30% less radius it used to have in older patches.

Controlled Blaze Support | Infobox | Fire , Support , Melee , Attack Icon: Z Level: (1-20) Cost & Reservation Multiplier: 130%. Requires Level 31. Supports melee attack skills. Supported Skills have (10-19)% chance to Ignite Each Supported Skill deals (10-14)% more Damage with Ignite for each Ignite inflicted with it Recently, up to a maximum of (230-364)% Each Supported Skill deals 3% less Damage for each Ignite inflicted with it Recently, up to a maximum of (69-78)% Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Supported Skills have (0.5-10)% chance to Ignite. Controlled Blaze Support | Description | Controlled Blaze is a support gem that causes melee attacks to have an increased chance to ignite and for each Ignite inflicted with the Skill recently, it deals less Damage but more Ignite Damage.
Controlled Blaze Support | Skill functions and interactions | Controlled Blaze will count all hits that have applied ignite, for each monster that received an ignite and any ignite that has been re-applied to an already ignited enemy.
Controlled Blaze Support | Skill functions and interactions | Due to the fact that more and less modifiers are multiplicative, Controlled Blaze will provide less DPS at high number of ignites applied recently. Depending on the gem level, the number of ignites you need to inflict to reach its highest damage bonus is 12 or 13. [2] This results in a "sweet spot" of 3 to 3.25 attacks per second (assuming no other targets); however, it is better to exceed this value to account for time not spent attacking while dodging/repositioning. That said, in general, you can be about 4 ignites off from the optimal range before you start experiencing a >5% ignite DPS loss.

Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 5
Conversion Trap | Description | Conversion Trap throws a trap that causes the enemy that triggered it to temporarily become allies with the user.
Conversion Trap | Skill Functions and Interactions | Most normal , magic , and rare rarity monsters can be converted into temporary allies using this skill, but unique monsters cannot. Magic and rare monsters that are converted keep all of their monster modifier effects, including aura like effects. Converted monsters also retain all buffs and debuffs that were present on the monster prior to conversion.
Conversion Trap | Skill Functions and Interactions | Duration : Modifiers to skill duration affect the duration of enemy's conversion state. Modifiers to buff and debuff duration or expiry rate do not apply.
Conversion Trap | Skill Functions and Interactions | Hostile totems can be turned into allies using Conversion Trap. Auras from the totem will affect the player and a Shockwave Totem will attack the player's enemies.
Conversion Trap | Skill Functions and Interactions | Temporary allies : Monsters converted by Conversion Trap are not considered minions and do not scale with minion-specific stats or minion support gems.
Conversion Trap | Skill Functions and Interactions | In PvP content, Conversion Trap can not affect players; however, their minions can be converted. [1]
Conversion Trap | Skill Functions and Interactions | Warriors found in the Trial of the Ancestors cannot be converted.

Corrupting Cry Support | Description | Corrupting Cry Support is a support gem that causes Corrupted Blood to be inflicted on enemies in the supported warcry ’s radius or attacks exerted by the supported warcry. The supported warcry has less area of effect and costs life instead of mana to use (with a very high cost multiplier).
Corrupting Cry Support | Skill functions and interactions | Corrupted Blood
Corrupting Cry Support | Skill functions and interactions | Corrupting Cry applies 4 stacks of Corrupted Blood on nearby enemies when you warcry , and 1 stack on exerted attacks, up to a total of 10 from all sources. Corrupted Blood debuff duration refreshes when a stack is gained, even from other sources such as Corrupting Fever.
Corrupting Cry Support | Skill functions and interactions | Corrupted Blood duration can be modified by skill duration or Warcry Duration modifiers. Warcry Buff Effect does not affect the damage of Corrupted Blood.
Corrupting Cry Support | Skill functions and interactions | Support Gems : while Corrupting Cry is not a skill gem and cannot be supported by other gems, gems supporting the linked warcry will modify the Corrupted Blood damage. Corrupting Cry modifies the properties of the linked warcry, enabling supports that would not otherwise be able to support the warcry to be linked. For example, Enduring Cry cannot normally be supported by Brutality Support , but linking Corrupting Cry adds a damage component to the warcry, satisfying the 'any skill that deals damage' requirement of that support gem. The properties of the supported warcry also affect which supports can be linked; for example the more Damage provided by Energy Leech Support can increase the Corrupted Blood damage, but as Corrupting Cry does not add a hit component to the warcry, it can only be linked to a warcry that is itself able to hit enemies, i.e. Infernal Cry.
Corrupting Cry Support | Item acquisition | Corrupting Cry Support can drop anywhere.

Crackling Lance | Description | Crackling Lance is a spell that fires a wide beam that deals lightning damage. Casting the spell while standing still builds intensity , increasing the damage of the beam and reducing its spread.
Crackling Lance | Skill functions and interactions | As with all spells which can Intensify , the caster cannot gain a stack of Intensity when this spell is repeated (e.g. Spell Echo Support ), triggered , or cast by a totem.
Crackling Lance | Skill functions and interactions | Area of Effect: Crackling Lance's area of effect extends invisibly in a radius around both the central beam and the branching smaller beams. Increases and decreases to Area of Effect modify the width of that area, and thus the effective coverage of the skill.

Creeping Frost | Description | Creeping Frost is a projectile cold spell that launches a frozen skull at a targeted area. The projectile will explode when it reaches the targeted area, or if it comes into contact with an enemy. This explosion causes initial damage and creates a Chilling Area that slows and deals cold damage over time to any enemies that enter it. This chilling area will move in the direction of nearby enemies.
Creeping Frost | Skill functions and interactions | Creeping Frost's chilling area is not considered Chilled Ground. Enemies affected by Creeping Frost's chilling area counts as being Chilled by you.

Cruelty Support | Infobox | Support , Duration Icon: Y Level: (1-20) Cost & Reservation Multiplier: 140%. Requires Level 18. Supports any skill that hits enemies. Minions cannot gain Cruelty. Place into an item socket connected to a socket containing the Skill Gem you wish to augment. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 18
Cruelty Support | Description | Cruelty Support is a support gem.
Cruelty Support | Skill functions and interactions | Hits from active skills supported by this gem grant the Cruelty buff. Per its reminder text, Cruelty makes all Skills Supported by Cruelty Support deal up to 40% more Damage over Time, based on the amount of Damage in the Hit which grants it. The buff affects all skills supported by any Cruelty Support. Damaging ailments inflicted by a hit that grants Cruelty are not affected by that instance of Cruelty, because damage calculation occurs before on-hit buffs are gained.
Cruelty Support | Skill functions and interactions | Proxies cannot gain or grant the player Cruelty. Damage over time from a proxy's skill can benefit from Cruelty Support if the player obtains a Cruelty buff from a different skill that deals damage directly. [1] (This is likely not an option for totems due to Ancestral Bond.)
Cruelty Support | Skill functions and interactions | Minion skills that also deal damage directly (e.g. Dominating Blow ) can be supported by Cruelty, but minions cannot gain the Cruelty buff, nor can they benefit from a buff on the player. Skills that summon minions but do not deal damage directly cannot be supported by Cruelty at all.
Cruelty Support | Item acquisition | Cruelty Support can drop anywhere.

Cyclone gains stages during channelling that increase its base radius and attack speed but lessens movement speed. The player can move while channelling Cyclone.
Cyclone of Tumult | Description | It is a transfigured version of Cyclone.
Cyclone of Tumult | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version has a slower attack speed, higher cost , and lacks a base movement speed penalty. The skill gains stages as you channel, granting more attack speed and radius but also less movement speed per stage. [1] Quality grants more attack speed per stage.

Damage on Full Life Support | Description | Damage on Full Life is a support gem that grants more damage made with the supported attacks , but only when the player (or minion) is on full life. Being even one life below the maximum will nullify the effect, unless the player uses something like Life Mastery to lower the threshold for "full life".
Damage on Full Life Support | Skill functions and interactions | The gem only improves the supported skill's damage if the player is on full life. If the player is affected by damage over time , such as from Blood Rage , the player can remain at full life by offsetting the damage with enough regeneration or leech. [1]
Damage on Full Life Support | Skill functions and interactions | Using the keystone Chaos Inoculation will ensure the player is always on full life.
Damage on Full Life Support | Skill functions and interactions | Blood Magic : If the supported skill uses Blood Magic, then using the skill will make the player no longer at full life. However, if the player can recover to full life before the attack skill hits an enemy, then the more Damage will still apply.
Damage on Full Life Support | Skill functions and interactions | When this gem supports a totem , the totem will deal more Damage with attack skills as long as the player, not the totem, has full life. [2]
Damage on Full Life Support | Item acquisition | Damage on Full Life Support can drop anywhere.

Dark Pact | Infobox | Minion , Spell , AoE , Chaining , Chaos , Nova Level: (1-20) Cost: (7-13) Mana Cast Time: 0.50 sec Critical Strike Chance: 7.00%. Requires Level 28. This spell removes some life from one of your Skeleton minions near you or the targeted location to deal chaos damage in an area around it. This effect will chain to your other nearby skeletons. If you have no skeletons near you or the targeted location, it will sacrifice your own life to deal damage instead. Deals (24-241) to (36-362) Chaos Damage Sacrifices 12% of Skeleton's Life to deal that much Chaos Damage Chains +2 Times Uses your Life if no Skeletons in range (56-136)% more Damage with Hits and Ailments if using your Life Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Sacrifices +(0.1-2)% of Skeleton's Life to deal that much Chaos Damage. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 28
Dark Pact | Description | Dark Pact is a chaos spell that removes some life from one of your Summon Skeletons minions near you or the targeted location to deal chaos damage in an area around it. This effect will chain to your other nearby skeletons. If you have no skeletons near you or the targeted location, it will sacrifice your own life to deal damage instead.
Dark Pact | Skill Functions and Interactions | Sacrifice life : Dark Pact sacrifices 12% of the player's maximum life in a manner similar to spending life with the Lifetap Support or the Blood Magic Keystone. This life loss is not considered damage and cannot be mitigated or trigger Cast when Damage Taken Support. Dark Pact cannot kill a player as it cannot lower life below 1. If 12% of maximum life cannot be removed (e.g. maximum life is 1 due to Chaos Inoculation ), the spell can still be used and will lower the player to 1 life, but will lose part or all of the additional base damage scaling from life. [1] The more damage multiplier will always take effect when not casting on a skeleton, even if no life is actually sacrificed.
Dark Pact | Skill Functions and Interactions | Life scaling : Dark Pact deals additional base damage equal to the amount of life removed from the player or skeleton. If used on a skeleton, minion life modifiers and Minion Life Support increase the damage dealt when cast on skeletons. The skill tooltip does not factor the base damage from sacrificed life into its calculation as it is conditional. [2]
Dark Pact | Skill Functions and Interactions | Proxy : Proxies will attempt to target an allied skeleton if one is nearby. If not, the proxy will use Dark Pact sacrificing its own life; totems can scale this with totem life modifiers. Proxies will get the more damage bonus from using life if not targeting an allied skeleton. [3]
Dark Pact | Skill Functions and Interactions | Minion interactions : Dark Pact is unable to kill your skeletons directly as the skill will not sacrifice life below 1. However, it can trigger Minion Instability by lowering the life of your Skeleton minions to low life. Despite having the minion tag, there are no minion-specific support gems that can support Dark Pact, and modifiers that affect minion stats do not affect Dark Pact. However, modifiers such as Overseer's Overseer's Prefix +2 to Level of all Minion Skill Gems found on helmets or modifiers that increase socketed Minion gem level, (e.g. The Baron , Chober Chaber , Femurs of the Saints ) will increase the level of Dark Pact. Using Dark Pact is considered using a minion skill, even if not cast on a skeleton.
Dark Pact | Skill Functions and Interactions | Chaining : If targeting a skeleton, Dark Pact will chain from the skeleton to other nearby skeletons based on your number of chains. Each skeleton used to chain Dark Pact will lose 12% of its maximum life; this is not considered taking damage. Additional chains can be added to help cover a larger area or return a chain to a previously targeted Skeleton using Chain Support , Ricochet from Deadeye , or items such as Gloomfang. Dark Pact is not a projectile and is not affected by projectile speed or other projectile modifiers, including chains specific to projectiles (e.g. Snakepit ).
Dark Pact | Skill Functions and Interactions | Divine Shrine : As Dark Pact sacrifices life rather than causing the player to take damage, being affected by a Divine Shrine will not prevent life loss.
Dark Pact | Skill Functions and Interactions | Nova targeting : Dark Pact damages in a nova around either the player or the targeted skeletons. This behaviour can be altered by Astral Projector or Runegraft of the Novamark only if the skill is cast by sacrificing player life. Novae around skeletons are unaffected.
Dark Pact | Item acquisition | Dark Pact can drop anywhere.
Dark Pact | Recipes | Dark Pact can be created from the following recipes:
Dark Pact | Vendor reward | This item can be bought at the listed NPC vendors , after completing the following quests:
Dark Pact | Related helmet enchantments | The following helmet enchantments affect Dark Pact.
Dark Pact | Related helmet enchantments | Note that helmet enchantments are no longer obtainable since version 3.23.0 and can only be obtained in permanent leagues as already enchanted items from before this patch.
Dark Pact of Trarthus | Infobox | Spell , AoE , Chaos , Nova Level: (1-20) Cost: (7-13) Mana Cast Time: 0.50 sec Critical Strike Chance: 7.00%. Requires Level 28. This spell deals chaos damage in an area around you. Casting this spell will cause you to gain Ruin. If this spell inflicts your seventh Ruin, it will sacrifice a large portion of your life to deal extra chaos damage based on the amount sacrificed, and remove all Ruin from you. Deals (24-241) to (36-362) Chaos Damage 50% less Damage with Ailments Gain one Ruin on use Reaching seven Ruin will Sacrifice 50% of your Life to deal 500% of Sacrificed Life as Chaos Damage Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: (1-20)% chance to gain an additional Ruin on use. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop restricted Drop level: 28 Mercenaries Can be obtained from reward chests that contain gems in Heist and Expedition Logbook.
Dark Pact of Trarthus | Description | Dark Pact of Trarthus is a chaos spell which deals chaos damage in an area around the user and grants a stack of Ruin. Upon gaining seven stacks of Ruin, it will sacrifice half of your maximum life to deal extra base chaos damage based on the amount of life sacrificed.
Dark Pact of Trarthus | Description | It is a Trarthan version of Dark Pact.
Dark Pact of Trarthus | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this Trarthan version has has no interaction with Skeletons, does not chain , and does not sacrifice your life on every skill use, and gains no bonuses for spending your own life. Instead, it grants stacks of Ruin on use (which count towards the Ruin mechanic from Ultimatum ), stacking up to 7 before sacrificing half of your life and dealing massive chaos damage based on the sacrificed life. It inherently deals less ailment damage.
Dark Pact of Trarthus | Changes from default version | Visually, Dark Pact of Trarthus has a stygian-themed blue colour instead of purple, and generates a sigil over the user's head tracking the number of Ruin stacks. If used by a proxy , it will appear over their location instead.
Dark Pact of Trarthus | Skill Functions and Interactions | Sacrifice life : Dark Pact of Trarthus sacrifices 50% of the player's maximum life upon reaching 7 Ruin stacks in a manner similar to spending life with the Lifetap Support or the Blood Magic Keystone. This life loss is not considered damage and cannot be mitigated or trigger Cast when Damage Taken Support. Dark Pact of Trarthus cannot kill a player as it cannot lower life below 1. If 50% of maximum life cannot be removed (e.g. maximum life is 1 due to Chaos Inoculation ), the spell can still be used and will lower the player to 1 life, but will lose part or all of the additional base damage scaling from life. [1]
Dark Pact of Trarthus | Skill Functions and Interactions | Life scaling : Dark Pact of Trarthus deals additional base damage equal to the amount of life removed from the player. The skill tooltip does not factor the base damage from sacrificed life into its calculation as it is conditional. [2]
Dark Pact of Trarthus | Skill Functions and Interactions | Proxy : Proxies will use Dark Pact of Trarthus sacrificing their own life; totems can scale this with totem life modifiers. Each proxy has independent Ruin stacks.
Dark Pact of Trarthus | Skill Functions and Interactions | Divine Shrine : As Dark Pact sacrifices life rather than causing the player to take damage, being affected by a Divine Shrine will not prevent life loss.
Dark Pact of Trarthus | Skill Functions and Interactions | Ruin : Ruin stacks granted by Dark Pact of Trarthus are a different debuff from Ultimatum 's Ruin. However, the two types of Ruins are summed and cause failure of Ultimatums if they exceed 7. This is an intended interaction. [3] It may also interact with Inscribed Ultimatums and Valdo's Maps with Ruin modifiers.
Dark Pact of Trarthus | Item acquisition | Dark Pact of Trarthus has restrictions on where or how it can drop.
Dark Pact of Trarthus | Item acquisition | Can be obtained from reward chests that contain gems in Heist and Expedition Logbook.
Dark Pact of Trarthus | Related helmet enchantments | The following helmet enchantments affect Dark Pact.
Dark Pact of Trarthus | Related helmet enchantments | Note that helmet enchantments are no longer obtainable since version 3.23.0 and can only be obtained in permanent leagues as already enchanted items from before this patch.

Deadly Ailments Support | Description | Deadly Ailments is a support gem that grants more damage with ailments , but causes the hit to deal less damage.
Deadly Ailments Support | Skill Functions and Interactions | Supported Skills deal 80% less Damage with Hits : Because damaging ailment DPS is calculated independently from hit damage, this modifier to hit damage does not interact with damaging ailments inflicted by the hit. For example, consider an ignite inflicted by a hit of 100 base damage:
Deadly Ailments Support | Skill Functions and Interactions | While the hit damage penalty does not affect ailment DPS, it may impede hit-based mechanics such as leech , stun , Cruelty Support , or non-damaging ailments such as Shock.

Decay Support | Infobox | Chaos , Support Icon: D Level: (1-20) Cost & Reservation Multiplier: 120%. Requires Level 38. Supports any skill that hits enemies. Supported Skills inflict Decay on Hit, dealing (162-1230) Chaos Damage per second for 8 seconds Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Supported Skills deal (0.5-10)% increased Chaos Damage. Place into an item socket connected to a socket containing the Skill Gem you wish to augment. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 38
Decay Support | Description | Decay Support is a support gem that makes hits from supported skills inflict Decay , a chaos damage over time debuff.
Decay Support | Mechanics and interactions | Decay is a chaos damage over time debuff. It is not affected by modifiers to skill effect duration [1] and does not add a duration to supported skills.
Decay Support | Mechanics and interactions | Since Decay is a debuff, only one instance will ever apply at a time - it does not stack. The highest damage active instance of the debuff will apply. Reapplying the debuff will refresh the duration.
Decay Support | Damage scaling | Decay damage is considered damage from the supported skill, and is affected by all relevant modifiers of that skill, including global stats from the player, stats from support gems , and stats inherent to the active skill. Examples of applicable modifiers are #% more/less/increased/reduced Damage/Chaos Damage/Damage over Time , but also skill-specific modifiers that affect all of a skill's damage, such as Storm Burst 's Exploding Orbs deal 75% of Skill Damage for each 0.4 seconds of remaining Duration. (This does not apply to Blade Vortex. [2] )
Decay Support | Damage scaling | If Decay is applied by a minion , trap , mine or totem , it will be affected by modifiers relevant for those entities, such as Blink Arrow 's Minions deal #% more Damage.
Decay Support | Damage scaling | Decay is not an ailment , so modifiers to "damage with hits and ailments" will never apply.
Decay Support | Item acquisition | Decay Support can drop anywhere.

Decay Support | Vendor reward | This item can be bought at the listed NPC vendors , after completing the following quests:

Defiance Banner | Description | Defiance Banner is a banner aura skill. After gaining Valour from melee combat, it can be activated, consuming all Valour and placing a banner that buffs the user and allies within an area with increased movement speed , additional chance to suppress spell damage, and a flat subtraction to damage taken from suppressed spell hits. The effect of the aura is multiplied based on the amount of Valour consumed.
Defiance Banner | Skill functions and interactions | When the banner is placed with 50 Valour, the buff provides 48% increased Movement Speed to You and Allies , +48% chance to Suppress Spell Damage to You and Allies , and -72 Damage taken of each Damage Type from Suppressed Hits to You and Allies.

Desecrate | Infobox | Spell , AoE , Duration , Chaos Level: (1-20) Cost: (11-26) Mana Cast Time: 0.60 sec. Requires Level 16. Desecrates the ground, spawning corpses based on monsters in the current area and dealing chaos damage over time to enemies. If you are using the Raise Spectre skill there is a chance to spawn spectral corpses matching your most recently raised Spectres. Spectral corpses cannot be interacted with except by Minion skills. Deals (8.2-294) Base Chaos Damage per second Base duration is 4.00 seconds Spawns 5 corpses Spawned corpses' level matches the area level, up to a maximum of Level (20-80) Maximum of 10 corpses allowed Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: (1-20)% increased Cast Speed. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 16
Desecrate | Description | Desecrate is a spell that summons corpses and a patch of desecrated ground , which deals chaos damage over time to enemies in the area.
Desecrate | Skill functions and interactions | Desecrate's pool of corpses depends on whether the player has socketed and previously used Raise Spectre , as well as the player's current area:
Desecrate | Skill functions and interactions | When Raise Spectre is socketed, and the skill has been used before, Desecrate has a 15% chance to spawn a Spectral Corpse instead (100% in hideout), which can only be interacted with using minion skills such as Bone Offering or Raise Zombie. They cannot be detonated, consumed, or otherwise affected by non-minion skills other than by overriding the corpse limit by using Desecrate or Death Walk.
Desecrate | Skill functions and interactions | A " spectre bank " is an internal list of the 10 most recent monsters raised by a Raise Spectre gem. This number is independent of your maximum spectre limit. This list is retained even upon exiting the area, removing the Raise Spectre gem, after your Spectres die or are unsummoned, or logging out. There is currently no way to clear a spectre bank; it can only be ignored by removing the Raise Spectre gem.
Desecrate | Skill functions and interactions | Spectral Corpses will only be created if at least one monster is in your spectre bank. Before 10 or more different monsters are saved in the spectre bank, each additional spectre saved dilutes the chance for the Spectral Corpse to be a specific spectre. After 10 or more spectres are saved, the chance does not get further diluted, and the oldest monster saved will be removed from the spectre bank.
Desecrate | Skill functions and interactions | To share spectres between players, the players should join the same hideout area. After doing so, either player will be able to spawn spectral corpses of other players in the area. [3] In non-hideout areas, the player who owns the desired spectre must be the one to use Desecrate for other players.
Desecrate | Skill functions and interactions | Because Spectral Corpses replace detonatable corpses, it is not recommended to have Raise Spectre socketed if the player has ever raised a spectre on that character, and wants to detonate corpses for damage.
Desecrate | Skill functions and interactions | Note that Raise Spectre of Transience , or the regular Raise Spectre while also equipping Soul's Wick , does not save spectres into the spectre bank.
Desecrate | Skill functions and interactions | Monsters from corpse items and certain other monsters such as Knitted Horrors cannot be added to the spectre bank.
Desecrate | Item acquisition | Desecrate can drop anywhere.
Desecrate | Recipes | Desecrate can be created from the following recipes:
Desecrate | Quest reward | This item is given as a quest reward for the following quests:
Desecrate | Vendor reward | This item can be bought at the listed NPC vendors , after completing the following quests:

Base duration is (8.00-9.90) seconds Linked target has +(30-49)% to Critical Strike Multiplier Linked target's Main Hand Critical Strike Chance is equal to yours Link breaks if target leaves range or line of sight for 4 seconds Maximum 1 Link from any source per target You die if Linked target dies Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Base duration is (0.075-1.5) seconds. Destructive Link | Description | Destructive Link is a Link skill that targets an allied player to apply a buff which links you to them for a duration. While linked, they use your critical strike chance for main hand hits. If the target dies while linked, you will also die. This skill cannot be used by Totems, Traps, or Mines.
Destructive Link | Skill functions and interactions | This skill replaces the linked target's main hand critical strike chance with yours; this uses the value in the player's character sheet which is affected by modifiers to critical strike chance, not just the base critical strike chance of the weapon. Tecrod's Gaze , for example, will affect this value.
Destructive Link | Skill functions and interactions | The critical strike chance of the linked target cannot be further modified through stats on the linked target. For example, a linked player with Marylene's Fallacy will not have their critical strike chance lowered by the item.
Destructive Link | Item acquisition | Destructive Link can drop anywhere.

Discharge of Misery | Description | Discharge of Misery is a spell that consumes all of the player's charges (specifically frenzy charges , power charges and endurance charges ) to deal elemental damage per charge consumed in an area around them.
Discharge of Misery | Description | It is a transfigured version of Discharge.
Discharge of Misery | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version has lower damage and damage effectiveness and smaller area of effect but also has no cooldown and no penalty to ailment damage.
Discharge of Misery | Skill functions and interactions | It replaced the effects granted by Endless Misery , but lacks the Discharge Cooldown is 250 ms modifier which can override the cooldown of other trigger cooldowns, causing this gem to lack the previous synergy with the Trigger a Socketed Spell when you Use a Skill, with a # second Cooldown crafted modifier.
Discharge of Misery | Item acquisition | Discharge of Misery has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

Divine Ire of Disintegration | Description | Divine Ire of Disintegration is a channelling skill that gathers energy while channelling to build up stages. Releasing the channel fires a beam of energy in front of the character, dealing physical damage converted to lightning damage.
Divine Ire of Disintegration | Description | It is a transfigured version of Divine Ire.
Divine Ire of Disintegration | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version has more damage and damage effectiveness and beam width, but has less scaling per stage and does not damage enemies around you while channelling. It has a slower cast time and higher mana cost. It also lacks the burst around the player on releasing the channel.

Double Strike of Impaling | Description | Double Strike of Impaling is a melee attack that performs two fast attacks on a target enemy with the player's melee weapon(s). It has a chance to impale on hit, and enemies killed by hits with this skill reflect the damage of their impale debuffs to nearby enemies.
Double Strike of Impaling | Description | It is a transfigured version of Double Strike.
Double Strike of Impaling | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version has lower damage effectiveness and has a chance to inflict Impale instead of Bleeding. Killing blows with this skill cause impales on the target to reflect their damage to nearby enemies, similar to Great Old One's Tentacles. Gem quality grants increased impale effect instead.

Double Strike of Momentum | Description | Double Strike of Momentum is a melee attack that performs two fast attacks on a target enemy with the player's melee weapon(s). It gains a stacking attack speed buff while you attack without moving.
Double Strike of Momentum | Description | It is a transfigured version of Double Strike.
Double Strike of Momentum | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version has less damage effectiveness and does not have additional chance to apply Bleeding. Instead, it gains stages at the start of each attack which quickly amplifies attack speed but are lost when you move, somewhat similar to Momentum Support. Gem quality grants more attack speed per stage instead.

Dread Banner | Infobox | AoE , Spell , Duration , Aura Level: (1-20) Cost: (15-19) Mana Cooldown Time: 1.00 sec Can Store 1 Use(s) Cast Time: Instant. Requires Level 24. You must gain Valour from melee combat to use this skill, which will consume all Valour to place a banner with an aura. The aura will be larger and more powerful the more Valour was consumed. The banner lasts for a duration, but will end early if you leave the aura, or use the skill again while it's active. Base duration is 10.00 seconds Base radius is 2.4 metres While you have no placed Banner, you can gain Valour (5-10)% more Area of Effect per Valour consumed (5-10)% more Aura effect per Valour consumed Banner grants +4% Chance to Block Attack Damage to You and Allies Banner causes you and Allies to Recover (9-34) Life when Blocking Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Base duration is (0.1-2) seconds. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 24
Dread Banner | Description | Dread Banner is a banner aura skill. After gaining Valour from melee combat, it can be activated, consuming all Valour and placing a banner that buffs the user and allies within an area with additional chance to block attack damage and life gain on block. The effect of the aura is multiplied based on the amount of Valour consumed.

Elemental Focus Support | Description | Elemental Focus is a support gem. It multiplies the elemental damage of linked skills, at the cost of them being unable to apply elemental status ailments.
Elemental Focus Support | Skill functions and interactions | This support gem has a drawback of disabling the application of any elemental ailments with supported skills, even if it has stats that grant the ability to always inflict ailments. This drawback makes it more difficult to trigger certain effects requiring ailments, forcing the player to lose some of their stats or require the application of ailments through other means, such as Summon Skitterbots or a different skill. Some notable support gems that are affected negatively when linked together with Elemental Focus include:
Elemental Focus Support | Skill functions and interactions | Elemental Focus does not prevent Vortex 's ground effect, Chilled Ground , and Shocked Ground from applying ailments, but does prevent other secondary ailment applications like Wintertide Brand or the chilling areas from Creeping Frost.

Elemental Hit | Description | Elemental Hit is an melee attack skill that randomly deals fire , cold , or lightning damage in an area of effect around the target with each use. That use of Elemental Hit will only deal damage of the chosen element, and has more area of effect against enemies affected by the elemental ailments of that element. The skill deals more damage for each elemental ailment present on enemies.

Elemental Hit of the Spectrum | Description | Elemental Hit of the Spectrum is a ranged attack skill that fires a projectile which deals fire , cold , and lightning damage. Hitting an enemy will cause it to also deal damage in an area of effect around the enemy. The skill deals more damage for each elemental ailment present on enemies. You cannot deal physical or chaos damage with this skill.
Elemental Hit of the Spectrum | Description | It is a transfigured version of Elemental Hit.
Elemental Hit of the Spectrum | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version can only be used with bows and wands and is no longer a Prismatic skill. It does not choose an element or prevent damage of other damage types, and does not gain area of effect based on specific ailments on the enemy. Instead, it deals a smaller amount of added damage of all three elements at once, and has a higher base radius. However, it still cannot deal physical or chaos damage.
Elemental Hit of the Spectrum | Skill functions and interactions | As Elemental Hit of the Spectrum is not a Prismatic skill, it cannot be modified by Combat Focus.
Elemental Hit of the Spectrum | Item acquisition | Elemental Hit of the Spectrum has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

Elemental Weakness | Area mods | Elemental Weakness is one of the curses that can be rolled on tier 6+ maps and vaal side areas (known as "secret_area" in spawn weight table below). The curse affects the entire area and cannot be removed. The debuff applied is not a skill, but has values equivalent to a curse from a level 1 skill gem. Its effect is not modified by increased effect of Modifiers on your (Non-Unique) Maps. It can be mitigated with the upgraded Soul of Yugul or other item modifiers that offer reduced/less effect of curses on you.

Empower Support | Description | Empower is a support gem that increases the gem level of supported active skill gems.
Empower Support | Skill function and interactions | Empower is a drop-only gem that doesn't have any effect at level one and has a level cap of 3. The required experience to reach level 3 is considerably greater than normal gems. Its level cap can be exceeded the same way as other gems.

Endurance Charge on Melee Stun Support | Description | Endurance Charge on Melee Stun is a support gem. When an enemy is stunned by a linked melee skill, the character will gain an endurance charge. Only one endurance charge can be gained per attack use, regardless of how many enemies are stunned. Supported skills also deal more damage per endurance charge and has reduced enemy stun threshold. Only supported skills are affected by the more damage multiplier. Despite stating "supports melee attack skills", the gem does support and provide more damage to skills with both melee and bow gem tags when used by bows, such as Elemental Hit , Frenzy and Puncture.

Enduring Cry | Description | Enduring Cry is a warcry skill that taunts enemies in an area around the character , and grants the user and allies a life regeneration buff and a number of endurance charges , both based on the power of nearby enemies. Unlike most warcry skills, Enduring Cry does not exert your attacks.
Enduring Cry | Skill functions and interactions | Power : Normal Monsters have 1 Power, Magic Monsters 2, Rare Monsters 10, Unique Monsters 20, and Players 5
Enduring Cry | Skill functions and interactions | Duration : Skill Effect Duration , Buff Duration , and Warcry Duration will only affect the duration of the regeneration buff, not the taunt or endurance charges.
Enduring Cry | Skill functions and interactions | Warcry Speed : Warcry Speed will accelerate the animation of enduring cry. Cast Speed and Attack Speed have no effect on warcry skills, as warcry skills are neither attacks nor spells.

Enlighten Support | Description | Enlighten is a support gem that has no modifiers, and its sole function is having a cost and reservation multiplier less than 100%.
Enlighten Support | Skill function and interactions | Enlighten is primarily used to decrease the amount of reservation of linked skills. This effect is multiplicative with the effects of x% Increased Reservation Efficiency modifiers and the Socketed Skill Gems get a 90% Cost & Reservation Multiplier corrupted implicit modifier.
Enlighten Support | Skill function and interactions | Though Enlighten can also be used to decrease the mana cost of linked active skills, Inspiration Support is generally better (and more cost effective) for that purpose. However, it is a valid option for some skills that cost Life , Energy Shield , or Rage instead.

Ensnared Enemies take (15-20)% increased Projectile Damage from Attack Hits Each Snare applies 40% less Movement Speed to Normal or Magic Enemies Each Snare applies 30% less Movement Speed to Rare Enemies Each Snare applies 25% less Movement Speed to Unique Enemies Maximum 3 Snares per Enemy Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: (1-20)% increased Debuff Effect. Ensnaring Arrow | Description | Ensnaring Arrow is a bow attack skill which fires an arrow that tethers to the ground behind the enemy it hits, debuffing it with Snared. The ensnared enemy has less movement speed per snare (up to 3), takes increased hit damage from projectile attacks. Snares are removed when the enemy moves out of the arrow's area of effect.
Ensnaring Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | Split/Pierce/Fork/Chain/Return: Ensnaring Arrow cannot ensnare an enemy if the arrow continues from the enemy due to any projectile behaviours. Arrows that pierce or fork will never ensnare a target. Returning arrows can ensnare an enemy if the arrow collides and stops at that enemy. Arrows that have remaining chains can ensnare an enemy if there are no other eligible enemies to chain to.

Essence Drain | Description | Essence Drain is a chaos spell that fires a projectile that hits for a small amount of chaos damage and applies a heavy chaos damage over time effect. The spell also causes the player to regenerate a portion of the damage over time as life. Essence Drain interacts favourably with Contagion , a skill that can spread the damage over time component of Essence Drain to nearby enemies when the afflicted dies.

Eternal Blessing Support | Description | Eternal Blessing Support is a support gem that makes Aura Skills lose their reservations and become a Blessing Skill. You will only be able to benefit from one Blessing at a time and while under the effect of a Supported Skill, all Non-Blessing Skills which reserve mana are disabled.
Eternal Blessing Support | Mechanics and Interactions | This support gem lets you use a single aura without reserving mana for it, but it disables reserving mana for any other skill.
Eternal Blessing Support | Mechanics and Interactions | Therefore, most auras , herald skills, mine skills, and aspect skills cannot be used alongside an Eternal Blessing aura, unless they are modified to reserve life (e.g. via Arrogance Support or Blood Magic ) or modified to have no reservation (e.g. Aul's Uprising , Farrul's Fur ).
Eternal Blessing Support | Mechanics and Interactions | Using any other Blessing skill will replace the aura linked to Eternal Blessing.
Eternal Blessing Support | Mechanics and Interactions | Supported skills count as Blessings but their initial tags remain the same.

Eviscerate | Description | Eviscerate is a retaliation melee attack skill that unleashes two arcing waves of force in front of the user that applies bleeding. This skill requires a shield and a sword or axe to use. This skill becomes usable after blocking a hit.
Eviscerate | Skill functions and interactions | Eviscerate fires two simultaneous rectangular damaging areas in a V shape in front of you. Because of the angle of the attack and the shape of the damaging areas, the attack may miss name-locked targets if they are far enough away from the user in the area between the damaging areas. However, close targets can be hit twice, once by each damaging area.

Explosive Arrow | Infobox | Attack , Projectile , AoE , Duration , Fire , Bow Level: (1-20) Cost: (7-10) Mana Projectile Speed: 3260 AoE Radius: 15. Requires Level 28. Fires an arrow which will stick into an enemy or wall, and then explode, dealing area damage around it, either after a duration or when the maximum number of arrows stuck to that target is reached. If an enemy has multiple Explosive Arrows stuck in them, the first one to explode will consume the others, adding their damage to its explosion. Base duration is 1.00 seconds Explosion deals 50% less Base Damage Explosion will have (47-522) to (71-783) added Fire Damage +0.2 metres to Explosion Radius per Explosive Arrow on Target, up to a maximum of +1.2 metres Explosion deals 6% more Damage with Hits per Explosive Arrow on Target Explosion deals 3% more Damage with Ailments per Explosive Arrow on Target Maximum 20 Explosive Arrows stuck in an Enemy Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: +(0.1-2) Maximum Explosive Arrows stuck in an Enemy. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 28
Explosive Arrow | Description | Explosive Arrow is a bow attack skill that fires an arrow which sticks to terrain or enemies and explodes after a duration , dealing damage in an area. Any additional arrows stuck to an enemy will add their damage to the explosion, while also granting more damage with hits , damaging ailments , and area of effect.
Explosive Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | Explosive Arrow has two separate damaging mechanics: arrow collisions and exploding arrows. [1]
Explosive Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | Collision: Attacking fires a regular arrow, colliding with enemies and dealing damage with no special mechanics. Instead of disappearing, arrows remain stuck to terrain or enemies for a duration. Sticking additional arrows in an enemy does not affect the duration of other arrows.
Explosive Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | Explosion : When the duration of an arrow expires, the maximum number of stuck arrows is reached, or a target dies, all arrows on a target will explode for area projectile damage. Each arrow calculates its own damage, after which the hit damage and ailment dps from each arrow are combined and applied as a single hit and debuff. [2]
Explosive Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | The Explosion deals 50% less Base Damage stat only modifies base damage , meaning flat damage contributed by local values on your bow. [3]
Explosive Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | Projectile Mechanics : Arrows cannot stick in enemies if they continue due to Split , Pierce , Fork , Chain , or Return.
Explosive Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | Duration : Modifiers to skill effect duration , such as from More Duration Support , will affect the duration an arrow sticks to terrain or enemies.
Explosive Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | Proxies/allies: Arrows from different sources such as Ballista Totem Support , Mirage Archer Support or party members may all contribute to a single combined explosion. Each arrow will calculate its damage independently. For the purpose of on-hit effects and non-damage stats like ailment duration, the combined hit is dealt by the entity that fired the arrow responsible for causing the explosion. This is the last arrow if the maximum was reached, or the first arrow if the explosion occurs due to duration or enemy death.
Explosive Arrow | Item acquisition | Explosive Arrow can drop anywhere.

Explosive Concoction | Description | Explosive Concoction is an unarmed attack skill gem that throws an explosive bottle, dealing area damage. Charges can be consumed from Ruby , Sapphire and Topaz utility flasks' to add additional effects. This skill gem requires an empty main hand, and no off-hand weapon. Quiver and shield are not classified as weapons, but this skill cannot use quiver, due to version 3.16.0 update. [1]
Explosive Concoction | Skill functions and interactions | The explosions from projectiles can overlap. Given sufficient investment into increased area of effect , many explosions can hit the same target. The projectiles from Explosive Concoction are ground-targeted, cannot collide with enemies, and cannot pierce , fork , split or chain ; only the explosion hit and do damage. Testing with 9 total projectiles showed that most projectiles will overlap when the radius is 2.0 metres (average 8.8 out of 9), and all projectiles will overlap when the radius is 2.1 metres. [2]
Explosive Concoction | Skill functions and interactions | AoE breakpoints : The base radius of the skill is 1.8 metres and follows standard area of effect calculations.

Explosive Trap | Description | Explosive Trap is a spell that throws a trap that creates multiple explosions when triggered. It deals physical damage that's converted to fire damage.
Explosive Trap | Skill functions and interactions | The base radius of the large explosion is 1.2m, and the base radius of the small explosions is 0.7m, with the default size having a radius of 1.0m. Both of these radii are increased by the +x metres to radius gained from gem levels and by modifiers to Area of Effect. The chance that a small explosion will hit a targeted enemy can be calculated as ( r s m a l l + r e n e m y − 0.1 m ) 2 / ( r l a r g e ) 2 \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}(r_{small}+r_{enemy}-0.1m)^{2}/(r_{large})^{2}. Increases to radius improve the overlap chance of small explosions on the targeted enemy by increasing the ratio of the small radius to the large radius, while increased area of effect may either increase or decrease the overlap chance, as different breakpoints in the calculation of the large and small radius are reached. [1]

Eye of Winter | Infobox | Spell , Projectile , Cold Level: (1-20) Cost: (14-26) Mana Cast Time: 0.80 sec Critical Strike Chance: 6.00% Effectiveness of Added Damage: 55%. Requires Level 28. Fires a single eye projectile which constantly releases shard projectiles in a spiral. The shards will damage enemies they impact, dealing cold damage. The eye cannot damage enemies. When the eye dissipates or collides with terrain, it releases a faster spiral of extra shards. Deals (16-245) to (25-367) Cold Damage Modifiers to number of Projectiles only apply to the final Spiral Fires 8 Shard Projectiles in a Spiral when Projectile ends Projectiles continuously gain Damage, up to 200% more Damage by the time they dissipate Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Fires +(0.1-2) Shard Projectiles in a Spiral when Projectile ends. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 28
Eye of Winter | Description | Eye of Winter is a cold spell that fires a projectile that deals no damage but that constantly releases shard projectiles in a spiral over its lifetime, and then on dissipation or collision with terrain explodes in an additional spiral of shard projectiles. The spell will fire 31 shards over its lifetime, and then explode for an additional 8 (+1 per 10 quality) shards.
Eye of Winter | Skill Functions and Interactions | Additional Projectiles : Sources of additional projectiles do not increase the number of primary eye projectiles fired through casting this spell. Additional projectiles for this spell, obtained through modifiers or support gems such as Lesser Multiple Projectiles Support or Greater Multiple Projectiles Support only apply to the final spiral of shards.
Eye of Winter | Skill Functions and Interactions | Projectile Behaviors : The standard behaviors of projectiles, such as Pierce , Fork , and Chain do not apply to the primary "eye" projectile, as the eye does not actually collide with enemies. These behaviors do apply to the secondary shard projectiles, however.
Eye of Winter | Skill Functions and Interactions | Returning Projectiles : When this skill is supported by Returning Projectiles Support , both the primary and secondary projectiles will return in the direction of the player's current position. Projectiles that dissipate before reaching their maximum range will not return however, thus this will usually only occur when the projectiles hit an obstacle, such as walls or elevated terrain, or if their maximum range is reduced (by Arcanist Brand ).
Eye of Winter | Skill Functions and Interactions | Projectile Speed : Modifiers to projectile speed affect both types of projectiles from this skill- the eye and the secondary shard projectiles. If projectile speed is increased, for example, the eye projectile travels further than it normally would, as do the shard projectiles, which would travel further before they dissipate. This can effectively increase the coverage area of this skill. Bear in mind, however, that the faster the eye projectile, the larger the gap formed between shard projectiles unleashed in the spiral; this may cause single targets close to the caster to take less damage than if the eye projectile travels slower.
Eye of Winter | Skill Functions and Interactions | Conversely, reducing projectile speed reduces the distance that projectiles travel before dissipating; sufficiently reduced duration may allow all projectiles from a single cast to hit the same target with their maximum damage bonus.
Eye of Winter | Skill Functions and Interactions | Spiral of Shard Projectiles : This skill fires an "eye" projectile which fires out secondary "shard" projectiles as it travels. These shards are released constantly as the eye travels, and if a target is directly in the center of the eye, the multiple shards can hit that target. This behavior also applies to the final spiral that is triggered upon the eye projectile reaching the end of its lifetime (through reaching its maximum distance or colliding with terrain). Though it may look like an explosion, it is indeed a very fast sequential spiral, meaning that with careful positioning by the player, a single target may be hit by all projectiles in this last spiral, effectively "shotgunning" this target.
Eye of Winter | Skill Functions and Interactions | Traps and Mines : This skill can be turned into traps or mines with the appropriate support gems.
Eye of Winter | Transfigured Variants | Eye of Winter has 2 variants:
Eye of Winter | Item acquisition | Eye of Winter can drop anywhere.

Eye of Winter of Finality | Description | Eye of Winter of Finality is a cold spell that fires a projectile that deals no damage but that explodes in a spiral of shard projectiles on dissipation or collision with terrain. The spell will explode for 16 shards.
Eye of Winter of Finality | Description | It is a transfigured version of Eye of Winter.
Eye of Winter of Finality | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version has more damage and damage effectiveness, higher mana cost, and gains more shard projectiles, but has less scaling for projectiles based on projectile lifetime. It also only fires projectiles upon collision with terrain or after the eye dissipates, rather than continuously. Quality grants more damage to projectiles over the skill's duration.
Eye of Winter of Finality | Item acquisition | Eye of Winter of Finality has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.
Eye of Winter of Finality | Recipes | Eye of Winter of Finality can be created from the following recipes:
Eye of Winter of Finality | Related helmet enchantments | The following helmet enchantments affect Eye of Winter.
Eye of Winter of Finality | Related helmet enchantments | Note that helmet enchantments are no longer obtainable since version 3.23.0 and can only be obtained in permanent leagues as already enchanted items from before this patch.
Eye of Winter of Finality | Alternate skill effects | Eye of Winter of Finality has the following alternate skill effects :

Eye of Winter of Transience | Description | Eye of Winter of Transience is a cold spell that fires a projectile that deals no damage but that constantly releases shard projectiles in a spiral over its lifetime. The spell will fire 12 shards over its lifetime.
Eye of Winter of Transience | Description | It is a transfigured version of Eye of Winter.
Eye of Winter of Transience | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version has more damage and damage effectiveness, lower mana cost, faster cast time, and more projectile speed, but has less scaling for projectiles based on projectile lifetime. It also dissipates immediately upon firing all of its continuous projectiles and does not do a final spiral.
Eye of Winter of Transience | Item acquisition | Eye of Winter of Transience has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

Faster Casting Support | Description | Faster Casting is a support gem that increases the cast speed of linked spells.
Faster Casting Support | Description | When linked to a minion skill, Faster Casting increases both the cast speed of the summoning spell, and the speed with which summoned minions cast their own spells.
Faster Casting Support | Description | Faster Casting also affects the speed with which totems, traps or mines cast spells, but not the speed of placing totems or laying traps or mines. Trap and mine laying speed is instead affected by Trap and Mine Laying Speed , and totem placement speed by Totem Placement Speed.

Fire Trap of Blasting | Description | Fire Trap of Blasting is a trap skill that explodes when triggered, dealing fire damage in an area.
Fire Trap of Blasting | Description | It is a transfigured version of Fire Trap.
Fire Trap of Blasting | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version does not create Burning Ground. Instead, it has less base damage, but much higher added fire damage against burning enemies. Due to the skill's 260% effectiveness of added damage , this results in considerably more damage if its condition is met. It also has a chance to trigger an additional time.
Fire Trap of Blasting | Skill functions and interactions | Despite not having a skill effect duration, this transfiguration still has a Duration gem tag , allowing it to benefit from modifiers like +# to Level of Socketed Duration Gems. (Unlike e.g. Seismic Trap of Swells.)
Fire Trap of Blasting | Skill functions and interactions | Immolate Support has excellent synergy with the added damage provided by the skill gem if its condition is met.

Firestorm | Infobox | Spell , AoE , Duration , Fire Level: (1-20) Cost: (13-25) Mana Cast Time: 0.75 sec Critical Strike Chance: 6.00% Effectiveness of Added Damage: (60-80)%. Requires Level 28. Flaming bolts rain down over the targeted area. They explode when landing, dealing damage to nearby enemies. Base duration is 1.40 seconds Deals (23-361) to (35-541) Fire Damage One impact every 0.15 seconds Base radius is 1.3 metres First Impact has 100% more Area of Effect First Impact deals 325% more Damage with Hits and Ailments Maximum 3 Firestorms at a time Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Base radius is (0.01-0.2) metres. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 28
Firestorm | Description | Firestorm is a spell that rains numerous fiery bolts in an area, each bolt dealing area of effect fire damage.
Firestorm | Skill functions and interactions | Area of Effect : Increased AoE will increase both the area in which the fireballs randomly fall and also the radius of the individual fireball damage. Full spell damage is done at any point within the AoE of a fireball. However, increasing the size of the spell area means that each monster within the spell area is within fewer individual fireball bursts [1] ; thus, less spell damage is dealt to any single enemy as area increases. For this reason increasing the AoE of Firestorm paradoxically decreases the average damage dealt to a single target.
Firestorm | Skill functions and interactions | Duration : Increasing duration with either supports or passives will increase the total number of fireballs that fall before the spell ends.
Firestorm | Skill functions and interactions | Damage Effectiveness : Damage from secondary sources like 'added damage' supports or heralds applies at only 80% of their listed value.
Firestorm | Skill functions and interactions | Damage per Second : Due to the complexity of estimating actual DPS of the skill including all possible factors, ingame tooltip shows only average damage per hit of one fireball, with actual DPS of the skill being considerably higher. The closest currently available estimate can be obtained by using the damage calculator.
Firestorm | Item acquisition | Firestorm can drop anywhere.
Firestorm | Recipes | Firestorm can be created from the following recipes:
Firestorm | Quest reward | This item is given as a quest reward for the following quests:
Firestorm | Vendor reward | This item can be bought at the listed NPC vendors , after completing the following quests:
Firestorm | Related helmet enchantments | The following helmet enchantments affect Firestorm.
Firestorm | Related helmet enchantments | Note that helmet enchantments are no longer obtainable since version 3.23.0 and can only be obtained in permanent leagues as already enchanted items from before this patch.
Firestorm | Alternate skill effects | Firestorm has the following alternate skill effects :

Flame Dash of Return | Description | Flame Dash of Return is a movement spell that causes the player to teleport to a location before teleporting back, damaging enemies each time and leaving a trail of Burning Ground.
Flame Dash of Return | Description | It is a transfigured version of Flame Dash.
Flame Dash of Return | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version repeats, teleporting the user back to their original location. It loses the travel and blink tags and has no cooldown or charges. The skill deals significantly more hit damage but deals less damage over time. It cannot be used by a proxy or triggered , or supported by Unleash Support. Quality grants chance to Scorch.
Flame Dash of Return | Skill functions and interactions | Repeat : This skill always repeats, creating some synergy with other repeat-dependent stats like Awakened Spell Echo Support , Pledge of Hands and Plume of Pursuit.
Flame Dash of Return | Skill functions and interactions | Additional repeats : By default, this skill repeats a single time. The player will dash to the targeted location, then dash back to their original location. When linked with Spell Echo Support , the skill will repeat a third time, leaving the player at the originally targeted location.
Flame Dash of Return | Item acquisition | Flame Dash of Return has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

Flame Link | Infobox | Spell , Duration , Link , Fire Level: (1-20) Cost: (15-32) Mana per second Cast Time: 0.50 sec. Requires Level 34. Targets an allied player to apply a buff which links you to them for a duration. While linked, they deal additional fire damage based on your life. If the target dies while linked, you will also die. This skill cannot be triggered, or used by Totems, Traps, or Mines. Base duration is (8.00-9.90) seconds Linked target gains (23-169) to (35-254) Added Fire Damage Linked target gains Added Fire Damage equal to 5% of your Maximum Life Link breaks if target leaves range or line of sight for 4 seconds Maximum 1 Link from any source per target You die if Linked target dies Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: +(0.075-1.5) seconds to Base duration. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 34
Flame Link | Description | Flame Link is a Link skill that targets an allied player to apply a buff which links you to them for a duration. While linked, they deal additional fire damage based on your life. If the target dies while linked, you will also die. This skill cannot be used by Totems, Traps, or Mines.
