Flame Surge | Description | Flame Surge is a spell that deals fire damage in a narrow area of effect in front of the caster. It deals more damage against burning enemies. The skill itself cannot inflict ignite , but hitting an ignited enemy with Flame Surge leaves behind burning ground that deals damage proportional to the ignite damage.
Flame Surge | Skill functions and interactions | Area of Effect : Flame Surge fires a wave of flame in a very narrow cone, increasing the area of effect will increase the effective range of the skill and the area of the burning ground.
Flame Surge | Skill functions and interactions | Damage Bonus : All sources of burning enable the damage bonus. These include ignite , burning ground , Herald of Ash , Searing Bond , Infernal Legion Support , Righteous Fire , and Scorching Ray.
Flame Surge | Item acquisition | Flame Surge can drop anywhere.

Flame Surge of Combusting | Description | Flame Surge of Combusting is a spell that deals fire damage in a narrow area of effect in front of the caster. It has a chance to Ignite , which also leaves Burning Ground that deals damage proportional to the ignite damage.
Flame Surge of Combusting | Description | It is a transfigured version of Flame Surge.
Flame Surge of Combusting | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version can ignite, and creates a larger burning ground on ignite rather than when hitting an ignited enemy, with a higher percentage of the ignite's DPS. It also costs more mana and has a slower cast time, but has more damage and damage effectiveness. It no longer has a multiplier for hits against burning enemies. Gem quality increases the burning ground damage scaling instead.
Flame Surge of Combusting | Skill functions and interactions | Burning Ground scaling: modifiers to burning damage, fire damage, etc. all apply to the initial ignite inflicted by this skill. To prevent double-dipping, these do not also affect the burning ground created by this skill.
Flame Surge of Combusting | Skill functions and interactions | However, due to the specific base damage source of the ground effect—raw DPS of the ignite—it can be increased at a greater degree than the total damage of the ignite. This can be accomplished through the use of "faster" modifiers to ignites, most notably Sadism Support which, while reducing total ignite damage, greatly increases actual second-to-second DPS of the ignite and burning ground alike.
Flame Surge of Combusting | Skill functions and interactions | As an independent skill effect, the burning ground is not tied to the duration of the ignite. Absent any skill effect duration modifiers, the burning ground will always last 4 seconds.
Flame Surge of Combusting | Item acquisition | Flame Surge of Combusting has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

Flame Wall | Infobox | Spell , AoE , Duration , Fire Level: (1-20) Cost: (4-16) Mana Cast Time: 0.50 sec. Requires Level 4. Create a wall of fire for a duration, which deals burning damage to everything in its area. Each enemy that enters the wall also receives a secondary burning debuff which persists for a short duration after leaving the wall. Any projectiles fired through the wall by you and allies deal added fire damage and apply the wall's secondary debuff on hit. Deals (2.1-679.9) Base Fire Damage per second Secondary Debuff Deals (7.2-2266.4) Base Fire Damage per second Base duration is (3.00-4.90) seconds Base secondary duration is (1.00-2.00) seconds Modifiers to Spell Damage apply to this Skill's Damage Over Time effect Wall will be (4-5.9) meters long Projectiles which pass through the wall deal (3-78) to (5-117) Added Fire Damage Maximum 3 Flame Walls at a time Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: +(0.05-1) Maximum Flame Walls at a time. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 5
Flame Wall | Description | Flame Wall is a spell that creates a wall of flames which deals fire damage over time to enemies inside it, and for a short time after leaving it. Any projectiles that pass through the wall will gain added fire damage and apply Flame Wall's damage over time effect.
Flame Wall | Debuffs | Flame Wall has a primary debuff ( You are taking Fire Damage while inside a Flame Wall ) and a secondary debuff ( You are taking extra Fire Damage from a Flame Wall ). Both debuffs are applied to enemies inside the Flame Wall, while the secondary debuff lingers on enemies even after they leave it.
Flame Wall | Debuffs | Casting a Flame Wall directly on an enemy will cause them to be inside its area, applying the primary debuff, and it will also count as them 'entering' the wall, applying the secondary debuff. Both debuffs can deal damage at the same time. Multiple copies of the same debuff cannot; only the highest dps primary and secondary debuffs will be active. The duration of the secondary debuff does not begin expiring until the enemy leaves Flame Wall's area. [1]
Flame Wall | Projectiles | Projectiles that travel through Flame Wall will gain added fire damage and apply Flame Wall's secondary debuff to enemies they hit. Expiration or replacement of a Flame Wall does not cancel its effects on projectiles that already passed through it. Projectiles that cannot directly collide with other objects (e.g. Molten Strike , Winter Orb ) can still pass through a Flame Wall and gain its bonuses, [2] but rain-type projectiles such as Rain of Arrows can not.
Flame Wall | Item acquisition | Flame Wall can drop anywhere.
Flame Wall | Recipes | Flame Wall can be created from the following recipes:

Flame Wall | Vendor reward | This item can be bought at the listed NPC vendors , after completing the following quests:

Flamewood Support | Description | Flamewood Support is a support gem that causes totems to trigger Avenging Flame when hit by an enemy, which fires a mortar into the air, dealing fire damage in an area around the target that hit it.
Flamewood Support | Skill functions and interactions | Avenging Flame is a triggered fire projectile spell that fires a projectile into the air that falls around the enemy that hit the totem, dealing damage in an area. The skill is somewhat similar to Blast Rain. Avenging Flame has a base cooldown of 1 second which recovers independently on each totem, and cannot be used directly. While Avenging Flame is the player's skill (granting it the player's offensive stats), it is triggered from totems; on-hit effects will be granted to the totem it triggered from, and Ancestral Bond will not prevent its damage.
Flamewood Support | Skill functions and interactions | Additional projectiles create additional damaging areas, allowing for area overlap to hit the same target. The recommended number of projectiles against a single target is 5 projectiles, similar to Forbidden Rite 's primary projectile mechanic. [ confirmation needed ]
Flamewood Support | Skill functions and interactions | Avenging Flame cannot be triggered through reflect or self-hitting with Eye of Innocence. Because of this, Taunt is important to ensure enemies target the totem. Possible synergies include:
Flamewood Support | Skill functions and interactions | Avenging Flame's base damage is based on the totem's life. Most totems have a base life modifier of 100-120%. However, Decoy Totem , Devouring Totem , Rejuvenation Totem , and Vaal Rejuvenation Totem all gain #% increased totem life with levels in additional to the base life gained from levels. Decoy Totem , Devouring Totem , and Rejuvenation Totem gain totem life from quality.
Flamewood Support | Skill functions and interactions | Black Zenith only adds a cooldown to placing totems if the totem itself uses a projectile spell, but will increase the cooldown of the Avenging Flame skill triggered by the totem (that is, each totem will only fire projectiles once every 5 seconds, but new totems can be summoned to replace the ones already on cooldown).

Flesh and Stone | Infobox | Spell , Aura , AoE , Stance , Physical Level: (1-20) Reservation: 25% Mana Cooldown Time: 2.00 sec Can Store 1 Use(s) Cast Time: Instant. Requires Level 16. Buffs you, and affects nearby enemies with an aura, differently depending on your stance. Both stances are more impactful against enemies closer to you. Base Aura radius is 2.8 metres (0-50)% increased Cooldown Recovery Rate While in Blood Stance, Aura makes Enemies take up to (10-20)% more Physical Damage from Hits While in Sand Stance, Buff makes you take up to (10-19)% less Damage from Enemies in Aura Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: (0.5-10)% increased Cooldown Recovery Rate. Flesh and Stone | Description | Flesh and Stone is an aura skill that activates Blood and Sand Stance. In Blood Stance (Flesh), the skill causes nearby enemies to take more physical damage from hits. In Sand Stance (Stone), the user is buffed to take less hit damage from entities inside the aura. Both effects are stronger the closer enemies are to the user.
Flesh and Stone | Description | Activating the skill while it's toggled on will alternate between stances. Certain skills also behave differently based on the stance you are currently in.
Flesh and Stone | Skill functions and interactions | Nearby : Enemies within 1.5 metres of the user receive the full effect, which scales linearly down to 0 at the edge of the area. Increasing the aura's radius only affects the edge of the area and does not affect the 1.5 metre range for maximum effect. Increasing the aura radius does affect the interpolation between the two circles, however. [1]
Flesh and Stone | Skill functions and interactions | Stance buffs : In Blood stance, the user is buffed by Flesh , while in Sand stance, the name of the buff is Stone. Stance setting is shared with Blood and Sand , and using either skill will swap the stance. The skill's cooldown recovery rate (from gem quality) only affects the cooldown for toggling between Blood and Sand stance.
Flesh and Stone | Skill functions and interactions | Flesh : While in Blood Stance, Aura makes Enemies take up to #% more Physical Damage from Hits
Flesh and Stone | Skill functions and interactions | Stone : While in Sand Stance, Buff makes you take up to #% less Damage from Enemies in Aura
Flesh and Stone | Skill functions and interactions | Damage over time : This skill does not interact with damaging ailments or other types of damage over time in either stance. [5] (Though Stone only states this limitation in its buff description.)

Flicker Strike | Description | Flicker Strike is a melee attack skill that teleports the character to a nearby enemy and strikes it with their weapon and grants increased movement speed for a short time. If no specific monster is chosen, one is picked at random. The skill has a cooldown that can be bypassed by expending a Frenzy Charge and has a chance to generate a Frenzy Charge when used.
Flicker Strike | Skill Functions and Interactions | Because teleportation is instant movement, the attacker is considered to be stationary at every point in time, but is also considered to move each time they teleport. This means the attacker benefits from effects such as the Inexorable notable passive skill while attacking, but never from while moving modifiers. Teleporting also continually resets the timer for per second you've been stationary effects. [1]
Flicker Strike | Skill Functions and Interactions | Minimum Frenzy Charges cannot be consumed to bypass the cooldown.
Flicker Strike | Skill Functions and Interactions | Dual Wielding while using Flicker Strike will cause the player to alternate weapons each strike.
Flicker Strike | Skill Functions and Interactions | Flicker Strike can target an enemy within a distance of 4 meters by default. This is unaffected by modifiers to area of effect. Modifiers to Melee Strike Range have no special interaction with teleports: the attacker will neither "land" farther from the target when flickering, nor will they be able to initiate an attack from farther away.
Flicker Strike | Item acquisition | Flicker Strike can drop anywhere.
Flicker Strike | Recipes | Flicker Strike can be created from the following recipes:
Flicker Strike | Vendor reward | This item can be bought at the listed NPC vendors , after completing the following quests:

Flicker Strike | Related helmet enchantments | Note that helmet enchantments are no longer obtainable since version 3.23.0 and can only be obtained in permanent leagues as already enchanted items from before this patch.

Flicker Strike of Power | Description | Flicker Strike of Power is a melee attack skill that teleports the character to a nearby enemy and strikes it with their weapon and grants increased movement speed for a short time. If no specific monster is chosen, one is picked at random. The skill has a cooldown that can be bypassed by expending a Power Charge and has a chance to generate a Power Charge on critical strike.
Flicker Strike of Power | Description | It is a transfigured version of Flicker Strike.
Flicker Strike of Power | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version gains and spends Power Charges , and gains increased critical strike chance per Power Charge.

Forbidden Rite | Infobox | Spell , AoE , Chaos , Projectile Level: (1-20) Cost: (8-21) Mana Cast Time: 0.75 sec Critical Strike Chance: 6.00% Effectiveness of Added Damage: 110%. Requires Level 16. Deals (15-498) to (22-747) Chaos Damage Deals 5% of your maximum Energy Shield as base Chaos Damage Deals 12% of your maximum Life as base Chaos Damage Take 40% of maximum Life and 25% of maximum Energy Shield as Chaos Damage Fires extra Projectiles at up to (6-7) surrounding Enemies Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Fires extra Projectiles at up to +(0.1-2) surrounding Enemies. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 16
Forbidden Rite | Description | Forbidden Rite is a chaos spell that fires a projectile near your cursor, as well as additional projectiles for each enemy within range. Casting this spell deals a Chaos Damage hit to you based on a percentage of your maximum life and energy shield. This includes when the spell is triggered.
Forbidden Rite | Skill Functions and Interactions | Taking Damage : Whenever the spell is used, including via Cast when Damage Taken Support or Cast On Critical Strike Support , this spell will cause you to take a hit of Chaos Damage equal to 40% of Maximum Life and 25% of Maximum Energy Shield added together. This self-damage is considered secondary damage and will trigger on-hit effects and Cast when Damage Taken Support , unlike Dark Pact. This does not count as a cost. You will take a hit for each repeat of the skill when used with Spell Echo Support or Unleash Support. This damage cannot be blocked, dodged, evaded or reduced by spell suppression.
Forbidden Rite | Skill Functions and Interactions | Life Scaling : Forbidden Rite deals additional damage based on the Maximum Life and Energy Shield of the user of the skill. Temporary buffs to Life or Energy Shield such as Massive Shrines or buffs from Headhunter will also boost the damage. When used with Spell Totem Support , the damage will scale off Totem Life instead.
Forbidden Rite | Skill Functions and Interactions | Projectiles : By default, Forbidden Rite fires a projectile near your cursor, as well as additional projectiles for each enemy within range. This means that on single target, two projectiles will hit the enemy. Sources of additional projectiles will only add projectiles to the initial cursor-targeted projectile, and not the extra projectiles fired towards nearby enemies. The projectiles appear to have an expanding ring-based hit distribution, and it is recommended not to get more than +4 additional projectiles from various sources due to the tendency for projectiles to miss on smaller single targets. The projectiles appear to have a homing behaviour and will bend towards targeted enemies. The projectile is ground targeted and does not benefit from Chain, Fork, Split, Pierce or any projectile behaviour modifiers. However, Projectile Speed will greatly reduce the time between casting the skill and the impact of the projectile, enhancing the skill performance against moving enemies.
Forbidden Rite | Skill Functions and Interactions | Totems : The totem playstyle has several mechanics that bypass or mitigate the downsides while being able to efficiently scale the spell. For example, the unique Soul Mantle acts as an effective 7-link, and scales Totem Life very well in addition to Torchoak Step , with much higher achievable Life values than self-cast. Rain of Splinters adds 2 projectiles for lower opportunity cost than Volley Support. Because the self-damage applies to the totem rather than you, you can also scale Totem Chaos Resistance with the Ancestral Preservation node on Totem Damage Medium Cluster Jewel s.
Forbidden Rite | Skill Functions and Interactions | Forbidden Rite can be socketed into a weapon with the Incursion modifier Trigger a Socketed Spell on Using a Skill, with a 4 second Cooldown to reliably automate a self-damage hit for various effects.
Forbidden Rite | Item acquisition | Forbidden Rite can drop anywhere.
Forbidden Rite | Recipes | Forbidden Rite can be created from the following recipes:
Forbidden Rite | Vendor reward | This item can be bought at the listed NPC vendors , after completing the following quests:
Forbidden Rite | Related helmet enchantments | The following helmet enchantments affect Forbidden Rite.
Forbidden Rite | Related helmet enchantments | Note that helmet enchantments are no longer obtainable since version 3.23.0 and can only be obtained in permanent leagues as already enchanted items from before this patch.

Fresh Meat Support | Skill functions and interactions | File:Wakened Fury status icon.png Wakened Fury
Fresh Meat Support | Skill functions and interactions | Fresh Meat Support's buffs apply to minions based on 10% of their minion duration, up to a maximum of 10 seconds. For permanent minions without a duration, this buff will always last for 10 seconds after they are summoned.
Fresh Meat Support | Skill functions and interactions | Minions that can respawn automatically (e.g. Dark Marionette spectres or golems with Liege of the Primordial ) gain the buff again when the revive.

Frost Blades | Infobox | Attack , Projectile , Melee , Strike , Cold Level: (1-20) Cost: (6-10) Mana Attack Damage: (143-409)% of base Effectiveness of Added Damage: (143-409)% Projectile Speed: 2250. Frost Blades | Description | Frost Blades is a cold melee attack skill that strikes with increased range , then has the secondary effect of releasing projectiles behind the struck enemies.
Frost Blades | Skill functions and interactions | An attack with Frost Blades consists of two parts:
Frost Blades | Skill functions and interactions | When an enemy is hit by this skill, multiple projectiles fly out from behind the enemy and target nearby enemies. This skill increases the melee range by an appreciable degree. Unlike Lightning Strike , if the initial attack does not hit an enemy, then no projectiles are released.
Frost Blades | Skill functions and interactions | The projectiles have the same damage multiplier as the initial melee hit, with the same cold damage conversion. However the initial melee attack scales with melee damage modifiers (such as Melee Physical Damage ), whereas the projectiles scale with projectile damage modifiers. Both components scale equally with (unspecified) attack , physical , and cold damage modifiers.
Frost Blades | Skill functions and interactions | Multistrike Support uses the increased attack range of this skill when selecting targets to attack. Melee Splash Support does not release additional projectiles from secondary targets struck by the initial melee splash, nor does it splash the projectiles' damage. Faster Projectiles Support will increase the speed and effective range of the secondary projectiles. Vicious Projectiles Support scales all the damage of the projectiles, but not the initial melee attack: attack speed is NOT reduced since the attack animation itself is melee , rather than projectile.
Frost Blades | Skill functions and interactions | Note: the tooltip DPS shows only the melee attack component damage and DPS. It does not show damage values for the projectile component.
Frost Blades | Skill functions and interactions | Interactions :

Frost Blades of Katabasis | Description | Frost Blades of Katabasis is a cold melee attack skill that strikes with greatly increased range , then, if it hits, it has the secondary effect of leaving a ground effect that both chills ( chilled ground ) and deals cold damage over time.
Frost Blades of Katabasis | Description | It is a transfigured version of Frost Blades.
Frost Blades of Katabasis | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version has a greater range and no projectiles. Instead, if it hits, the attack leaves a ground effect that both chills ( chilled ground ) and deals cold damage over time.
Frost Blades of Katabasis | Damage over time | When Frost Blades of Katabasis hits with its melee strike, a chilling area is created, chilling enemies and dealing cold damage over time to them. The chilling area does not inflict its own hit. [2] The area of this chilling area cannot be modified. Additional strikes will each create a chilling area. Multiple chilling areas do not stack their effects.
Frost Blades of Katabasis | Damage over time | Scaling : The damage over time is not affected by modifiers to attack damage or damage with specific weapons. It is affected by Damage with Attack Skills modifiers, however. It is also affected by modifiers to area damage which does not affect the initial strike.
Frost Blades of Katabasis | Item acquisition | Frost Blades of Katabasis has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.
Frost Blades of Katabasis | Recipes | Frost Blades of Katabasis can be created from the following recipes:
Frost Blades of Katabasis | Threshold jewels | The following threshold jewels augment Frost Blades of Katabasis:
Frost Blades of Katabasis | Threshold jewels | Note that the jewels may be drop disabled and can only be found in permanent leagues as legacy items.
Frost Blades of Katabasis | Related helmet enchantments | The following helmet enchantments affect Frost Blades.
Frost Blades of Katabasis | Related helmet enchantments | Note that helmet enchantments are no longer obtainable since version 3.23.0 and can only be obtained in permanent leagues as already enchanted items from before this patch.
Frost Blades of Katabasis | Alternate skill effects | Frost Blades of Katabasis has the following alternate skill effects :

Requires Level 4. Creates a crystal that pulses with cold for a duration. Each pulse applies a debuff to nearby enemies for a secondary duration which reduces life regeneration rate, and also inflicts Cold Exposure. When the crystal's duration ends, it explodes, dealing heavy cold damage to enemies around it. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 5
Frost Bomb | Description | Frost Bomb is a cold spell that places an orb that releases pulses which reduce life regeneration , along with applying Cold Exposure. It will rapidly pulse before it explodes, dealing cold damage in an area.

Frost Bomb of Forthcoming | Description | Frost Bomb of Forthcoming is a cold spell that places a crystal that explodes after a duration, dealing cold damage in an area based on the delay.
Frost Bomb of Forthcoming | Description | It is a transfigured version of Frost Bomb.
Frost Bomb of Forthcoming | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version does not apply exposure or reduced life regeneration. Instead, it has a longer cooldown and duration and deals more damage with hits and ailments based on the duration. It has higher damage and damage effectiveness. It also lacks the Orb gem tag.

Frost Shield | Description | Frost Shield is a spell that creates a chilling area around the caster for a duration. The area drains your energy shield when initially cast, and uses the absorbed energy shield to redirect a percentage of the damage the user takes to itself while the user is within it. The user also gains increased spell critical strike chance while within the field.
Frost Shield | Skill functions and interactions | Chilling Area : Enemies that are inside the chilling area become chilled by the player. Modifiers to chill and cold/non-damaging ailments apply, including from support gems. It can apply chilled by you stats, enable if you have chilled recently stats, and cannot chill when the player or skill cannot chill.
Frost Shield | Skill functions and interactions | Energy Shield Recharge: Recharge is disabled while the effect is draining energy shield. Despite not being damage , the drain also interrupts ES recharge, which Wicked Ward may prevent. [1]
Frost Shield | Item acquisition | Frost Shield can drop anywhere.

Frostblink of Wintry Blast | Description | Frostblink of Wintry Blast is a spell that teleports the user to the target location and deals cold damage in an area at both ends of the teleport.
Frostblink of Wintry Blast | Description | It is a transfigured version of Frostblink.
Frostblink of Wintry Blast | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version has less damage, lower damage effectiveness , does not create Chilled Ground , cannot Chill , does not have a cooldown , and is no longer instant. It also lacks the Blink gem tag. It shares the minimum and maximum travel distance with the regular version, but loses the increases to maximum travel distance from gem levels and quality. Instead, it has a cast time, higher mana cost, a hit damage multiplier based on the enemy's action speed reduction from chill, and removes chill after damaging. It also adds its cast time to its cooldown, if granted a cooldown by a trigger.
Frostblink of Wintry Blast | Skill functions and interactions | Cooldown : The skill's tooltip cast time will be added onto any cooldowns triggering this skill, including any modifiers to cast speed. Attaching Faster Casting Support will reduce the overall cooldown, unlike typical triggers. Action Speed modifiers are disregarded for this interaction. Cooldown Recovery Rate modifiers will affect the sum of the original trigger's cooldown and Frostblink of Wintry Blast's additional cooldown.
Frostblink of Wintry Blast | Skill functions and interactions | Removes Chill after Damaging :
Frostblink of Wintry Blast | Skill functions and interactions | When chill is removed, enemies typically need to leave and re-enter the area of effect before chill can be reapplied.
Frostblink of Wintry Blast | Skill functions and interactions | When used with mines (eg using High-Impact Mine Support ), Detonate Mines does not function (either the skill gem or done manually). This is speculated to be due to needing an enemy target to trigger.
Frostblink of Wintry Blast | Item acquisition | Frostblink of Wintry Blast has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.
Frostblink of Wintry Blast | Recipes | Frostblink of Wintry Blast can be created from the following recipes:
Frostblink of Wintry Blast | Related helmet enchantments | The following helmet enchantments affect Frostblink.
Frostblink of Wintry Blast | Related helmet enchantments | Note that helmet enchantments are no longer obtainable since version 3.23.0 and can only be obtained in permanent leagues as already enchanted items from before this patch.
Frostblink of Wintry Blast | Alternate skill effects | Frostblink of Wintry Blast has the following alternate skill effects :

Fire a trio of electrical arrows that quickly dissipate, vanishing shortly after being loosed. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 5
Galvanic Arrow | Description | Galvanic Arrow is a ranged bow attack skill that fires arrows which deals lightning damage in an area of effect cone. The arrows have a short range, but it can be extended with increased projectile speed , which also increases the AoE.
Galvanic Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | Both the arrow and lightning cone are modified by projectile damage modifiers, including Point Blank. Area damage modifiers (like Concentrated Effect Support ) will affect the cone but not the arrow damage. The cone and arrow deal damage independent of each other, effectively doubling damage dealt to monsters directly in the line of fire.
Galvanic Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | Galvanic Arrow shoots a lightning cone each time you shoot your bow. Barrage Support makes your projectiles fire sequentially instead of simultaneously, meaning a lightning cone is triggered for each projectile shot during your attack.
Galvanic Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | When supported by Arrow Nova Support , the lightning cone will fire in a random direction from where the payload arrow lands.
Galvanic Arrow | Item acquisition | Galvanic Arrow can drop anywhere.
Galvanic Arrow | Recipes | Galvanic Arrow can be created from the following recipes:
Galvanic Arrow | Quest reward | This item is given as a quest reward for the following quests:
Galvanic Arrow | Vendor reward | This item can be bought at the listed NPC vendors , after completing the following quests:

Galvanic Arrow | Threshold jewels | Note that the jewels may be drop disabled and can only be found in permanent leagues as legacy items.

Fire a trio of electrical arrows that quickly dissipate, vanishing shortly after being loosed. Galvanic Arrow of Surging | Description | Galvanic Arrow is a ranged bow attack skill that fires arrows which deals lightning damage to the target and in a cone behind the target. The arrows have a short range, but it can be extended with increased projectile speed , which also increases the area of effect.
Galvanic Arrow of Surging | Description | It is a transfigured version of Galvanic Arrow.
Galvanic Arrow of Surging | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version has the lightning cone appear behind the target each arrow hits, similar to Ice Shot. The cones do not gain additional radius with level and modifying projectile speed will not adjust their area of effect.
Galvanic Arrow of Surging | Skill functions and interactions | Damage : Both the arrow and lightning cone are modified by projectile damage modifiers, including Point Blank. Area damage modifiers (like Concentrated Effect Support ) will affect the cone but not the arrow damage. The cone and arrow deal damage independent of each other.
Galvanic Arrow of Surging | Skill functions and interactions | Explosion: On hitting a target, Galvanic Arrow of Surging creates a cone-shaped explosion that damages enemies behind the primary target, and upon the arrow hitting a target that ends its flight. Targets that create a cone-shaped explosion will not be damaged by their own cone, but can be damaged by other cones created by additional projectiles. The cones will not trigger upon hitting an enemy the arrows pierce , fork , chain , or return from.
Galvanic Arrow of Surging | Item acquisition | Galvanic Arrow of Surging has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

Galvanic Field | Description | Galvanic Field is a spell that grants a buff which grants additional shock chance when cast. Upon shocking an enemy, the buff is removed to attach an electric field around the target, which hits a random enemy within its area of effect to deal lightning damage for a duration. The field has increased area of effect and damage based on the magnitude of the shock that was applied.

General's Cry | Description | General's Cry is a warcry skill that taunts nearby enemies, and summons a number of mirage warriors from nearby corpses based on the power of nearby enemies and corpses. Mirage warriors perform one linked attack skill, then disappear.
General's Cry | Description | The gem has two parts: the active General's Cry, and General's Cry Support , which supports melee attack skills and allows them to be used by the summoned mirages.
General's Cry | Skill functions and interactions | Power : Normal Monsters have 1 Power, Magic Monsters 2, Rare Monsters 10, Unique Monsters 20, and Players 5
General's Cry | Skill functions and interactions | Corpses: Only one mirage can be summoned per corpse, regardless of warcry power. Corpses may be created with skills such as Desecrate , Unearth , or Corpsewalker. Mirages may also be summoned from Raise Zombie minions in combination with Aukuna's Will.
General's Cry | Skill functions and interactions | Exertion: General's Cry does not Exert attacks by the user, but attacks supported by General's Cry count as Exerted by it, allowing them to benefit from bonuses to Exerted attacks and apply Corrupted Blood when linked to Corrupting Cry Support. General's Cry does not benefit from Measured Fury.
General's Cry | Mirage Warriors | Mirage Warriors are a type of allied proxy , with the limitation of disappearing after a single attack animation. They are unable to be damaged and are ignored by enemies. Like other proxies, they do not possess the player's global stats or equipment; they are separate entities with access to the skill supported by General's Cry, which contains the player's offensive stats. Meaning:

General's Cry | Mirage Warriors | Appearance:
General's Cry | Mirage Warriors | Mirage Warriors take the form of a Marauder (regardless of player class ) with a red character effect. They will also have Skin Transfer s of Karui-themed unique items based on the player's equipped weapons/shield:
General's Cry | Mirage Warriors | Additionally, they wear a variety of microtransactions such as the Bear Armour Pack , Wild Armour Pack , Bestiary Helmet , Wasteland Horns , Bear Cloak , and Wrangler Crossbow.

Generosity Support | Infobox | Support , Aura Icon: G Level: (1-20). Requires Level 31. Supports aura skills that affect you and allies, increasing the aura's effect on allies, but preventing it from affecting you at all. Cannot support curse auras, or other auras that only affect enemies. Cannot support skills used by totems. Cannot modify the skills of minions. Supported Auras do not affect you Non-Curse Auras from Supported Skills have (20-39)% increased Effect Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Supported Skills have (2-40)% increased Aura Area of Effect. This is a Support Gem. It does not grant a bonus to your character, but to skills in sockets connected to it. Place into an item socket connected to a socket containing the Skill Gem you wish to augment. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 31
Generosity Support | Description | Generosity Support is a support gem. It increases the effect of linked non- curse auras on allies , but prevents them from affecting the player.
Generosity Support | Skill functions and interactions | This gem can be used to increase the effect of auras on the player's allies. Generosity can only affect auras cast by the player, and cannot support curse auras [1] or mine skills. Auras cast through a Spell Totem cannot be supported by Generosity, nor can Rejuvenation Totem / Vaal Rejuvenation Totem.
Generosity Support | Skill functions and interactions | Despite the gem's description stating " increasing the aura's effect on allies ", Generosity increases all effects of any aura that affects allies. For example, when used with Dread Banner , it increases both the impale chance/effect granted to allies as well as the accuracy reduction granted to enemies.
Generosity Support | Skill functions and interactions | Non-totem proxies can be linked to Generosity.
Generosity Support | Skill functions and interactions | Supported Auras do not affect You : Proxies inherit most of the player's offensive stats but have independent defensive stats. This means that player auras supported by Generosity also follow this rule; defensive auras (e.g. Purity of Elements ) linked to Generosity will affect proxies, but offensive auras (e.g. Hatred and Haste ) linked to Generosity will have no effect. This also applies to secondary effects that can be affected by auras; for example, Grace supported by Generosity will not increase the damage of a totem using a Dreamfeather to attack, as the weapon stat scales off the player's evasion, not the totem's evasion.
Generosity Support | Item acquisition | Generosity Support can drop anywhere.

Glacial Cascade | Infobox | Spell , AoE , Cold , Physical Level: (1-20) Cost: (10-20) Mana Cast Time: 0.60 sec Critical Strike Chance: 5.00% Effectiveness of Added Damage: 65%. Requires Level 28. Icicles emerge from the ground in a series of small bursts, each damaging enemies caught in the area and knocking them back in the direction of the next burst. Deals (21-300) to (32-450) Physical Damage Knocks Enemies Back on Hit 100% of Physical Damage Converted to Cold Damage Cannot Knockback Unique Enemies more than once per second Final Burst Deals 200% more Damage Final Burst has 100% more Radius Final Burst cannot Knockback Causes 4 Bursts Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Final Burst Deals +(2.5-50)% more Damage. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 28
Glacial Cascade | Description | Glacial Cascade is a cold spell that launches a wave of icicles. Damage from this skill converts physical damage to cold damage.
Glacial Cascade | Skill functions and interactions | Area of Effect: Glacial Cascade fires as a set of multiple area of effect circles in a line, which the skill calls bursts. Increasing the Area of Effect increases the size of each burst, but the distance between the centre of each burst and the effective range don't change. Instead, larger bursts overlap with each other more. This can allow you to hit an enemy several times with a single cast much more reliably.
Glacial Cascade | Skill functions and interactions | Number of Hits: Glacial Cascade can hit an enemy a variable number of times, based on its location and the increased area of effect of the glacial cascade. An enemy can be hit twice if it is near the central line of the skill where the bursts overlap. This becomes increasingly likely if the skill has increased area of effect or the enemy has a large hitbox. An enemy that is very close to the caster or at full range will only be hit once, regardless of increases to area of effect, because they are only over the area of effect of the nearest or furthest burst. In unusual circumstances with high amounts (over 150%) of increased area of effect, an enemy can be hit by three bursts from the same spell.
Glacial Cascade | Skill functions and interactions | Note: Despite what the spell may look like, Glacial Cascade is not a projectile. Thus, effects that normally affect projectiles such as Lesser Multiple Projectiles Support do not have an effect on Glacial Cascade.
Glacial Cascade | Item acquisition | Glacial Cascade can drop anywhere.

Greater Multiple Projectiles Support | Description | Greater Multiple Projectiles (often referred to as GMP ) is a support gem that causes any linked projectile skills to fire four more projectiles than they otherwise would.
Greater Multiple Projectiles Support | Description | For instance, Spark , which normally launches three projectiles, would instead launch seven sparks when supported by this gem.
Greater Multiple Projectiles Support | Skill functions and interactions | The projectiles fan out in an arc, facing the direction that the skill is aimed. The spread of the arc is controlled by the positioning the mouse cursor. Positioning the cursor close to the character will result in a wide arc, while positioning it at the edges of the screen will result in a narrow arc. This gem has a wider minimum spread angle than Lesser Multiple Projectiles Support.
Greater Multiple Projectiles Support | Skill functions and interactions | This gem can be combined with Lesser Multiple Projectiles for a total of 6 additional Projectiles.
Greater Multiple Projectiles Support | Skill functions and interactions | Ailments : The "less projectile damage" on the gem doesn't decrease the damage of ailments such as poison, bleed and ignite, so gmp can be used safely to increase the number of targets hit. This can be especially useful with poison and skills that hit the same enemy multiple times, like Barrage and Tornado Shot, as they will stack multiple poisons without diminishing their strength.
Greater Multiple Projectiles Support | Item acquisition | Greater Multiple Projectiles Support can drop anywhere.

Greater Multiple Projectiles Support | Vendor reward | This item can be bought at the listed NPC vendors , after completing the following quests:

Greater Volley Support | Infobox | Support , Projectile Icon: ≣ Level: (1-20) Cost & Reservation Multiplier: 150%. Requires Level 38. Supports skills that fire projectiles from the user. Does not affect projectiles fired from other locations as secondary effects. Does not support skills that fire projectiles in a spiral. Supported Skills fire 4 additional Projectiles Supported Skills also fire Projectiles from up to 4 points on each side of you Supported Skills deal (30-21)% less Projectile Damage Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Supported Skills deal (1-20)% increased Projectile Damage. This is a Support Gem. It does not grant a bonus to your character, but to skills in sockets connected to it. Place into an item socket connected to a socket containing the Skill Gem you wish to augment. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 38
Greater Volley Support | Description | Greater Volley Support is a support gem that adds additional projectiles to a linked projectile gem, similar to Greater Multiple Projectiles Support (GMP).
Greater Volley Support | Skill functions and interactions | While the projectiles from Greater Multiple Projectiles fan out from the main projectile, Greater Volley's projectiles shoot out parallel to the main projectile, and originate from points on either side of the player.

Ground Slam of Earthshaking | Description | Ground Slam of Earthshaking is a melee slam attack creating a wide wave that travels forwards and damages enemies with an increased chance to stun using a two-handed weapons.
Ground Slam of Earthshaking | Description | It is a transfigured version of Ground Slam.
Ground Slam of Earthshaking | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version has a higher damage effectiveness and wider angle but does not gain any bonus damage to closer targets without gem quality. It also has a higher cost, slower attack time, and can only be used with two-handed maces , axes , and staves / warstaves.
Ground Slam of Earthshaking | Item acquisition | Ground Slam of Earthshaking has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

Guardian's Blessing Support | Infobox | Support , Aura , Blessing , Minion Icon: g Level: (1-20) Cost & Reservation Multiplier: 200%. Requires Level 31. Must support both a skill that creates minions which can be damaged, and an aura skill that creates permanent auras around you. Cannot support triggered skills, banner skills or stance skills. Supported Skills have (-30-27)% increased Aura effect Supported Aura Skills have no Reservation Supported Skills also count as Blessing Skills You can only have one Aura on you from your Blessing Skills Supported Skills cannot apply Auras on you unless you have Minions from a Supported Skill Minions from Supported Skills take (8-14.3)% of their total Maximum Life and Energy Shield as Physical Damage per second while you have an Aura from a Supported Skill on you Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Supported Skills have (0.25-5)% increased Aura effect. This is a Support Gem. It does not grant a bonus to your character, but to skills in sockets connected to it. Place into an item socket connected to a socket containing the Skill Gem you wish to augment. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 31
Guardian's Blessing Support | Description | Guardian's Blessing Support is a support gem that makes an aura skill lose its reservation and become a Blessing Skill. You will only be able to benefit from one Blessing at a time. The aura can only be used while you have a supported minion , which takes a percentage of their maximum life as physical damage over time.
Guardian's Blessing Support | Skill functions and interactions | Blessing : Blessing skills are limited to 1 aura at a time and cannot be used with other blessing skills. The blessing must be activated by casting it while the linked minion is alive. The aura is cast on the character, like other auras. If the minion dies or expires, Guardian's Blessing is turned off and must be re-activated.
Guardian's Blessing Support | Skill functions and interactions | It also works on Hex with Blasphemy Support.
Guardian's Blessing Support | Skill functions and interactions | Meat Shield Support provides less damage taken, which is beneficial for mitigating the damage over time. Blessed Rebirth can be used to guarantee a minimum uptime of the Guardian's Blessing while the minion cannot be damaged.
Guardian's Blessing Support | Skill functions and interactions | Minions : Cannot support minions which are invulnerable (e.g. Raise Spiders ). A tanky minion is required, as the blessing aura is turned off when the minion dies or expires.
Guardian's Blessing Support | Skill functions and interactions | Synergistic minions :
Guardian's Blessing Support | Skill functions and interactions | Non-synergistic minions :

Haste | Legacy mods | The Haste has #% increased Mana Reservation Efficiency has the same function as the current mod. But since this legacy mod is converted from the legacy Reduced Reservation mod, so that the coding behind the mod is different, which store the value as negative integer, instead of the positive integer for the current mod. The official trade site has been fixed so that both legacy and current mod use the same item filter.

Heavy Strike of Trarthus | Infobox | Attack , Melee , Strike Level: (1-20) Cost: (7-12) Mana Attack Speed: 85% of base Attack Damage: (224-552)% of base Effectiveness of Added Damage: (224-552)%. Heavy Strike of Trarthus | Description | Heavy Strike of Trarthus is a melee strike attack skill that hits an enemy with a staff. Each hit has a chance to deal double damage based on the user's intelligence and modifiers to spell damage affects the damage dealt by the skill.
Heavy Strike of Trarthus | Description | It is a Trarthan version of Heavy Strike.
Heavy Strike of Trarthus | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this version can only used with a staff. It does not have reduced enemy stun threshold, but increases and decreases to spell damage will increase or decrease the damage dealt with the skill. The skill's chance to deal double damage is based on the user's intelligence rather than a static amount based on gem level. Gem quality grants increased magnitude of increased/decreased attack damage from spell damage instead.
Heavy Strike of Trarthus | Skill functions and interactions | Only increases and reductions to Spell Damage are applied to attacks. More and less modifiers do not apply to attacks.
Heavy Strike of Trarthus | Skill functions and interactions | The Increases and Reductions to Spell Damage also apply to Attacks at #% of their value modifier does not stack with similar modifiers such as Crown of Eyes.
Heavy Strike of Trarthus | Skill functions and interactions | Double Damage chance is capped at 100%.
Heavy Strike of Trarthus | Item acquisition | Heavy Strike of Trarthus has restrictions on where or how it can drop.
Heavy Strike of Trarthus | Item acquisition | Can be obtained from reward chests that contain gems in Heist.

Herald of Agony | Infobox | Spell , Herald , Minion , Chaos , Physical Level: (1-20) Reservation: 25% Mana Cooldown Time: 1.00 sec Can Store 1 Use(s) Cast Time: Instant. Requires Level 16. Grants a buff giving more poison damage and a chance to inflict poison. When you poison an enemy while you have this buff, you gain Virulence, and summon an Agony Crawler minion that uses projectile and area attacks. You will lose Virulence over time, at a rate which increases the more Virulence you have. The minion will die when you have no Virulence. Maximum 1 Summoned Agony Crawler Buff grants 20% chance to Poison on Hit Grants Virulence when you Poison an Enemy Buff grants 10% more Damage with Poison 40 Maximum Virulence Minions cannot Taunt Enemies Agony Crawler has (2-4)% increased Attack Speed per Virulence you have Agony Crawler deals (5-9)% increased Physical Damage per Virulence you have Agony Crawler has (1-45) to (3-84) Added Physical Damage per Virulence you have Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: +(0.25-5) to Maximum Virulence. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 16
Herald of Agony | Description | Herald of Agony is a herald skill gem. When activated it reserves mana and grants a buff that gives a chance to inflict poison on enemies. Whenever the user poisons an enemy, it grants Virulence and summons an Agony Crawler minion , which becomes stronger with more stacks of Virulence.
Herald of Agony | Skill functions and interactions | Virulence is gained by poisoning enemies, 1 for each poison. Poisoning while at 0 Virulence grants 2 Virulence instead. Reflecting poison to yourself with The Golden Rule grants 1 additional Virulence per poison.
Herald of Agony | Skill functions and interactions | The regular maximum is 40 stacks, which can be raised with the Cluster Jewel notable passive Pure Agony , additional quality on the gem gained via methods such as Enhance Support or Ashes of the Stars , and from the Herald's Scourge node.
Herald of Agony | Skill functions and interactions | Virulence decays at an accelerating rate based on the number of stacks you have. Stacks have a base duration rate of 533ms, minus 12.5ms per stack of Virulence capping out at a minimum duration of 33ms (1 server tick) at 40+ stacks. In other words, poison must be inflicted at a rate of 30 stacks per second to sustain maximum Virulence higher than 40. [1]
Herald of Agony | Minion | Agony Crawler Minion has an implicit 40% Physical to Chaos damage conversion.
Herald of Agony | Minion | Minion damage can be increased through many methods, such as:
Herald of Agony | Minion | For faster clear speed consider supports such as Pierce , Greater Multiple Projectiles , Faster Attacks , and Minion Speed.
Herald of Agony | Minion | Consider using Convocation to quickly teleport the Crawler to your location. This is useful when dealing with Proximity Shield mobs.
Herald of Agony | Item acquisition | Herald of Agony can drop anywhere.

Herald of Agony | Vendor reward | This item can be bought at the listed NPC vendors , after completing the following quests:

Herald of Ice | Infobox | Spell , AoE , Cold , Herald Level: (1-20) Reservation: 25% Mana Cooldown Time: 1.00 sec Can Store 1 Use(s) Cast Time: Instant Effectiveness of Added Damage: 80%. Requires Level 16. Grants a buff adding cold damage to spells and attacks. If you shatter an enemy, this skill will cause them to explode and deal AoE cold damage to enemies near them. Deals (18-308) to (26-462) Cold Damage Damage cannot be Reflected Base radius is 1.2 metres Buff grants (4-38) to (5-56) Added Attack Cold Damage Buff grants (4-38) to (5-56) Added Spell Cold Damage Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: +(0.01-0.2) metres to radius. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 16
Herald of Ice | Description | Herald of Ice is a Herald skill. It reserves mana to grant added cold damage to attacks and spells and causes frozen enemies you shatter to explode, dealing cold damage in an area.
Herald of Ice | Skill functions and interactions | Explode on Shatter : While Herald of Ice is active, if you shatter an enemy (usually by killing a frozen enemy but also Call of the Void works for killing chilled enemies), an explosion will be created that deals cold secondary damage to other enemies in an area. This explosion can be modified by support gems linked to Herald of Ice, such as Hextouch Support or Increased Area of Effect Support.
Herald of Ice | Skill functions and interactions | Spell Damage : Because of the line Damage is not affected by modifiers to spell damage , support gems which grant more spell damage, increases and reductions to spell damage on the passive tree, or even critical modifiers such as increased critical strike chance for spells will not apply.
Herald of Ice | Skill functions and interactions | Global modifiers like flat damage from Nightgrip or Increased damage modifiers that do not specify attacks or spells still apply to Herald of Ice.
Herald of Ice | Skill functions and interactions | Area of Effect : Area of Effect modifiers from passives, items, and gems affect the radius of the on-kill explosion.

Hexblast | Infobox | Spell , AoE , Chaos , Hex Level: (1-20) Cost: (16-30) Mana Cooldown Time: 2.00 sec Can Store 3 Use(s) Cast Time: 1.00 sec Critical Strike Chance: 4.00% Effectiveness of Added Damage: (300-340)%. Requires Level 28. Deals chaos damage to a single enemy, dealing more damage if they are Hexed, then removing the Hex. If the enemy was Hexed, also deals area damage to other enemies around the target, boosting damage and removing Hexes from those enemies in the same way. Deals (111-1514) to (166-2271) Chaos Damage Base radius is 3.2 metres All Damage can Ignite, Freeze, and Shock Chaos Damage with Hits is Resisted by lowest Resistance instead 90% more Damage with Ailments if Enemy is Hexed 200% more Damage with Hits if Enemy is Hexed 100% chance to remove a Hex after Damaging Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: -(0.5-10)% chance to remove a Hex after Damaging. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 28
Hexblast | Description | Hexblast is a spell that deals chaos damage to a single enemy. If the target has a hex curse on it, it deals area of effect damage around the target. On hit, the spell removes the hex curse with the shortest remaining duration from each target hit. Hexblast removes the hex after dealing damage, and thus benefits from the effects of the removed curse.
Hexblast | Skill functions and interactions | Area component : By default, Hexblast only deals a vertical laser-like beam to a single target. This damage is not area damage. However, if the targeted enemy has a hex on them (even if they are unaffected), the hit will also deal damage in an area doing area damage around the target. The targeted enemy cannot be hit by the area damage as they are already hit bit the single-target hit. Concentrated Effect Support will lessen the area of the AOE and increase the area damage, but has no impact on single-target DPS.
Hexblast | Skill functions and interactions | Hex interactions : Hexblast deals more damage with hits and ailments against hexed enemies. The method in which the target is cursed does not matter. For example, they can be applied by curse on hit modifiers, Blasphemy Support , or by directly casting a hex.
Hexblast | Skill functions and interactions | Chaos Damage with Hits is Resisted by lowest Resistance instead : Chaos damage dealt by this skill is resisted by the lowest resistance on an enemy, including chaos resistance. This means that if the enemy's fire resistance is lower than cold, lightning, or chaos, it will be resisted by fire resistance instead of chaos resistance. Note that if chaos resistance is lowest, it will be resisted by chaos resistance as normal.
Hexblast | Skill functions and interactions | All Damage can Ignite, Freeze, and Shock : this modifier allows all damage types dealt by Hexblast (not necessarily just chaos) to be able to contribute to Ignite , Freeze , and Shock. This does not affect other ailments like Chill , and does not grant any #% chance to actually inflict the ailment. Chance to inflict ailments must be obtained elsewhere, or applied via a critical strike.
Hexblast | Skill functions and interactions | Critical strike chance : Hexblast has one of lowest chances to crit among all spells at 4% base critical strike chance. A popular combination of items for Hexblast - Sandstorm Visage in combination with a Prophecy Wand with a local crit roll and Harvest enchantment - can exceed 11% base crit, almost tripling its base crit chance.
Hexblast | Mines | Supporting Hexblast with High-Impact Mine Support (or Blastchain Mine Support ) bypasses Hexblast's slow cast speed. Mines are preferable to traps as traps trigger simultaneously and curse on hit is removed when traps expire, preventing Hexblast from gaining its AOE component and damage reliably. [8]
Hexblast | Hex choice | The choice of hexes that are ideal for Hexblast depends on the build type.
Hexblast | Item acquisition | Hexblast can drop anywhere.

Hexblast of Contradiction | Description | Hexblast of Contradiction is a spell that deals chaos damage to a single enemy. If the target has a hex curse on it, it deals area of effect damage around the target. On hit, the spell removes the hex curse with the shortest remaining duration from each target hit. Hexblast removes the hex after dealing damage, and thus benefits from the effects of the removed curse.
Hexblast of Contradiction | Description | It is a transfigured version of Hexblast.
Hexblast of Contradiction | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version has a higher critical strike chance , higher bonus to hit and ailment damage against hexed targets, and is resisted by the highest resistance rather than the lowest. Because of this, reducing resistances through curses and exposure is not an effective strategy for hits. Instead, it is better to use mechanics that invert or ignore resistances.
Hexblast of Contradiction | Skill functions and interactions | Chaos Damage with Hits is Resisted by highest Resistance instead :
Hexblast of Contradiction | Skill functions and interactions | Alternatively, another method is to stack extremely large amounts of penetration , e.g. via Crystallised Omniscience ; however, this is worse than doing so with the regular version of Hexblast with the appropriate curses and exposure.
Hexblast of Contradiction | Skill functions and interactions | This mod only applies to hits; damaging ailments ( Ignite and Poison ) are still resisted by fire resistance and chaos resistance respectively, unless the player equips a Blackflame. Because of this, Hexblast of Contradiction is strictly superior over regular Hexblast for ailments due to the higher base crit and damage bonus against hexed enemies.
Hexblast of Contradiction | Item acquisition | Hexblast of Contradiction has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

Hexblast of Havoc | Description | Hexblast of Havoc is a spell that deals chaos damage to a single enemy. If the target has a hex curse on it, it deals area of effect damage around the target.
Hexblast of Havoc | Description | It is a transfigured version of Hexblast.
Hexblast of Havoc | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version has a higher critical strike chance , shorter cast time, lower cost, smaller area of effect, and lower base damage but higher damage effectiveness. It also becomes significantly simpler, losing all of its gimmicks related to ailments , resistances , and curses , except doing area damage when the initial hit hits a hexed target. It also no longer has a cooldown.

Hextouch Support | Infobox | Support , Hex , Trigger Icon: & Level: (1-20) Cost & Reservation Multiplier: 130%. Requires Level 38. Must support both a skill that hits enemies, and a hex curse skill to work. The hex will be applied when enemies are hit by the other skill. Cannot support totems, traps, or mines. Minions cannot apply hexes this way. Supported Skills apply supported Curses on Hit You cannot Cast Supported Curse Spells 35% less Effect of Supported Curses Supported Skills have (35-26)% less Curse Duration Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Supported Skills have (0.5-10)% increased Curse Duration. This is a Support Gem. It does not grant a bonus to your character, but to skills in sockets connected to it. Place into an item socket connected to a socket containing the Skill Gem you wish to augment. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 38
Hextouch Support | Description | Hextouch Support is a support gem that causes a linked skill to apply linked hex (es) on hit.
Hextouch Support | Mechanics and interactions | Hexes linked to Hextouch cannot be cast directly. Another linked skill that can hit enemies is required to enable the ability to curse enemies. Hexes are applied after the first hit; the first hit will not be affected by debuffs from the applied hex. Skills that hit multiple times will benefit from the hex after the first hit.
Hextouch Support | Mechanics and interactions | Damage over time effects applied by a hexing hit (for example, Decay or ignite ) will benefit from the hex.
Hextouch Support | Mechanics and interactions | Hexes applied by supported skills no longer have an area of effect , as they only affect enemies hit with the skill.
Hextouch Support | Triggering | Minions, totems, mines, and traps cannot apply hexes linked to Hextouch Support.
Hextouch Support | Triggering | As Hextouch does not actually trigger the linked hex and instead applies the equivalent hex to an enemy, the player does not spend mana or other resources when the hex is applied.
Hextouch Support | Triggering | Hextouch is only considered a trigger for the linked hex and not the skill which is applying it (see Trigger ). This means supporting a triggered skill (e.g. The Hidden Blade 's Unseen Strike) does not disable that skill by giving it two triggers. It can also be used with Cast when Damage Taken Support as long as the hex's required level is higher than the threshold from the CwDT support, preventing it from being supported by CwDT (and therefore disabling it due to having two triggers).
Hextouch Support | Item acquisition | Hextouch Support can drop anywhere.

Holy Flame Totem | Description | Holy Flame Totem summons a totem that channels a stream of fire at nearby enemies, dealing fire damage to them. Damage from the totem deals physical damage converted to fire damage. Each totem also creates consecrated ground around it that also makes allies standing on it immune to curses.
Holy Flame Totem | Skill Functions and Interactions | Additional projectiles : Only one projectile will hit an enemy per cast. Increasing the number of projectiles will only help to hit more enemies per cast, but will not multiply the damages on enemies.
Holy Flame Totem | Skill Functions and Interactions | Projectile speed: Affects the distance that the projectile will travel as well as its speed.
Holy Flame Totem | Skill Functions and Interactions | Channelling: The totem's spell is considered a channelling skill , so it will benefit from bonuses related to channelling.
Holy Flame Totem | Skill Functions and Interactions | Range: Totem range is 100 units.
Holy Flame Totem | Skill Functions and Interactions | This skill is affected by any passives that affect totems, however, nodes such as Totemic Zeal will not be displayed in tooltip damage per second due to engine limitations.
Holy Flame Totem | Skill Functions and Interactions | If you wear The Three Dragons unique helmet and use Herald of Thunder skill, shocked enemies killed by your Flame Totem will not activate Herald of Thunder's on kill effect and this is intentional. [1]

Hydrosphere | Infobox | Spell , AoE , Duration , Cold , Orb , Lightning , Physical Level: (1-20) Cost: (12-20) Mana Cast Time: 0.60 sec Critical Strike Chance: 5.00% Effectiveness of Added Damage: (65-110)%. Requires Level 34. Create a sphere of water, or move an existing sphere and reset its duration. The sphere continually applies a drenched debuff with a short duration to enemies in its area or those it moves through. You can hit the sphere with other skills to inflict Cold and Lightning Ailments (other than Chill). The sphere pulses when created, at regular intervals while afflicted by an Ailment, and after moving, damaging all drenched enemies in a large area. Deals (37-510) to (56-765) Physical Damage Base duration is 8.00 seconds Base secondary duration is 4.00 seconds Pulses every 0.4 seconds while Frozen, Shocked, Brittle or Sapped Can only be Hit once every 1 second Drenched Enemies have Cold and Lightning Exposure, applying -10% to Resistances 100% of Physical Damage Converted to Cold Damage while Sphere has no Ailments 100% of Physical Damage Converted to Lightning Damage while sphere is Shocked or Sapped 100% of Physical Damage Converted to Cold Damage while Sphere is Frozen or Brittle Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: +(0.5-10)% increased Pulse Frequency. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 34
Hydrosphere | Description | Hydrosphere is a skill gem that creates a sphere of water, or moves an existing sphere and resets its duration. The sphere continually applies a drenched debuff with a short duration to enemies in its area or those it moves through. You can hit the sphere with other skills to inflict cold and lightning ailments (other than chill ). The sphere pulses at regular intervals while afflicted by an ailment, and after moving, damaging all drenched enemies in a large area with that ailment. It is classified as an Intelligence Skill Gem.
Hydrosphere | Skill functions | Using the same skill again while a Hydrosphere already exists will cause it move to the new location, but will not reset the timer to be able to hit the orb again. However, by alternating two copies of the Hydrosphere gem, it will instead destroy the existing orb and create a new one, allowing it to be hit-able immediately. [1] [2]
Hydrosphere | Conversion | The gem's advanced description specifies (If both conversions apply, each will convert 50%). In other words, if affected by both a Cold (excluding Chill) and Lightning Ailment, it will convert its Physical damage to 50% Cold and 50% Lightning damage.
Hydrosphere | Conversion | You can only have one Hydrosphere at a time; whenever you recast the skill, it will either cause the sphere to pulse and refresh its duration in the same location, or the existing hydrosphere moves to the targeted location without pulsing and the duration is refreshed. Refreshing or moving the hydrosphere will not affect the pulsing behaviour from inflicting an ailment on it.
Hydrosphere | Repeat | When Echoed , the placement of the sphere will be repeated, but the pulses will always count as a final repeat, making it synergize well with Plume of Pursuit.
Hydrosphere | On-hit mechanics | Skills that hit enemies can also hit the hydrosphere. This can happen even when no enemies are around. [3] The hydrosphere itself cannot be directly targeted, so skills that require a specific target (e.g. Flicker Strike) and are not merely cast at a location will typically not work with Hydrosphere.
Hydrosphere | On-hit mechanics | A Hydrosphere is considered a solid entity; it can block narrow passageways or bridges unless an entity has Phasing or can blink over small obstacles. Projectiles that hit a Hydrosphere will consume a hit and cause any relevant projectile behaviours to occur. It can only be hit once per second, a cooldown that cannot be modified by increased cooldown recovery rate. Only the owner of the Hydrosphere skill (or their proxies ) can hit the Hydrosphere; it cannot be hit by enemies, minions, or party members. [4]
Hydrosphere | On-hit mechanics | Ailments inflicted by the orb's own damage can proliferate , but ailments inflicted on the orb cannot.
Hydrosphere | On-hit mechanics | The orb has a minimum ailment threshold as if it were a monster. This ailment threshold scales with gem level, requiring more damage to inflict an ailment on it at higher gem levels.
Hydrosphere | Skill interactions | Brands : Brands cannot attach to hydrosphere. Activations of brands attached to other creatures can hit the hydrosphere.
Hydrosphere | Skill interactions | Chain Hook : Grants Rage when hitting the hydrosphere. At a sufficient distance, Chain Hook will pull the player towards the hydrosphere.
Hydrosphere | Skill interactions | Chaining skills : Skills can chain off the hydrosphere on their first hit. They will not chain from the enemies to the hydrosphere.
Hydrosphere | Skill interactions | Curses : Hydrosphere cannot be affected by hexes or marks. However, since Hydrosphere itself does hit with its pulses, it can be used to curse on hit when linked with Hextouch Support.
Hydrosphere | Skill interactions | Explosive Arrow : Explosive Arrows can stick into a hydrosphere and will explode as expected.
Hydrosphere | Skill interactions | Frenzy : Grants frenzy charge when hitting the hydrosphere.
Hydrosphere | Skill interactions | Herald of Thunder : Lightning strikes will target the hydrosphere but since it cannot shock , it would need to be able to inflict another valid ailment to make it pulse.
Hydrosphere | Skill interactions | Strike skills : When combined with skills that deal area of effect damage, such as Molten Strike or skills supported by Melee Splash Support , these extra strikes can cause an area of effect overlap back onto the primary target that may increase single target DPS. However, after the addition of a 1 second hit internal cooldown, this may no longer be an effective strategy.
Hydrosphere | Reflected Ailments | Maligaro's Restraint : Shocking hydrosphere reflects the same shock on the player.
Hydrosphere | Reflected Ailments | The Fulcrum : All Cold and Lightning ailments applied to hydrosphere reflect to the player.
Hydrosphere | Reflected Ailments | Note: Ailments inflicted on your Hydrosphere cannot proliferate , even after they are reflected on you.
Hydrosphere | Item acquisition | Hydrosphere can drop anywhere.
Hydrosphere | Recipes | Hydrosphere can be created from the following recipes:
Hydrosphere | Quest reward | This item is given as a quest reward for the following quests:

Hypothermia Support | Description | Hypothermia is a support gem that grants supported skill gems more damage with hits and ailments against chilled enemies, more cold damage over time against chilled enemies, and a chance to freeze enemies that are already chilled.
Hypothermia Support | Skill functions and interactions | Hypothermia only works with hits , damaging ailments , and cold damage over time. This excludes non-ailment damage over time effects from skills like Scorching Ray and Righteous Fire. If applicable, the gem will multiply damage dealt of all types, not just the cold portion.
Hypothermia Support | Skill functions and interactions | Since the gem requires the enemy to be in an already-chilled state this means that, in general, the attacker's initial hit will not receive the damage bonus. Only subsequent hits will receive the damage bonus, unless the attacker is able to apply chill prior to the first hit (such as with a ground effect spell like Vortex or Cold Snap , or from minion such as Summon Skitterbots ).
Hypothermia Support | Item acquisition | Hypothermia Support can drop anywhere.

Ice Crash | Description | Ice Crash is an attack skill that strikes the ground, converting your physical damage to cold damage , and creating a wave of expanding icicles in an area around you. The damage is weaker further away from you.
Ice Crash | Description | Ice Crash requires a sword, mace, scepter, axe or a staff, but can also be used while unarmed.
Ice Crash | Skill functions and interactions | Any enemy hit by a stage cannot be hit again by subsequent stage(s). The tooltip damage reported is for the first (central) stage. [1]

Ice Crash of Cadence | Description | Ice Crash of Cadence is an attack skill that strikes the ground, converting your physical damage to cold damage , and creating a wave of icicles from both weapons in an area around you. Ice Crash requires dual wielding a sword, mace, sceptre, or axe (or unarmed with Hollow Palm Technique ; see below).
Ice Crash of Cadence | Description | It is a transfigured version of Ice Crash.
Ice Crash of Cadence | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version can only be used while dual wielding. Instead of creating an expanding wave with three stages, it creates one wave. The wave deals more damage if the main hand weapon was used, and the wave has more radius if the off-hand weapon was used. It has faster attack speed and less mana cost, but lower damage effectiveness.
Ice Crash of Cadence | Item acquisition | Ice Crash of Cadence has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

Ice Shot | Infobox | Attack , Projectile , AoE , Cold , Bow Level: (1-20) Cost: (6-10) Mana Attack Damage: (149-200)% of base Effectiveness of Added Damage: (149-200)% Projectile Speed: 3260 AoE Radius: 23 / 10. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 5
Ice Shot | Description | Ice Shot is a ranged attack skill that fires an arrow that deals cold damage to the target and in a cone behind the target. The skill has a stronger threshold to apply the chill.
Ice Shot | Skill Functions and Interactions | Cone explosion: On hitting a target, Ice Shot creates a cone-shaped explosion that damages enemies behind the target. A target hit by an arrow cannot also be hit by the cone from that arrow [1]. However, cones from other arrows or exploding from other targets can still hit the target. The cones can overlap. The cone effect occurs on every hit against an enemy or obstacle, regardless of projectile behaviours.
Ice Shot | Skill Functions and Interactions | Area of effect modifiers scale the size of the cone; however, it does not scale the angle of the cone itself.
Ice Shot | Item acquisition | Ice Shot can drop anywhere.

Ice Shot of Penetration | Description | Ice Shot of Penetration is a bow attack skill that fires a piercing arrow that deals cold damage to the target and in two cones perpendicular to the target on both sides. The skill has a stronger threshold to apply the chill.
Ice Shot of Penetration | Description | It is a transfigured version of Ice Shot.
Ice Shot of Penetration | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version has innate pierce and deals damage in cones on either side of hit targets instead of behind them. It also has a higher damage effectiveness, but slower attack speed and higher cost.
Ice Shot of Penetration | Skill functions and interactions | Cone explosion: On hitting a target, Ice Shot of Penetration creates two cone-shaped explosions that damage enemies perpendicularly to either side of the target. A target hit by an arrow cannot also be hit by the cone from that arrow [1] [2]. However, cones from other arrows or exploding from other targets can still hit the target. The cones can overlap. The cone effect occurs on every hit against an enemy or obstacle, regardless of projectile behaviours.
Ice Shot of Penetration | Skill functions and interactions | General area of effect modifiers scale the area of the cone, not the angle. The angle is scaled by #% Area of Effect angle found primarily on the gem's quality stat. This angle can exceed 180° with enough investment, causing the two cones to overlap part of their areas. Note that extreme values are not reflected by the visual effect, which is limited to a semicircle.
Ice Shot of Penetration | Skill functions and interactions | Fury Valve can be used to bypass the default piercing behaviour with split.
Ice Shot of Penetration | Item acquisition | Ice Shot of Penetration has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

Ice Shot of Penetration | Threshold jewels | Note that the jewels may be drop disabled and can only be found in permanent leagues as legacy items.

Ice Spear | Description | Ice Spear is a cold spell that fires projectiles in sequence. After traveling a short distance the projectiles enter a second form. The second form pierces all targets and has increased projectile speed and critical strike chance and multiplier. This unique feature of Ice Spear makes it ideal for damaging distant targets, but at the same time makes it noticeably weaker at close range.

Immolate Support | Description | Immolation Support is a support gem that grants additional fire damage with hits against burning enemies.
Immolate Support | Skill functions and interactions | Immolate will only increase the damage of skills capable of hitting , so skills such as Scorching Ray and Righteous Fire receive no benefit from the added damage.
Immolate Support | Skill functions and interactions | The added damage only applies if the enemy is burning, such as if they are Ignited. If relying on the skill supported by Immolate to inflict ignite, given no other sources of burning, the first hit will not benefit from the added damage of Immolate, even if it ignites the target. The enemy must be burning prior to the hit for Immolate to apply.

Impending Doom Support | Description | Impending Doom is a support gem for hex curses. When the hex on the supported skill expires, it triggers Doom Blast to deal chaos damage in an area around it. It cannot support curse skills which are triggered or applied as an aura with Blasphemy Support.
Impending Doom Support | Skill functions and interactions | Doom Blast is considered a spell , therefore modifiers to spell damage apply to it.
Impending Doom Support | Skill functions and interactions | Doom Blast is triggered when a hex ends, and its damage benefits from bonuses of the ending hex. E.g. the reduced chaos resistance from Despair.
Impending Doom Support | Skill functions and interactions | Due to animation timing differences between classes , some classes like rangers require slightly more cast speed than classes like witches when using a curse with Impending Doom and a different curse with Spell Cascade Support triggered through Vixen's Entrapment. [1]
