Incinerate | Description | Incinerate is a channelling fire spell that launches a torrent of fire from the user, rapidly dealing fire damage. The longer the channel is held, the bigger the damage and area becomes. Releasing the channel launches a wave of fire that deals more hit and ignite damage, and has 100% ignite chance.
Incinerate | Item acquisition | Incinerate can drop anywhere.
Incinerate | Recipes | Incinerate can be created from the following recipes:
Incinerate | Vendor reward | This item can be bought at the listed NPC vendors , after completing the following quests:
Incinerate | Basic Mathematical Modelling | The following calculation aims towards characters that have a critical strike chance of either 0% or a critical strike multiplier that is strictly 100% (e.g. for characters with the keystones Resolute Technique or Elemental Overload allocated). It can be easily extended to account for critical hits which can be implemented as a multiplicative factor to the skills average base damage.
Incinerate | Basic Mathematical Modelling | The additional damage from ignite is assumed to be negligible. Characters that specialise in burning damage are aiming towards the largest ignite damage possible and always want to release at maximum stage if possible.
Incinerate | Basic Mathematical Modelling | Under these assumptions, one can calculate the average base damage dealt per second while channelling at n stages as
Incinerate | Basic Mathematical Modelling | DMG a v g = DMG m i n + DMG m a x 2 \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}{\begin{aligned}{\text{DMG}}_{avg}={\frac {{\text{DMG}}_{min}+{\text{DMG}}_{max}}{2}}\end{aligned}}
Incinerate | Basic Mathematical Modelling | DPS(n) = DMG a v g CastTime ( 1 + n ⋅ 0.25 ) \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}{\begin{aligned}{\text{DPS(n)}}={\frac {{\text{DMG}}_{avg}}{\text{CastTime}}}\left(1+n\cdot 0.25\right)\end{aligned}}
Incinerate | Basic Mathematical Modelling | The average damage dealt while ramping to n stages is
Incinerate | Basic Mathematical Modelling | RampUpDPS = 1 n + 1 DMG a v g CastTime ∑ k = 0 n ( 1 + k ⋅ 0.25 ) = DMG a v g CastTime ( 1 + n ⋅ 0.25 2 ) \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}{\begin{aligned}{\text{RampUpDPS}}&={\frac {1}{n+1}}{\frac {{\text{DMG}}_{avg}}{\text{CastTime}}}\sum _{k=0}^{n}\left(1+k\cdot 0.25\right)\\&={\frac {{\text{DMG}}_{avg}}{\text{CastTime}}}\left(1+{\frac {n\cdot 0.25}{2}}\right)\end{aligned}}
Incinerate | Basic Mathematical Modelling | Similarly, the average damage dealt by the final wave after channelling to n stages (2n casts) and releasing can be calculated to be
Incinerate | Basic Mathematical Modelling | ReleaseDPS = 6 ⋅ DMG a v g 2 n ⋅ CastTime ( 1 + n ⋅ 0.25 ) = DMG a v g CastTime ( 0.75 + 3 n ) \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}{\begin{aligned}{\text{ReleaseDPS}}&={\frac {6\cdot {\text{DMG}}_{avg}}{2n\cdot {\text{CastTime}}}}\left(1+n\cdot 0.25\right)\\&={\frac {{\text{DMG}}_{avg}}{\text{CastTime}}}\left(0.75+{\frac {3}{n}}\right)\end{aligned}}
Incinerate | Basic Mathematical Modelling | for the average ramp-up dps when building stages. To calculate the average damage dealt by repeatedly releasing at maximum stages, we have to sum the latter two formulas as follows
Incinerate | Basic Mathematical Modelling | FinalDPSContinuedReleasing = RampUpDPS + ReleaseDPS = DMG a v g CastTime ( 1.75 + n 8 + 3 n ) \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}{\begin{aligned}{\text{FinalDPSContinuedReleasing}}&={\text{RampUpDPS}}+{\text{ReleaseDPS}}\\&={\frac {{\text{DMG}}_{avg}}{\text{CastTime}}}\left(1.75+{\frac {n}{8}}+{\frac {3}{n}}\right)\end{aligned}}
Incinerate | Basic Mathematical Modelling | Thus the average dps multiplier of channelling to 10 stages and releasing will be 1.75 + 10 8 + 3 10 {\textstyle \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}1.75+{\frac {10}{8}}+{\frac {3}{10}}} = 3.3, while the average dps multiplier of sustained channelling at 10 stages (disregarding ramp-up and final wave damage) is 1 + 10 4 {\textstyle \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}1+{\frac {10}{4}}} = 3.5.

Incinerate | Related helmet enchantments | Note that helmet enchantments are no longer obtainable since version 3.23.0 and can only be obtained in permanent leagues as already enchanted items from before this patch.

Incinerate of Expanse | Description | Incinerate of Expanse is a channelling fire spell that launches a torrent of fire from the user, rapidly dealing fire damage. The longer the channel is held, the bigger the damage and area becomes. Releasing the channel launches a slow-moving wave of fire that always Ignites , dealing more hit and ignite damage.
Incinerate of Expanse | Description | It is a transfigured version of Incinerate.
Incinerate of Expanse | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version has fewer maximum stages, a higher mana cost, and a slower cast time. Its final wave has higher damage multipliers and greater range, but moves considerably slower.
Incinerate of Expanse | Skill functions and interactions | Note that the base DPS of Incinerate of Expanse is 0.66 of the base DPS of Incinerate due to its longer cast time, so the attached DPS graphs (which show the multipliers over each skill's respective base dps) cannot be directly compared.

Increased Area of Effect Support | Infobox | Support , AoE Icon: E Level: (1-20) Cost & Reservation Multiplier: 130%. Requires Level 38. Supports any skill with an area of effect. Increased Area of Effect Support | Description | Increased Area of Effect is a support gem. This gem will increase the radius of any areas of effect produced by the linked skill. For a list of skills that feature an area of effect, see AoE skills.
Increased Area of Effect Support | Skill functions and interactions | If a skill has more than one area of effect, all of them will be affected. For instance Firestorm has two areas of effect, one which defines the area that the fireballs can fall in, and another which defines the area in which the individual fireballs deal damage. Using Increased Area of Effect with Firestorm will result in larger fireballs, but spread out over a larger area.
Increased Area of Effect Support | Skill functions and interactions | If linked to a skill that creates a minion , totem , trap , or mine , this gem will increase the radius of any AoE skills used by the summoned ally. For instance if a Necromancer is raised as a spectre , any curses it casts will have a larger AoE. Monster auras on rare monsters are not skills, they're an inherent property of the monsters, and will not be affected by this gem.
Increased Area of Effect Support | Skill functions and interactions | The gem will not affect the trigger radius of a trap or mine, or a totem's range.
Increased Area of Effect Support | Item acquisition | Increased Area of Effect Support can drop anywhere.

Increased Area of Effect Support | Vendor reward | This item can be bought at the listed NPC vendors , after completing the following quests:

Increased Critical Strikes Support | Description | Increased Critical Strikes Support is a support gem. Any skills linked to this gem will have an increased chance of scoring a critical strike.
Increased Critical Strikes Support | Skill functions and interactions | Increased Critical Strikes Support grants both increased percentage chance and added base critical strike chance, which stacks additively with other increased Critical Strike Chance modifiers.
Increased Critical Strikes Support | Skill functions and interactions | The supported skill must have the ability to score a critical strike for this gem to have any effect. For example, linking this gem to a curse will have no effect.
Increased Critical Strikes Support | Skill functions and interactions | Linking Increased Critical Strikes to a skill that creates a minion or remote ( totem , trap , mine ) will affect its chance to crit if it can already crit (curses, auras and the like being exempt).

Infernal Blow | Infobox | Attack , AoE , Melee , Strike , Fire , Duration Level: (1-20) Cost: (6-10) Mana Attack Damage: (164-377)% of base Effectiveness of Added Damage: (164-377)% AoE Radius: 15. Requires Level 12. Attacks with your weapon, applying a charged debuff to enemies hit by your strike, and an uncharged debuff to any other enemies hit. Upon reaching 6 charges, expiring, or the enemy's death, the charged debuff is removed to damage that and other surrounding enemies. Enemies with either debuff explode when they die, damaging other surrounding enemies. Damage from this explosion cannot be reflected. Requires a Sword, Axe, Mace, Sceptre, Staff or Unarmed. Base duration is 0.80 seconds Explosion deals base Fire Damage equal to 6% of the corpse's Maximum Life 60% of Physical Damage Converted to Fire Damage Base radius is 2.4 metres Base explosion radius is 1.8 metres Debuff deals 66% of Damage per Charge Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Debuff deals +(0.5-10)% of Damage per Charge. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 12
Infernal Blow | Description | Infernal Blow is a melee attack skill that converts 60% of Physical Damage to Fire Damage and applies a debuff to enemies. The debuff causes enemies to explode on death, dealing secondary damage. Additionally, if the debuff has charges, it deals attack area damage when an enemy gains 6 stacks, when the debuff expires, or when the enemy dies. This skill requires a sword, axe, mace, staff or unarmed.
Infernal Blow | Skill functions and interactions | Damage conversion : Like all converted damage, the fire portion of Infernal Blow's damage will be modified by both fire damage increases and physical damage increases.
Infernal Blow | Skill functions and interactions | Uncharged debuff : At 0 charges, the debuff only causes enemies to explode on death, scaling with the affected enemy's maximum life.
Infernal Blow | Skill functions and interactions | Charged debuff : With 1 or more charge, the debuff will also deal attack area damage on expiration, enemy death, or reaching 6 charges.
Infernal Blow | Skill functions and interactions | Multiple skill gems : If an enemy with a charged debuff from one gem is hit by infernal blow from another gem, the previous debuff is removed without dealing damage. This means that you can't use one set of links to apply the first 5 debuffs and a different one to cause 6-charge debuff damage. Different sources (i.e. General's Cry mirages) can stack charged debuffs, as long as the skill is provided by the same gem.

Infernal Blow of Immolation | Description | Infernal Blow of Immolation is a melee strike skill that deals physical damage to fire damage and applies a charge debuff to the user on hit. When the debuff reaches six charges, it deals damage in an area of effect around the user. This skill requires a sword, axe, mace, staff or unarmed.
Infernal Blow of Immolation | Description | It is a transfigured version of Infernal Blow.
Infernal Blow of Immolation | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version has a longer duration, more area of effect , and applies the charge debuff to the user instead of to enemies. It no longer applies uncharged debuffs to surrounding enemies or explodes enemies on death.
Infernal Blow of Immolation | Skill functions and interactions | Only the debuff hit is considered area damage ; the strike which applies the debuff does not scale with area.
Infernal Blow of Immolation | Skill functions and interactions | Charged debuff : With 1 or more charge, the debuff will also deal attack area damage on expiration or reaching 6 charges.
Infernal Blow of Immolation | Skill functions and interactions | Corpses : Since this version does not explode enemies like the non-transfigured gem, it can interact with mechanics that require corpses, such as General's Cry or Kaom's Command.
Infernal Blow of Immolation | Item acquisition | Infernal Blow of Immolation has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

Infernal Cry | Description | Infernal Cry is a warcry skill gem that taunts nearby enemies, debuffs enemies to explode on death, buffs the user and allies to gain extra fire damage based on their physical damage, and exerts a number of subsequent attacks, causing those attacks to trigger the Combust attack.
Infernal Cry | Skill functions and interactions | Note: Due to technical limitations, this page's infobox displays gem stats belonging to both Infernal Cry and Combust. These are distinct skills and do not share all their stats. Infernal Cry's Damage cannot be Reflected only applies to its debuff's explosion, not hits by Combust. Similarly, Combust's 60% of Physical Damage Converted to Fire Damage will not affect physical damage added to Infernal Cry's debuff explosion.
Infernal Cry | Skill functions and interactions | Both the explosion and Combust can be affected by support gems linked to Infernal Cry. [1]
Infernal Cry | Debuff | The on-death explosion caused by the debuff deals secondary damage , not attack damage. It is affected by relevant stats from both support gems and the player's global modifiers , such as increased fire or area damage.
Infernal Cry | Exerting | Attacks exerted by Infernal Cry trigger the Combust skill, a melee attack that deals damage in an area.
Infernal Cry | Buff | Buff grants gain 5% of Physical Damage as Extra Fire Damage per 5 power, counting a maximum of 25 Power :

Innervate Support | Skill function and interactions | Comparison with Added Lightning Damage Support :
Innervate Support | Skill function and interactions | Because the Innervation applies to all attacks and spells, it is wise to use this gem with a side, AoE skill. Shock then kill an enemy to gain Innervation, then switch to your main skill for maximum damage for 8 seconds. Inspiration Support | Infobox | Critical , Support Icon: r Level: (1-20) Cost & Reservation Multiplier: 120%. Requires Level 31. Supports any skill. This is a Support Gem. It does not grant a bonus to your character, but to skills in sockets connected to it. Place into an item socket connected to a socket containing the Skill Gem you wish to augment. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 31
Inspiration Support | Description | Inspiration Support is a support gem that lessens the mana cost of linked skills. When the supported skill is used, it grants an Inspiration Charge , which grants more elemental damage and increased critical strike chance to linked skills. You lose all Inspiration Charges after you spend a certain amount of mana.
Inspiration Support | Skill functions and interactions | Supported skills grant the user Inspiration Charges when they spend mana on upfront costs or effects (e.g. Arcane Cloak ), up to 5 charges. Paying mana costs without actually spending mana (e.g. Eldritch Battery ) will not grant Inspiration charges. [1]
Inspiration Support | Skill functions and interactions | Inspiration Charges last for 10 seconds. If you haven't generated a charge within those 10 seconds, you will lose all Inspiration Charges. Gaining a charge refreshes the duration of all charges.
Inspiration Support | Skill functions and interactions | The mana counter is shared between all skills linked with Inspiration Support, up to the respective cap. As soon as a skill is used that puts the total mana spent above the threshold, the mana counter is reset to 0 and all Inspiration Charges are removed.
Inspiration Support | Skill functions and interactions | Charges gained by using a skill are not associated with that specific skill; all skills supported by Inspiration Support will gain the support's bonuses while you have charges, even skills that never spend mana, like Righteous Fire.
Inspiration Support | Skill functions and interactions | The less mana cost of skills modifier is independent, multiplicative with other less modifiers and reductions. It does not affect mana reservations.
Inspiration Support | Effective usage | Generally, lower mana costs result in better usage of Inspiration Charges. For example, if it takes 100 mana to cast a skill, the first 5 casts will average at 50% of the gem's potential and then the next 3 casts will be at 100% potential, while a mana cost of 1 will result in 795 casts at maximum efficiency after the first 5 initial casts.
Inspiration Support | Effective usage | Cast On Critical Strike Support - Since attacks usually cost much less mana to use than spells , especially Cyclone , it results in more efficient use of Inspiration Support. Both the attack and spell(s) in a common Cast On Critical Strike set-up will be supported, granting increased critical strike chance and more elemental damage to both.
Inspiration Support | Effective usage | Herald of Ice and Herald of Thunder - The effects of these Heralds only scale off of elemental damage and not spell damage. Generic critical strike chance works for Herald Skills aswell (assuming they are given a base critical strike chance through other means like Increased Critical Strikes Support ). Supporting both an attack like Shield Charge , which has a low mana cost, and the Heralds with Inspiration Support will make maintaining Inspiration Charges easy.
Inspiration Support | Effective usage | Righteous Fire will appreciate the very rare "more elemental damage" multiplier. By supporting both Righteous Fire and another skill, like Shield Charge, with Inspiration Support, it is possible to generate charges with the attack, while having Righteous Fire benefit from the support gem.
Inspiration Support | Effective usage | Builds that use Ignite as their main damage source can use the "more elemental damage" multiplier to scale both the initial hit and the ailment.
Inspiration Support | Effective usage | So while this gem doesn't have the highest more -multiplier, the downside of generating and maintaining charges can be easily managed while other support gems come at a steep opportunity cost such as reducing critical strike chance with Controlled Destruction Support or being unable to inflict elemental ailments with Elemental Focus Support.
Inspiration Support | Effective usage | Inspiration Support has a breakpoint at gem level 25 to hit 6% more Damage per charge. This can be achieved with the modifier +1 to Level of Socketed Gems on gear, a level 21 Inspiration Support Gem, and +3 to Level of all Critical Support Gems from a Critical Mastery.
Inspiration Support | Item acquisition | Inspiration Support can drop anywhere.

Inspiration Support | Vendor reward | This item can be bought at the listed NPC vendors , after completing the following quests:

Intimidating Cry | Description | Intimidating Cry is a warcry skill that taunts and intimidates nearby enemies and grants a buff that grants increased movement speed , based on the power of nearby enemies. It also exerts your next attacks, making them deal double damage.
Intimidating Cry | Skill functions and interactions | Power : Normal Monsters have 1 Power, Magic Monsters 2, Rare Monsters 10, Unique Monsters 20, and Players 5
Intimidating Cry | Skill functions and interactions | Enemies Taunted by this Warcry are Intimidated : The Intimidate debuff from this warcry is conditional based on this warcry's taunt affecting the target. Overwriting the taunt, such as with another warcry or through #% chance to Taunt on Hit , will remove the Intimidate debuff early. This can make its uptime unreliable in any setup that uses more than one warcry.

Item Quantity Support | Description | Item Quantity is a support gem. It increases the quantity of items dropped by monsters killed by the supported skill. This skill gem no longer drops in the game, and can only be obtained by trading for existing ones in the permanent leagues. [1] However, it can be found as a foil in the Legacy league through Reliquary Key. [ citation needed ]
Item Quantity Support | Description | See also: Item Rarity Support
Item Quantity Support | Skill functions and interactions | Damage over time : Increased item quantity from this gem applies also to enemies killed by damage over time effects. [2]
Item Quantity Support | Skill functions and interactions | Containers : The modifier from this gem does not apply to the quantity of items dropped by opening chests, strongboxes, or other containers. Mechanics that have a defined drop count are also unaffected. For example, the number of abyss jewel drops from Abyssal Troves, essences from imprisoned monster, Blight reward chests, etc, are not affected.
Item Quantity Support | Skill functions and interactions | Boss drop : While this will affect the quantity of items drops from a map boss, it will not affect the boss specific drop quantity. Fragments from guardians and conquerors, boss specific uniques and boss specific currencies are not affected by item quantity.
Item Quantity Support | Skill functions and interactions | Maps : This gem does not affect the number of maps that drop.
Item Quantity Support | Skill functions and interactions | When linked with a skill that creates a minion , trap , mine , or totem , the item quantity modifier will apply to kills made by the minion/trap/mine/totem. This includes deaths caused by Minion Instability explosions.
Item Quantity Support | Item acquisition | Item Quantity Support is drop disabled.

Item Rarity Support | Description | Item Rarity Support is a support gem that increases the rarity of items dropped by monsters when they are killed using the supported skill.
Item Rarity Support | Skill functions and interactions | The rarity bonus from this gem applies to enemies killed by damage over time effects sourced by linked skills. Poisons/ignites inflicted by Master Toxicist , Bino's Kitchen Knife , Berek's Respite , or Abberath's Hooves will not be supported by Item Rarity support, but will use the players generic IIQ/IIR modifiers. [1] [2]
Item Rarity Support | Skill functions and interactions | The degeneration effect that is first applied to a monster is responsible for its death [3]. So, if Item Rarity is used with multiple sources of damage over time (like Essence Drain + Contagion ), it should be linked with the skill that is cast first (assuming both the initial Contagion and Essence Drain are still applying to the enemy when it dies).
Item Rarity Support | Skill functions and interactions | When linked with a skill that creates a minion , trap , mine , or totem , the item rarity modifier does apply to kills made by the minion/trap/mine/totem. This includes deaths caused by Minion Instability explosions.
Item Rarity Support | Skill functions and interactions | Since the increased Rarity of Items only applies on kill, combining this gem with Culling Strike Support can be useful when using multiple skills, or while playing in a party , as it increases the chances of the linked skill being the one that lands the kill.
Item Rarity Support | Item acquisition | Item Rarity Support can drop anywhere.
Item Rarity Support | Recipes | Item Rarity Support can be created from the following recipes:
Item Rarity Support | Vendor reward | This item can be bought at the listed NPC vendors , after completing the following quests:
Item Rarity Support | Related modifiers | The following modifiers are related to Item Rarity Support: No results found

Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 28
Kinetic Blast | Description | Kinetic Blast is an attack skill that fires a projectile from a wand , causing explosions around the location of the projectile's impact, dealing damage to enemies caught in their area of effect.
Kinetic Blast | Skill functions and interactions | Damage calculations : Only increases and reductions to Spell Damage are applied to attacks. More and less modifiers do not apply to attacks.
Kinetic Blast | Skill functions and interactions | Explosions : Explosions occur around each collision, divided across equal arcs (like pizza slices). When there is no space in an arc to create an explosion, the explosion for that arc is skipped. A typical-sized target is not hit by explosions created from a collision against them, and because modifiers to area of effect apply to both the explosions' spread and size, increasing or reducing the aoe does not cause overlap against a single target. [1] Greater area will however increase coverage against multiple targets.
Kinetic Blast | Skill functions and interactions | Multiple Projectiles : Because each collision can create explosions, and each explosion can hit each target inside its area, the number of hits against multiple enemies or enemies near terrain can be greatly increased by firing additional projectiles, such as with Greater Multiple Projectiles Support. Shotgun prevention will still limit the number of direct collision from each set of simultaneous projectiles to 1 per enemy, however.
Kinetic Blast | Skill functions and interactions | Projectile behaviours : Unlike Fireball , Kinetic Blast projectiles do not need to end to create aoe effects. Explosions will be created on collision, even when the projectile continues with Split , Pierce , Fork , Chain or Return.
Kinetic Blast | Skill functions and interactions | Targets : In addition to enemies and terrain, projectiles may also create explosions from collisions with Tornado and Hydrosphere , which may be triggered by Kinetic Blast through Spellslinger.
Kinetic Blast | Item acquisition | Kinetic Blast can drop anywhere.

Kinetic Blast of Clustering | Description | Kinetic Blast of Clustering is a wand attack skill that fires a projectile that causes a series of explosions when it collides with enemies or terrain, damaging enemies caught in each area of effect.
Kinetic Blast of Clustering | Description | It is a transfigured version of Kinetic Blast.
Kinetic Blast of Clustering | Changes from default version | This transfigured version has several changes compared to its original version:
Kinetic Blast of Clustering | Skill functions and interactions | Greater Multiple Projectiles Support Lesser Multiple Projectiles Support Volley Support : The skill still fires a single projectile, though the support's additional projectiles do grant additional explosions.
Kinetic Blast of Clustering | Skill functions and interactions | Fury Valve : This amulet's unique projectile modifier has no effect, as Kinetic Blast of Clustering projectiles cannot split. Additional projectiles (from this amulet or otherwise) still grant additional explosions.
Kinetic Blast of Clustering | Skill functions and interactions | When used with Spellslinger linked to Tornado , KBoC will hit the tornado, allowing it to benefit from fork (e.g. Fork Support , Runegraft of Refraction ) to create additional projectiles.
Kinetic Blast of Clustering | Item acquisition | Kinetic Blast of Clustering has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

Kinetic Bolt of Fragmentation | Description | Kinetic Bolt of Fragmentation is a wand attack skill that fires a bolt that forks in a zig-zag pattern at it travels, releasing secondary projectiles as it forks.
Kinetic Bolt of Fragmentation | Description | It is a transfigured version of Kinetic Bolt.
Kinetic Bolt of Fragmentation | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version deals less damage per hit, changes directions fewer times, and no longer applies increases and reductions to spell damage to attack damage. In exchange, it fires additional projectiles, and can bypass the game's shotgun prevention to hit the same enemy with all projectiles from one attack. Quality causes projectiles to change direction additional times instead.

Kinetic Fusillade | Infobox | Attack , Projectile , Duration , AoE Level: (1-20) Cost: (4-6) Mana Attack Speed: 200% of base Attack Damage: (93-107)% of base Effectiveness of Added Damage: (93-107)%. Requires Level 12. Wave your wand to create a hovering kinetic projectile. After a short duration, the projectile is fired at an enemy, dealing area damage on impact and chaining to further targets. Using this attack again will reset the duration for all hovering projectiles. Multiple projectiles fired in sequence will attempt to target different enemies. Cannot be directly used by traps or mines. Increases and Reductions to Spell Damage also apply to Attack Damage from this Skill at 150% of their value Base duration is 0.70 seconds Chains +3 Times Base radius is 1.2 metres Projectiles deal (3-8)% more Damage per previous Projectile fired in sequence Maximum 12 hovering Projectiles Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Projectiles deal +(0.2-4)% more Damage per previous Projectile fired in sequence. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 12
Kinetic Fusillade | Description | Kinetic Fusillade is a projectile attack skill. Each attack creates a hovering energy projectile that follows you. After not creating any more projectiles for a duration , all the hovering projectiles are released and fly towards a nearby enemy to deal damage on impact, which can chain to nearby enemies. This skill works similarly to Ember Fusillade in Path of Exile 2.
Kinetic Fusillade | Skill functions and interactions | Kinetic Fusillade will attempt to target a nearby enemy; if no valid targets are in range, the projectiles will fire in the direction the character is facing.
Kinetic Fusillade | Skill functions and interactions | Maximum 12 hovering Projectiles : Sources of additional projectiles (e.g. Volley Support ) will increase the number of hovering projectiles created per skill use, up to the maximum of 12, allowing the cap to be reached faster.
Kinetic Fusillade | Skill functions and interactions | Duration : Stacking sufficient reduced duration will cause hovering projectiles to be fired between attacks, effectively causing each attack to fire projectiles instead of refreshing the duration. Note that sources of additional projectiles will still cause you to generate multiple hovering projectiles at once, thus benefitting (at least in part) from the more damage modifier per projectile in sequence.
Kinetic Fusillade | Item acquisition | Kinetic Fusillade can drop anywhere.

Kinetic Fusillade | Vendor reward | This item can be bought at the listed NPC vendors , after completing the following quests:

Kinetic Instability Support | Description | Kinetic Instability Support is a hybrid support gem that causes wand attacks to trigger Kinetic Flux on kill, a skill also granted by this gem. Kinetic Flux creates an anomaly for a duration , which explodes when enemies are near it, dealing non-projectile area damage.
Kinetic Instability Support | Description | Note: Due to technical limitations, this page's infobox displays gem stats belonging to both Kinetic Instability Support and Kinetic Flux.
Kinetic Instability Support | Skill functions and interactions | Projectile : While most Wand skills are projectile skills, Kinetic Flux is not. Projectile support gems such as Greater Multiple Projectiles Support and projectile damage modifiers such as Point Blank will not affect Kinetic Flux.

Kinetic Rain | Description | Kinetic Rain is a wand attack skill that causes projectiles to rain down on enemies in a targeted area. Each projectile deals area damage on impact and triggers Kinetic Anomaly , a second skill also granted by this gem. Kinetic Anomaly creates an anomaly for a duration , which explodes when enemies are near it, dealing non-projectile area damage.
Kinetic Rain | Description | Note: Due to technical limitations, this page's infobox displays gem stats belonging to both Kinetic Rain and Kinetic Anomaly. These are distinct skills and do not necessarily share the same stats or support gem compatibility.
Kinetic Rain | Skill functions and interactions | Projectile : While Kinetic Rain is a projectile skill, Kinetic Anomaly is not. Projectile support gems such as Greater Multiple Projectiles Support and projectile damage modifiers such as Point Blank will only affect Kinetic Rain, not Kinetic Anomaly.

Kinetic Rain of Impact | Description | It is a transfigured version of Kinetic Rain.
Kinetic Rain of Impact | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version deals has higher damage and damage effectiveness, lower mana cost, and faster attack speed. The projectiles do not create Kinetic Anomalies when they land, and the gem does not have a duration or trigger tag.

Lacerate | Description | Lacerate is a melee attack skill that performs two slashes: one aimed just to the left, then one just to the right. The slashes damage all enemies in a cone in front of the attacker. Each slash does less damage than a single hit from Cleave , but the two slashes overlap in the middle, hitting enemies in front twice. [1]

Leap Slam | Description | Leap Slam is an attack skill that performs a leap to a target area, damaging and knocking back enemies landed on. It is guaranteed to stun enemies at full life.
Leap Slam | Skill functions and interactions | Attack speed can be calculated with this formula: ( 1 + G ) × 1 1 B × ( 1 + L ) +.55 \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}(1+G)\times {1 \over {1 \over B\times (1+L)}+.55} , where B is the base attack speed of your weapon, L is the local attack speed modifier, and G is the total global attack speed modifier applicable to your weapon type. Since weapons already list the locally modified attack speed, you can use this formula instead: ( 1 + G ) × 1 1 W +.55 \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}(1+G)\times {1 \over {1 \over W}+.55} , where W is the weapon's attack speed after local modifiers have been taken into account.
Leap Slam | Strategy | Leap Slam is primarily used as a movement skill. Leap Slam can be used to cross cliffs, chasms, and obstacles. Faster Attacks Support is a good support for this purpose as it will increase the speed of travel.
Leap Slam | Item acquisition | Leap Slam can drop anywhere.

Less Duration Support | Description | Less Duration is a support gem that reduces the duration of any effects produced by the supported skill. It is the counterpart to More Duration Support.
Less Duration Support | Strategy | Less Duration is best used with skills that applies the effect a short time after the attack or cast, such as Earthquake , Lightning Warp , Storm Call , Frost Bomb or Explosive Arrow. It can also be used to reduce the time to change between the stances of Aspect skills such as Aspect of the Cat (if the Aspect is crafted onto an item with sockets), which is very effective for quicker generation of power and frenzy charges via Farrul's Fur or Replica Farrul's Fur. This is a particularly effective strategy for charge generation for the use of Discharge or The Gluttonous Tide.
Less Duration Support | Strategy | For more information, see duration.

Lesser Multiple Projectiles Support | Description | Lesser Multiple Projectiles Support (often referred to as LMP ) is a support gem that causes any linked projectile skills to fire two more projectiles than they otherwise would. For instance, Spark , which normally launches three projectiles, would instead launch five sparks when supported by this gem.
Lesser Multiple Projectiles Support | Skill functions and interactions | The projectiles fan out in an arc, facing the direction that the skill is aimed. The spread of the arc is controlled by the positioning the mouse cursor. Positioning the cursor close to the character will result in a wide arc, while positioning it at the edges of the screen will result in a narrow arc.
Lesser Multiple Projectiles Support | Skill functions and interactions | This gem can be combined with Greater Multiple Projectiles Support for a total of 6 additional Projectiles However, LMP's Supported Skills deal #% less Projectile Damage mod is also multiplicative with GMP's Supported Skills deal #% less Projectile Damage so that the two gems may not suitable to use together. This also applies to Volley Support and Greater Volley Support which have similar mod.

Life Gain on Hit Support | Description | Life Gain on Hit is a support gem. For every hit made using an attack supported by this gem, you will gain the listed amount of life instantly. The life gain is applied for every hit, not every attack - an arrow that pierces three enemies will cause the attacker to gain life for each of the three enemies that is hit.
Life Gain on Hit Support | Description | The effect of this gem will stack with other sources of life gain on hit.
Life Gain on Hit Support | Description | This gem should not be confused with the Life Leech Support gem, which also works with spells and causes the life to be recovered over time.

Lightning Arrow of Electrocution | Description | Lightning Arrow of Electrocution is a bow attack skill that fires an arrow that deals lightning damage. The arrow sticks to the final target it hits, causing them to be struck with a bolt of lightning over time.
Lightning Arrow of Electrocution | Description | It is a transfigured version of Lightning Arrow.
Lightning Arrow of Electrocution | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version does not cause each arrow to hit additional targets, but causes them to stick to the final target it hits. Enemies stuck with an arrow get struck by lightning, taking damage from the skill again. The skill has less damage effectiveness and does not have increased shock effect. Gem quality increases the number of time a target gets struck by lightning instead.
Lightning Arrow of Electrocution | Skill functions and interactions | Projectile Mechanics : Arrows cannot stick in enemies if they continue due to Chain , Pierce , Fork , Split or Return.

Lightning Conduit of the Heavens | Description | It is a transfigured version of Lightning Conduit.
Lightning Conduit of the Heavens | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version has more cost, slower cast time, more damage and damage effectiveness, and less area of effect. It no longer only hits Shocked enemies, is unable to apply shock, deal more damage based on the effect of shock removed, or deal damage in a single area of effect; instead, it targets specific enemies in an area, and enhances shock. It also no longer has a penalty to cooldown when triggered and can be supported by Unleash Support.

Lightning Spire Trap | Description | Lightning Spire Trap is a lightning spell that throws a trap. When activated, the trap spawns a spire that strikes random areas around it for the duration, dealing lightning damage in an area.
Lightning Spire Trap | Skill functions and interactions | Area of Effect : Area of effect modifiers for Lightning Spire Trap do not increase the overall area of the skill, but rather only scale the radius of the impact sites themselves. Because of this interaction, increased area of effect will increase the amount of overlapping damage zones rather than spread them apart.
Lightning Spire Trap | Skill functions and interactions | If lightning spire trap does not have enough room to land in its maximum range, it will land its hits where it can. Therefore small spaces are more likely to cause all hits to hit the same enemy.

Lightning Spire Trap of Zapping | Description | Lightning Spire Trap of Zapping is a spell that hits multiple enemies within a radius of where you targeted to deal lightning damage.
Lightning Spire Trap of Zapping | Description | It is a transfigured version of Lightning Spire Trap.
Lightning Spire Trap of Zapping | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version has less cost, more damage and damage effectiveness, less duration, and strikes fewer areas. It no longer has a cooldown , and no longer gains strike frequency based on trap throwing speed or critical strike chance against Shocked enemies and instead strikes at fixed intervals.
Lightning Spire Trap of Zapping | Item acquisition | Lightning Spire Trap of Zapping has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

Lightning Strike | Infobox | Attack , Projectile , Melee , Strike , Lightning Level: (1-20) Cost: (6-10) Mana Attack Damage: (198-456)% of base Effectiveness of Added Damage: (198-456)%. Requires Level 12. Infuses your melee weapon with electrical energies as you swing. In addition to converting some of your physical damage to lightning damage, the stored energy is released from the weapon as projectiles as you strike, flying out to hit farther-away enemies. The projectiles cannot miss if the melee attack hit a target. 50% of Physical Damage Converted to Lightning Damage Fires 3 Projectiles Projectiles deal 50% less Damage with Hits and Ailments Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Fires +(0.05-1) Projectiles. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 12
Lightning Strike | Description | Lightning Strike is a melee strike attack skill that converts physical damage to lightning damage. It has a secondary effect where it launches projectiles that deal damage based on the player character's weapon damage with each swing.
Lightning Strike | Skill functions and interactions | An attack with Lightning Strike consists of two parts:
Lightning Strike | Skill functions and interactions | When the melee attack is made, a default of three lightning projectiles will shoot out in a ~85 degree arc, traveling in a straight line until they strike an enemy. Projectiles will do full weapon damage, of which 50% of physical damage is converted to lightning damage (the bolts benefit from added damage, e.g. as from aura's), against any target struck. Projectiles created by a melee strike that hit an enemy do not immediately hit that enemy (though one could through return ). Linking a Lesser Multiple Projectiles Support or Greater Multiple Projectiles Support support gem will multiplicatively reduce the damage of each projectile and spread further projectiles out within the 85 degree arc.
Lightning Strike | Skill functions and interactions | Cannot Miss: This applies only to the projectiles launched after you initiated the strike. Swinging in enemies' general direction will result in projectiles with a hit/miss chance equal to that of the skill itself. Projectiles that miss will be dissipated without doing damage.
Lightning Strike | Skill functions and interactions | Damage conversion : Like all converted damage, the lightning portion of Lightning Strike's damage will be modified by both lightning damage and physical damage increases. Furthermore, the damage done by the lightning bolts is considered projectile damage (instead of melee), therefore any Strength bonus does not apply.
Lightning Strike | Skill functions and interactions | It is important to note that despite the confusing wording of this skill, the projectile portion of this attack in no way scales off the melee portion of the attack - in essence, the Lightning Strike skill does two completely separate things simultaneously, a melee attack (which deals x% of the base damage, and of which 50% of the physical damage is converted to lightning damage), and a ranged attack (which shoots projectiles dealing full weapon damage, and of which 50% of the physical damage is converted to lightning damage).
Lightning Strike | Skill functions and interactions | This means that increasing the melee portion of the hit through melee-exclusive means such as Melee Physical Damage Support will not actually increase the projectile damage; the projectile damage is not "converted melee damage", a common misconception. Do not be deceived by the tooltip DPS of this skill which will change if you apply a melee-exclusive modifier; the tooltip DPS only shows the DPS of the melee portion of the attack and things that modify that, but not the projectile part of the attack.
Lightning Strike | Skill functions and interactions | That said, passive points that grant Increased damage with one-handed melee weapons for example will in fact affect the projectile attack as well if one is using a one-handed weapon, since it is still an attack that is dealt with a melee weapon (though the damage you're dealing is not actually melee damage). However, Increased melee damage with one-handed weapons would only apply to the melee portion of the attack, as it increases melee damage.
Lightning Strike | Skill functions and interactions | Any flat elemental damage gains from gear, auras, weapons or any other source will apply to both the projectile and the melee portion of the attack.
Lightning Strike | Item acquisition | Lightning Strike can drop anywhere.

Lightning Tendrils | Infobox | Critical , Spell , AoE , Lightning , Channelling Level: (1-20) Cost: (1-5) Mana Cast Time: 0.23 sec Critical Strike Chance: 6.00% Effectiveness of Added Damage: (70-130)% AoE Radius: 22. Deals (1-75) to (6-1420) Lightning Damage Releases a Stronger Pulse every 3 Pulses Stronger Pulses always Critically Strike Base radius is (2.4-3.1) metres Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: +(2-40)% to Critical Strike Multiplier. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 5
Lightning Tendrils | Description | Lightning Tendrils is a channelled spell that releases surges of lightning damage in quick succession in an arc in front of you, with every third surge always dealing a critical strike.
Lightning Tendrils | Skill functions and interactions | Area of Effect : Lightning Tendrils deals Area Damage in a semicircular arc with a base radius of 24 units. Modifiers such as increased Area of Effect will affect the radius of the cone but not the angle.
Lightning Tendrils | Skill functions and interactions | Trigger Gems : Similarly to Incinerate , Flameblast and other channelled skills, Lightning Tendrils cannot be supported by trigger gems like Cast when Damage Taken Support or Cast On Critical Strike Support. It also cannot be supported by Blastchain Mine Support or Trap Support. The only trigger gems which will interact with it is Cast while Channelling Support.
Lightning Tendrils | Skill functions and interactions | Stronger pulses : Every third pulse is guaranteed to deal a critical strike. Effects which reduce or limit maximum critical strike chance, such as Marylene's Fallacy and The Winds of Fate , will not affect its chance to deal a critical strike on the 3rd pulse. However, effects that entirely prevent crits from happening (such as Resolute Technique ) will prevent the 3rd pulse from critting.

Projectiles fired will pierce targets and chain off of terrain. It also has higher base damage, higher added damage effectiveness and fires fewer projectiles than default Lightning Trap.

Lightning Warp | Infobox | Spell , AoE , Duration , Movement , Lightning , Travel Level: (1-20) Cost: (9-26) Mana Cast Time: 0.70 sec Critical Strike Chance: 5.00% Effectiveness of Added Damage: 90% AoE Radius: 16. Requires Level 10. Waits for a duration before teleporting to a targeted destination, with the duration based on the distance and your movement speed. When the teleport occurs, lightning damage is dealt to the area around both where the player was and where they teleported to. Casting again will queue up multiple teleportations to occur in sequence. Deals (1-51) to (19-965) Lightning Damage +(0-0.4) metres to radius (30-49)% reduced Duration Can queue up to 50 uses Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: (1-20)% increased Cast Speed. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 10
Lightning Warp | Description | Lightning Warp is a movement travel spell that causes the player character (or totem when supported by Spell Totem ) to teleport to a location after a delay. When the teleport occurs, lightning damage is dealt in a small area around both the previous location and the new location.
Lightning Warp | Strategy | Lightning Warp is primarily used as a movement skill. To improve Lightning Warp's effectiveness as a movement skill, it can be supported with Less Duration Support , Faster Casting Support , and Swift Affliction Support. If only one support gem can be used, Less Duration Support will generally result in faster travel. Taking increased Skill Effect Duration passives will render Lightning Warp much less useful as a movement skill, in which case alternatives such as Flame Dash , Leap Slam , or Whirling Blades can be considered.
Lightning Warp | Strategy | The skill's inherent reduced duration (50% at level 21) is additive with other sources of reduced duration such as Timeclasp , Warped Timepiece , or Window of Opportunity and its adjacent small passives. With 100% reduced duration only the 50ms added delay will apply, allowing the skill to be cast or triggered up to 20 times per second without causing movement stuttering.
Lightning Warp | Strategy | Compared with Leap Slam, Lightning Warp has the advantages of not requiring any particular weapon, potentially higher speed, and slightly longer range. However, it costs more mana and requires three support gems to be fully effective. Compared with Flame Dash, Lightning Warp has the advantage of being able to be cast as many times as possible, but costs slightly more mana and has a noticeable delay.
Lightning Warp | Item acquisition | Lightning Warp can drop anywhere.

Living Lightning Support | Description | Living Lightning Support is a support gem that causes lightning damage hits by the supported skill to trigger Summon Living Lightning , which summons a Living Lightning minion for a duration. Living Lightning minions perform a limited number of chaining melee attacks against nearby enemies.
Living Lightning Support | Skill functions and interactions | Minion : Living Lightning minions are not targeted by enemies and cannot impede enemies' movement. They cannot be damaged, which allows them to use Damage on Full Life Support effectively. They are also very fast and aggressive, which makes them a good for Infernal Legion Support. Multistrike Support can be used to effectively triple their attack limit as attack repeats do not count towards the count.

Locus Mine Support | Description | Locus Mine Support is a support gem that modifies the supported bow or wand attack skill so that instead of casting it directly, the player throws a mine which uses the skill when the player detonates them. Mines are thrown in an arc shape, and fire projectiles toward the player.
Locus Mine Support | Skill functions and interactions | This support gem cannot support channelling skills like Scourge Arrow or Snipe or skills supported by Arrow Nova Support.
Locus Mine Support | Skill functions and interactions | By default, Locus Mine throws three mines per skill use.
Locus Mine Support | Skill functions and interactions | Locus Mine throws mines out in an arc towards your cursor, and fires projectiles at or towards the player character 's location. Returning projectiles will move away from the player. Nimis can be used to circumvent the aiming behaviour. When combined with a trap , the mine will instead throw a trap at your location that aims normally when firing projectiles. Ballistas ignore the aiming behaviour entirely. Power Siphon also ignores the aiming behaviour due to its mechanics specifically firing at each enemy.
Locus Mine Support | Skill functions and interactions | Attacks used supported by Locus Mine Support will always fire at least 1 projectile.

Manaforged Arrows Support | Infobox | Bow , Support , Trigger Icon: U Level: (1-20) Cost & Reservation Multiplier: 200% Cooldown Time: 0.50 sec Can Store 1 Use(s). Requires Level 8. Supports bow attack skills, causing them to trigger when you've spent enough mana in total on other bow attacks. Cannot modify the skills of minions. Cannot support totems, traps, mines, Vaal skills, or channelling skills. Supported Skills deal (40-31)% less Damage Supported Skills deal 1% more Damage with Hits and Ailments per 1 Mana Cost Supported Skills Trigger once total Mana Spent on other Bow Attacks exceeds 300% of their Mana Cost Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Supported Skills have (0.5-10)% increased Cooldown Recovery Rate. This is a Support Gem. It does not grant a bonus to your character, but to skills in sockets connected to it. Place into an item socket connected to a socket containing the Skill Gem you wish to augment. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 8
Manaforged Arrows Support | Description | Manaforged Arrows is a support gem that triggers a linked bow attack skill when the player spends enough mana on other bow attacks.
Manaforged Arrows Support | Skill functions and interactions | Supported Skills Trigger once total Mana Spent on other Bow Attacks exceeds 300% of their Mana Cost : tracks cumulative mana spent on all bow attack skills, including ballista totem skills, traps , and mines that use bow skills. "Their" refers to "Supported Skills", not "other Bow Attacks". That is, the threshold is 300% of the mana cost of each supported skill, not 300% of the mana cost of the other bow attacks. Example: Tornado Shot is linked to Manaforged Arrows Support and costs 14 mana. Those 14 mana times 300% are 42 mana, which is the threshold. The other bow skill you are using is barrage (not supported by manaforged arrows support) and costs 6 mana. After attacking for the 7th time (7*6=42) you have reached the threshold and Tornado Shot will trigger.
Manaforged Arrows Support | Skill functions and interactions | Manaforged Arrows can be linked with Cast On Critical Strike Support to trigger spells.
Manaforged Arrows Support | Skill functions and interactions | Manaforged Arrows can also be linked with Lifetap Support to trigger linked bow skills on every usage of another bow skill (assuming that the cooldown is ready) because its mana cost will be zero, so spending any mana will always exceed the mana cost of linked bow skills. Reducing the mana cost of the triggered skill to zero by other means also works. (However, the mana cost of the other bow skill must still be nonzero for this to work.)

Meat Shield Support | Description | Meat Shield Support is a support gem for minion skills. It changes the minions' behaviour to be defensive , causing them to stay close to their owner and prioritise attacking close enemies by lowering their aggro radius. It also causes the supported minions to take less damage, grants increased movement speed , a chance to taunt on hit, and grants them more damage against enemies near you.
Meat Shield Support | Skill functions and interactions | Minions are Defensive: If a minion is both Aggressive and Defensive , the effects will cancel each other out. This can be beneficial in situations where the stats provided by Meat Shield Support and Feeding Frenzy Support are desired, but a change to the minion's normal aggro range isn't.

Melee Splash Support | Description | Melee Splash Support is a support gem that can be linked with a melee strike skill gem to add a splash effect to the attack, dealing damage around each enemy struck by it.
Melee Splash Support | Skill functions and interactions | Melee Splash only supports strike skills and has no interaction with other types of skills like slam skills. It causes the skill to deal damage around the strike target in a greater area than strike skill's default splashing behaviour.
Melee Splash Support | Skill functions and interactions | The splash damage is considered area melee damage and is not considered as part of the strike itself. The splash is affected by modifiers to area of effect and area damage , but will not affect the strike's damage if it was not originally dealing area damage. [1] The area of effect created by Melee Splash has a base radius of 14 units. [2] Splash can apply on-hit effects; for example, a hit that would knockback the target would also knockback enemies hit by the splash damage.
Melee Splash Support | Skill functions and interactions | The less damage multiplier for surrounding targets only applies to the splash effect and not other mechanics that hit enemies such as Boneshatter 's pulses. [3]

Mirror Arrow | Infobox | Attack , Minion , Duration , Movement , Travel , Bow Level: (1-20) Cost: (14-20) Mana Cooldown Time: 3.00 sec Can Store 1 Use(s). Requires Level 10. Fires an arrow at the target destination. When the arrow lands, a clone is summoned. The clone is a minion that uses your bow and quiver. Base duration is 3.00 seconds (0-47)% increased Cooldown Recovery Rate Minions deal (0-57)% more Damage Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: (2-40)% increased Cooldown Recovery Rate. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 10
Mirror Arrow | Description | Mirror Arrow is a bow attack skill that fires an arrow, dealing damage and summoning a clone when it reaches its destination. The clone is a minion that uses the player's bow and quiver.
Mirror Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | The skill happens in three stages:
Mirror Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | The length of the first stage is affected by anything that affects regular attacks - weapon base speed, attack speed modifiers etc. The length of the second stage is affected both by bonuses to projectile speed and by global bonuses to attack speed, but not by the weapon's base speed. [2] [3] The delay is unaffected by distance travelled.
Mirror Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | The delay of the first two stages can be reduced by linking Faster Attacks Support and Faster Projectiles Support ; for situational movement uses, it can be socketed into a Quill Rain on swap.
Mirror Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | The minion generated by this skill will spawn with the current % of total life as the player; if the player is on Low Life (e.g. via Petrified Blood ), the minion will also spawn at Low Life, causing it to instantly explode with Minion Instability. The minion uses a bow Default Attack supported by all applicable support gems linked to Mirror Arrow. It is unable to use other skills or move around.
Mirror Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | This skill can be used multiple times in a row by getting additional uses via Second Wind Support.
Mirror Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | Clones copy the visuals of all buffs , debuffs, and similar effects on the summoner at the time they are created. This is intended to obscure whether the player used Blink Arrow or Mirror Arrow in PvP and has no effect on minions' actual stats. [4]

Damage other than the chosen element cannot be dealt, meaning the minions' arrows can never deal chaos damage or Poison. [1] The skill does not choose the same element twice in a row unless choices are limited by Combat Focus jewels. [2]

Molten Shell | Monster versions | Monsters may use Molten Shell (with alternative skill effects) if the monster has the mod of the Barrier , or the legacy archnemesis modifier Magma Barrier which grants the monster multiple skills and effects. Goatman Shaman also uses Molten Shell and different skill visuals to the player version. Other example is General Gravicius and his map boss variants.
Molten Strike | Infobox | Attack , Projectile , AoE , Melee , Strike , Fire Level: (1-20) Cost: (6-10) Mana Attack Damage: (126-329)% of base Effectiveness of Added Damage: (126-329)% Projectile Speed: 750. Infuses your melee weapon with molten energies to attack with physical and fire damage. This attack causes balls of molten magma to launch forth from the enemies you hit, divided amongst all enemies hit by the strike. These will deal area attack damage to enemies where they land. Fires 4 Projectiles 60% of Physical Damage Converted to Fire Damage Projectiles deal 60% less Damage with Hits and Ailments Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Projectiles deal (0.5-10)% more Damage with Hits and Ailments. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 5
Molten Strike | Description | Molten Strike is a strike ( attack ) skill that strikes an enemy, with some of the physical damage converted to fire damage. Each strike creates multiple molten ball projectiles that land to deal damage in a small area.
Molten Strike | Skill functions | An attack with Molten Strike consists of two parts:
Molten Strike | Skill functions | Modifiers that require the keywords Melee , Projectile , or AoE only apply to the appropriate portions of the attack. For example, supporting Molten Strike with Melee Physical Damage Support will increase the damage of the melee hit but not the projectiles. That said, passive points that grant Increased damage with one-handed melee weapons will affect the projectile attack as well if one is using a one-handed weapon, since it is still an attack that is dealt with a melee weapon (though the damage you're dealing is not actually melee damage).
Molten Strike | Skill functions | Magma projectiles: On a successful hit Molten Strike will launch a number of projectiles a variable distance in different directions before each lands simultaneously, hitting in a circular area around each projectile. Projectiles will travel a random distance between 2 and 25 units by default, with projectiles from the same attack travelling different distances. Modifiers to both projectile speed and area of effect will alter the distance projectiles can travel. [1] The direction of each projectile is also chosen randomly along a 270 degree (3/4 circle) arc, [2] with the center of the blank zone pointed at the attacking character. Upon landing each projectile explodes with a hit radius of 9 [3] dealing (55 to 92.5)% of full weapon damage, of which 60% of physical is converted to fire damage.
Molten Strike | Skill functions | Projectile overlap: It is possible for more than one projectile from this skill to hit a single enemy, including the enemy that was already hit by the melee attack. The likelihood of hitting the initial target is reduced due to the ability of each projectile to travel much further than its hit radius. Molten Strike's projectiles tend to be clumped towards the centre and away from the edges. [4] This is because unlike other skills such as Firestorm which sample uniformly across their potential landing area, Molten Strike's projectile landing positions are determined by choosing their direction and distance from two separate uniform distributions.
Molten Strike | Skill functions | Damage conversion : Like all converted damage , the fire portion of Molten Strike's damage will be modified by both fire damage and physical damage increases.
Molten Strike | Skill functions | Tooltip damage: Damage per hit and DPS displayed on the skill tooltip show only the melee portion of the attack, with that of projectiles dependent on the average number of hits expected from the skill. An estimate to the area DPS and total single target DPS of the skill can be obtained using this calculator.
Molten Strike | Skill interactions | Dual Wielding : Dual wielding attacks with Molten Strike will alternate the weapon being swung.
Molten Strike | Skill interactions | Gain on hit : The stated amount on hit will be restored for both the initial hit and for each projectile that strikes an enemy.
Molten Strike | Skill interactions | Iron Grip : This will allow Strength's melee physical damage bonus to apply to the projectile portion of the attack.
Molten Strike | Skill interactions | Area of effect : Increases and reductions to AoE change both the explosion area of individual projectiles and the area they are spread over, resulting in similar degree of coverage over a larger or smaller area. AoE bonuses only affect the outer radius; the inner radius is fixed.
Molten Strike | Skill interactions | Projectile Count: Supporting Molten Strike with Lesser Multiple Projectiles Support or Greater Multiple Projectiles Support will increase the number of projectiles fired while reducing their damage, but will not affect the initial melee hit in any way.
Molten Strike | Skill interactions | Piercing, forking and chaining projectiles : Molten Strike projectiles cannot chain, pierce or fork by default, since they are ground-targeted. The threshold jewel Wildfire allows the projectiles to chain, which can be then be further enhanced with support gems and items. Chained projectiles continue in the same direction, but can change their speed between impacts.
Molten Strike | Skill interactions | Projectile Speed: Reducing projectile speed (such as with Slower Projectiles Support ) will reduce the distance projectiles travel without reducing their AoE, making them more likely to hit the initial melee target.
Molten Strike | Skill interactions | Point Blank : Projectiles are affected by Point Blank even though the melee attack is not. [5] As Point Blank measures from the origin point of the projectile, the damage multiplier received is proportional to the distance the projectile has travelled from the melee target's location (where the projectile was created).
Molten Strike | Skill interactions | Vicious Projectiles Support : The Projectiles will receive the damage multiplier, however Molten Strike attack speed will not be lessened because it is a Melee Attack that triggers a Projectile Attack, and is not a Projectile Attack itself.

Molten Strike of the Zenith | Description | Molten Strike of the Zenith is a strike ( attack ) skill that strikes an enemy with a two-handed weapon, with some of the physical damage converted to fire damage. Each strike creates multiple molten ball projectiles that land to deal damage in a small area. Every fifth strike creates additional, stronger projectiles.
Molten Strike of the Zenith | Description | It is a transfigured version of Molten Strike.
Molten Strike of the Zenith | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version has less damage effectiveness, slower attack time, more cost, and a higher damage penalty to projectiles. However, it now adds additional projectiles and significantly more damage to every 5th attack, and requires a two-handed melee weapon.
Molten Strike of the Zenith | Skill functions and interactions | Every fifth Attack : Repeats from Multistrike Support and Awakened Multistrike Support do not count towards the number of attacks.
Molten Strike of the Zenith | Item acquisition | Molten Strike of the Zenith has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

More Duration Support | Description | More Duration is a support gem. It modifies the duration of any inherent duration effects of the supported skill.
More Duration Support | Skill functions and interactions | For example this gem will increase:
More Duration Support | Skill functions and interactions | When linked to a remote skill or a skill that creates a minion , this gem will also increase the duration of skills used by the created minion or ally. For instance, if a Goatman Shaman in The Climb is raised as a spectre , the duration of the goatman's Molten Shell will be increased.
More Duration Support | Skill functions and interactions | Ailments do not gain more duration unless otherwise specified. Viper Strike and Puncture explicitly increase the duration of the ailment it applies.
More Duration Support | Skill functions and interactions | This gem also does not increase the duration of Endurance, Frenzy, or Power Charges. These durations can be increased with passive skills and equipment.
More Duration Support | Skill functions and interactions | For a list of skills that have a duration that can be affected, see Category:Duration skills.

Multiple Totems Support | Description | Multiple Totems Support is a support gem that causes the supported totem skill to summon an additional totem on cast and increases the maximum amount of totems for that skill, but lessens the damage dealt with each totem.
Multiple Totems Support | Skill functions and interactions | Multiple Totems Support can support inherent totem skills such as Holy Flame Totem , skills supported by Ballista Totem Support or Spell Totem Support , and minions who can summon totems (e.g. Raise Spectre ).
Multiple Totems Support | Skill functions and interactions | While it can support totem skills with cooldowns, namely Decoy Totem , it cannot bypass cooldowns, so only one totem will be summoned at a time. Second Wind Support grants an additional use of Decoy Totem, so in conjunction with Multiple Totems Support it allows summoning two Decoy Totems at once.
Multiple Totems Support | Damage Scaling | The total possible damage provided by multiple totems support with all totems placed is dependent on how many total totems the character has.
Multiple Totems Support | Damage Scaling | The formula for calculating a level 21 multiple totems support gem is (base totems without the gem + 2) x (.8) / (base totems without the gem). Some examples include:
Multiple Totems Support | Attack and Cast Speed Scaling | Certain skills, like Wither are unaffected by the downside of less damage, while benefiting from more totems casting. The formula calculates how fast the withered debuff is applied when all totems are placed.
Multiple Totems Support | Attack and Cast Speed Scaling | The formula for calculating any level of multiple totems support gem is (base totems without the gem + 2) / (base totems without the gem). Some examples include:

Multistrike Support | Infobox | Attack , Melee , Support Icon: 3 Level: (1-20) Cost & Reservation Multiplier: 150%. Requires Level 38. Supports melee attack skills, making them repeat twice when used, targeting a random enemy each time. Cannot support Vaal skills, channelling skills, travel skills, retaliation skills or triggered skills. Supported Skills Repeat 2 additional times Supported Skills have (35-44)% more Melee Attack Speed Supported Skills deal (30-20)% less Attack Damage First Repeat of Supported Skills deals 22% more Damage Second Repeat of Supported Skills deals 44% more Damage Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Supported Skills deal (0.5-10)% increased Melee Damage. This is a Support Gem. It does not grant a bonus to your character, but to skills in sockets connected to it. Place into an item socket connected to a socket containing the Skill Gem you wish to augment. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 38
Multistrike Support | Description | Multistrike is a support gem. Melee attack skills that are linked to Multistrike will repeat themselves twice after the initial attack (e.g.: linking Double Strike with it will make it hit six times instead of just two per use of the skill), [1] automatically selecting valid targets within range for the successive attacks. Each repeat deals more damage.
Multistrike Support | Skill functions and interactions | Because attacks that repeat are still the same action, the attack and its repeats share the same critical strike roll, are usually performed with the same weapon while dual wielding , and modifiers like when you Attack / use an attack skill / the first time this Attack Hits an Enemy occur only once. [2] [3]
Multistrike Support | Skill functions and interactions | When Multistrike is linked to attacks that involve player movement, such as Flicker Strike , the skill will first travel to the destination selected and then will travel to any enemy within range for the next two attacks. If no enemy is available, the attacks will be targeted at or near player location.
Multistrike Support | Skill functions and interactions | First/Second Repeat of Supported Skills deals #% more Damage will scale ailments and damage over time.
Multistrike Support | Skill functions and interactions | For the spell counterpart, see Spell Echo Support.
Multistrike Support | Repeated attacks interactions and attack time | Generally, attacks will grant a skill gem-specific benefit or require an extra cost once per set of attacks, rather than once per repeat.
Multistrike Support | Repeated attacks interactions and attack time | Since Multistrike repeats your attack, the time between uses of the attack is equal to three times your attack time. (Or four times in the case of Awakened Multistrike Support.) For example if you have an attack time of 0.22s displayed on the attack's tooltip, you will only use the attack once every 0.66s. So skills like Boneshatter will prefer to not use Multistrike if they wish to build up stacks quickly.
Multistrike Support | Item acquisition | Multistrike Support can drop anywhere.

Orb of Storms | Description | Orb of Storms is a lightning spell that creates a stationary orb of lightning. Beams from the orb strike nearby enemies every few seconds, as well as when the user uses a Lightning- tagged skill within the orb's radius.
Orb of Storms | Skill functions and interactions | Strikes : Lightning strikes occur on an interval, as well as whenever a lightning-tagged skill is used near the orb; whether this skill use results in attacking, casting, or throwing/summoning a proxy does not matter. This secondary method of channelling skills only cause lightning to strike every 0.5 seconds.
Orb of Storms | Skill functions and interactions | Area of effect : The orb is always summoned near the player's location. Its beams will only target enemies within its area of effect, modified by area of effect modifiers. However, damage dealt by Orb of Storms is not area damage.
Orb of Storms | Skill functions and interactions | Chaining : Orb of Storms does not chain by default, but can be granted chains by e.g. Chain Support. Beams can chain to enemies beyond the primary area of effect.
Orb of Storms | Skill functions and interactions | Critical strikes : Unlike most skills, Orb of Storms performs a new critical strike roll for each damaging event.
Orb of Storms | Skill functions and interactions | Duration : The orb does not have a skill effect duration. It will disperse after a certain number of lightning strikes instead.
Orb of Storms | Item acquisition | Orb of Storms can drop anywhere.

When the brand is detached by any means, enemies with Energy are dealt damage.
Penance Brand of Conduction | Description | It is a transfigured version of Penance Brand.
Penance Brand of Conduction | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version has a short, limited Attached duration. It does not apply stacks of energy nor does it explode. It instead adds Energy to nearby enemies upon activation and deals damage when the brand is Detached. Compared to the original, it has lower damage and damage effectiveness and a slower activation frequency, but deals more damage with Hits to the branded enemy and has no penalty to damage with Ailments.
Penance Brand of Conduction | Item acquisition | Penance Brand of Conduction has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

Penance Brand of Dissipation | Description | Penance Brand of Dissipation is a spell that creates a brand at the target location, which when attached to an enemy adds Energy to the branded enemy and causes a pulse on activation, dealing damage to the branded enemy and nearby enemies.
Penance Brand of Dissipation | Description | It is a transfigured version of Penance Brand.
Penance Brand of Dissipation | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version has a limited Attached duration. It repeatedly pulses on activation, increasing in size and damage with the amount of energy on the target. Compared to the original, it has lower damage and damage effectiveness and slower activation frequency, but more damage with Hits and Ailments to the branded enemy, scaling with the amount of energy added. The brand does not explode on reaching maximum energy, nor is it consumed at maximum energy.

Pestilent Strike | Description | Pestilent Strike is a melee strike skill that deals chaos damage and applies poison. If the poisoned enemy dies while poisoned it will inflict other nearby enemies with another chaos damage over time debuff.
Pestilent Strike | Skill functions and interactions | The base debuff radius is 2.7m. [1]
Pestilent Strike | Skill functions and interactions | Enemies killed while under the effect of Pestilent Strike’s signature debuff will leave no corpse. This can be useful for bypassing some monsters’ on-death effects. It does not prevent the poison spreading effect of Master Toxicist or Bino's Kitchen Knife.
Pestilent Strike | Skill functions and interactions | Multiple debuffs can be on the same monster, but only the highest damage debuff will cause damage at any given time.
Pestilent Strike | Skill functions and interactions | The debuff requires an enemy to die with poison on it near the enemy you want the debuff on. For solo enemy fights, Vaal Breach can spawn enemies to spread the debuff.
Pestilent Strike | Skill functions and interactions | The debuff takes half of the poison damage left on the monster when it died and deals it incrementally over 1 second. The gem's advanced description specifies (The Expected Damage of Poison is its Damage per second multiplied by its Duration).
Pestilent Strike | Scaling | Pestilent Strikes debuff can scale poison damage in unique ways that would not be beneficial to most other poison attacks. Some examples:

Petrified Blood | Description | Petrified Blood is a spell that reserves mana to protect the character's lower half of their maximum life by causing a portion of the immediate life loss from hits to be lost over time instead. At higher skill levels, the amount of life loss over time is reduced, even though the percentage of immediate life loss prevented remains constant. As a drawback, the character cannot recover life above low life by any means except with flasks and Temporal Rift. Skills also gain an additional life cost while the character is above low life.
