Phase Run | Description | Phase Run is a skill gem that applies a buff to the character that grants increased movement speed , increased stealth , and Phasing. Using any skill while Phase Run is active will replace the primary buff with a secondary buff that grants more physical melee damage for a shorter duration. Phase Run consumes Frenzy Charges to increase its duration.
Phase Run | Skill functions and interactions | Duration : Modifiers to skill effect duration or buff duration affect the duration of both the primary and secondary buffs. The secondary buff only begins once the primary buff expires or is removed early through using a skill. Using Phase Run again will reset the primary buff.
Phase Run | Skill functions and interactions | Triggered skills : Triggered skills do not count as "performed" and therefore do not cause Phase Run's primary buff to be replaced.
Phase Run | Skill functions and interactions | Damage buff - The more Melee Physical Damage modifier applies for the secondary duration after the character uses a skill. As hit damage is not snapshotted , the buff may expire before hit damage is calculated, though strike skills targeting nearby enemies will usually benefit from the buff regardless of attack time. [1]

Pierce Support | Description | Pierce Support is a support gem that grants piercing to projectiles created by skills linked to this gem.
Pierce Support | Skill functions and interactions | Some projectiles, such as those from Freezing Pulse and Holy Flame Totem , already have infinite Pierces , so increased pierces will have no effect. These projectiles will benefit from the more damage multiplier from this support gem (as well as the increased projectile damage from a quality gem). Ice Spear also has infinite Pierces in its armed form, but before arming, it doesn't have the pierce effect - this gem will give the projectile's first form target piercing.
Pierce Support | Skill functions and interactions | The Pierce from this support gem is additive with other Pierces modifiers from other sources such as the Piercing Shots notable passive.

Plague Bearer | Infobox | Spell , Chaos , AoE Level: (1-20) Cost: (6-13) Mana Cooldown Time: 0.50 sec Can Store 1 Use(s) Cast Time: Instant. Requires Level 24. Gain an Incubating buff, adding to its Plague Value as you inflict poisons. Use the skill again to begin Infecting, dealing chaos damage over time to nearby enemies until the Plague Value is depleted. Your damage modifiers don't apply to this skill's damage. Using the skill again alternates between Incubating and Infecting. +(0-0.3) metres to radius 20% less Damage with Poison while Incubating and not at Maximum Plague Value When you inflict Poison while Incubating, adds 40% of the Expected Poison Damage to Plague Value Maximum Plague Value is (4000-1000000) While Infecting, deals Chaos Damage per second equal to 12% of the Plague Value when Infecting began, and loses Plague Value at the same rate Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Plague Bearer deals Damage based on an additional +(0.2-4)% of Plague Value. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 24
Plague Bearer | Description | Plague Bearer is a chaos spell skill that grants the user a buff that can be toggled between Incubating or Infecting. While Incubating, the buff causes the user to deal less poison damage, and charges Plague Value based on inflicted poisons. While Infecting, the buff depletes its Plague Value to deal chaos damage over time to nearby enemies.
Plague Bearer | Skill functions and interactions | The gem's advanced description specifies (The Expected Damage of Poison is its Damage per second multiplied by its Duration). This is based on the poison's base DPS and duration, and is not affected by the defender's stats such as resistances. Poisoning through The Golden Rule 's reflection also charges Plague Bearer, effectively doubling Plague Value gain.
Plague Bearer | Skill functions and interactions | Plague Bearer inflicts its Infecting debuff in an area around the player, similar to Righteous Fire. This damage cannot be further scaled by the user's offensive stats. Its damage is mitigated normally by the enemy through e.g. resistance and damage taken modifiers (including Delirium [1] [2] ), and accordingly can be scaled through debuffs like Despair , Shock , or Withered. Its damage is not considered poison.
Plague Bearer | Skill functions and interactions | By default, the Plaguebearer Infecting stage lasts 8.33 seconds (12%/second). With 20% quality, it instead lasts 6.25 seconds (16%/second). Combined with the legacy Eternal Labyrinth helmet enchantment, it lasts 4.76 seconds (21%/second).
Plague Bearer | Skill functions and interactions | Since the damage from Plague Bearer can't be directly increased, increasing its gem level or quality becomes the only ways to increase its base DPS (gem level increases the maximum plague value stored, while gem quality increases the rate the plague value is released). Empower Support , Enhance Support , Increased Area of Effect Support are the highest-priority support gems for Plague Bearer.
Plague Bearer | Skill functions and interactions | Plague Bearer cannot currently be triggered , except by Automation.
Plague Bearer | Item acquisition | Plague Bearer can drop anywhere.

Poisonous Concoction | Description | A Poisonous Flask is visibly attached to your Character's Belt when the skill is socketed in one of your equipped items.
Poisonous Concoction | Skill Functions and Interactions | The explosions from projectiles can overlap. Given sufficient investment into increased area of effect , many explosions can hit the same target, making Greater Volley Support and Greater Multiple Projectiles Support effective ways to scale Poison. Projectile damage modifiers will only scale the explosion's hit damage and will not affect poison.
Poisonous Concoction | Skill Functions and Interactions | The projectiles from Poisonous Concoction are ground-targeted, cannot collide with enemies, and cannot split , pierce , fork , chain or return ; only the explosion hits and does damage.
Poisonous Concoction | Skill Functions and Interactions | The explosion will visually turn red if the skill consumed Life Flask charges on that attack.
Poisonous Concoction | Skill Functions and Interactions | AoE breakpoints : The base radius of the skill is 18. 13% increased AoE gives the skill a radius of 19, 26% increased AoE gives a radius of 20, and 37% increased AoE gives a radius of 21. Testing with 9 total projectiles showed that most projectiles will overlap when the radius is 20 (average 8.8 out of 9), and all projectiles will overlap when the radius is 21 [1]
Poisonous Concoction | Skill Functions and Interactions | Added Chaos Damage equal to #% of Flask's Recovery Amount if Charges were consumed : This skill will add chaos damage if a life flask's charges were consumed. The added damage is only based on the value of "Recovers # Life over x seconds" on the life flask itself, the one that can be seen at the top of the life flask when moused over. [2] To maximize the added chaos damage from consumed Life Flask charges, a maximum Quality Divine Life Flask with the Saturated Saturated Prefix (65-70)% increased Amount Recovered 33% reduced Recovery rate prefix is ideal. For reference, see the list of life flask modifiers.
Poisonous Concoction | Skill Functions and Interactions | Flask charges consumption : According to some tests made by User:RoOoBerT on 2024-07-17 (3.24 Necropolis ), with Poisonous Concoction level 12 gem, the skill removes 1 flask charge per projectile from the equipped life flask with the highest number of current charges. Hybrid flasks are candidates as they count both as life flasks and mana flasks. Unique life or hybrid flasks such as Blood of the Karui are also valid choices. Modifiers granting #% reduced Flask Charges used affect the charges consumed by the skill.
Poisonous Concoction | Skill Functions and Interactions | The Harvest crafting option to enchant a flask with +# to Maximum Charges. -1 to this value when used can be used to store extra flask charges.
Poisonous Concoction | Item acquisition | Poisonous Concoction can drop anywhere.

Poisonous Concoction of Bouncing | Infobox | Attack , AoE , Chaos , Projectile , Chaining Level: (1-20) Cost: (6-10) Mana. Requires Level 12. Throws a single bottle that releases a burst when it hits the ground, dealing unarmed attack damage in an area with a chance to poison. The skill chains, bouncing randomly to deal damage multiple times. Can consume charges from a Life flask to add further damage. Requires an empty main hand, and no off-hand weapon. (22-537) to (33-806) Added Chaos Damage Modifiers to number of Projectiles instead apply to the number of times this Skill Chains Chains +2 Times 40% chance to Poison on Hit +6% to Critical Strike Chance Base radius is 1.8 metres Projectiles can Chain when impacting the ground Projectiles Chain in random directions Consumes 8 Charges per Projectile Fired from 1 Life Flask, if possible Added Chaos Damage equal to (6-18)% of Flask's Recovery Amount if Charges were consumed from a Life Flask Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: (1-20)% chance to inflict Withered for 2 seconds on Hit. Poisonous Concoction of Bouncing | Description | Poisonous Concoction of Bouncing is an unarmed attack skill gem that throws a bottle that bounces in random directions, prioritizing enemies, dealing area damage each bounce with a chance to poison. Charges can be consumed from a Life Flask to add further damage. This skill gem requires an empty main hand, and no off-hand weapon or quiver. A Poisonous Flask is visibly attached to your Character's Belt when the skill is socketed in one of your equipped items.
Poisonous Concoction of Bouncing | Description | It is a transfigured version of Poisonous Concoction.
Poisonous Concoction of Bouncing | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this cannot fire multiple projectiles; instead, it chains on the ground in random directions. It also has a slower attack speed, but deals higher added damage based on your life flask's recovery amount. It uses 8 charges per projectile, but since the skill can only ever launch one projectile per attack, Added Projectiles do not cause it to use more flask charges. As an example: when supported by Greater Multiple Projectiles Support , Greater Volley Support , and one additional projectile from Multishot , for a total of 9 additional projectiles, the default version uses 10 flask charges and launches 10 projectiles per attack. Conversely, Poisonous Concoction of Bouncing still only uses 8 flask charges, launches one projectile, which bounces 11 times.
Poisonous Concoction of Bouncing | Skill functions and interactions | This skill's random direction behaviour appears to be different from Nimis ; instead of being purely random, it will attempt to target a nearby enemy with each bounce [1]. If no targets are within range, it will then bounce in a random direction until it finds a new target or expires. This targeting range does not appear to be affected by chaining range.
Poisonous Concoction of Bouncing | Skill functions and interactions | Projectiles created by the skill do not interact with player-made walls, e.g. Frost Wall.
Poisonous Concoction of Bouncing | Skill functions and interactions | Note that modifying the projectile speed will not affect anything other than the speed at which the projectiles travel between bounces (bounce range remains exactly the same); this means that gems like Slower Projectiles Support or any of the projectile speed passives or masteries on the tree will have no effect on the chaining range. However, the Crucible modifiers that reduce chaining range will cause projectiles to land closer to each other, allowing for greater and easier overlaps for increased damage and poison stacking. Similarly, any increases in chaining range will cause projectiles to land further apart. See chaining range for ways to increase the distance between bounces. However, the increase in damage from Slower Projectiles Support still applies to the damage of the skill.
Poisonous Concoction of Bouncing | Item acquisition | Poisonous Concoction of Bouncing has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.
Poisonous Concoction of Bouncing | Recipes | Poisonous Concoction of Bouncing can be created from the following recipes:
Poisonous Concoction of Bouncing | Related helmet enchantments | The following helmet enchantments affect Poisonous Concoction.
Poisonous Concoction of Bouncing | Related helmet enchantments | Note that helmet enchantments are no longer obtainable since version 3.23.0 and can only be obtained in permanent leagues as already enchanted items from before this patch.
Poisonous Concoction of Bouncing | Alternate skill effects | Poisonous Concoction of Bouncing has the following alternate skill effects :
Portal (skill gem) | Infobox | Spell Level: 1 Cast Time: 2.50 sec. Requires Level 10. Creates a portal to the current area's town. Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: (3-60)% increased Cast Speed. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 10
Portal (skill gem) | Description | Portal is a spell that performs the same function as a Portal Scroll , creating a portal to the hideout or the current act's town.
Portal (skill gem) | Skill functions and interactions | Portal is a colourless gem and can be placed into any colour item socket. It is considered a spell and is affected by relevant support gems like Faster Casting Support , Spell Echo Support , Cast on Death Support , High-Impact Mine Support , Spell Totem Support , etc.
Portal (skill gem) | Skill functions and interactions | Unlike most gems, Portal has a default maximum gem level of 1 and does not gain experience. It is possible to raise its level through e.g. corruption , item modifiers, or Empower Support , but as Portal has no stats that scale with levels, this is functionally useless.
Portal (skill gem) | Skill functions and interactions | Hits from enemies interrupt Portal's cast animation, self-damage does not.
Portal (skill gem) | Item acquisition | Portal can drop anywhere.
Portal (skill gem) | Recipes | Portal can be created from the following recipes:
Portal (skill gem) | Ruthless mode | Portal cannot be obtained from quests or traded for using an Uncarved Gemstone through Siosa in Ruthless mode.

Power Charge On Critical Support | Description | Power Charge On Critical Support is a support gem that gives supported skills a chance of granting the player a power charge when they deal a critical strike , as well as more damage per power charge.
Power Charge On Critical Support | Skill functions and interactions | Although each critical strike has an independent chance to grant a charge, most skills are limited to gaining 1 charge per attack or cast, including repeats. [1] Some skills bypass this limitation, see charge gain.

Power Siphon | Description | Power Siphon is a wand attack skill that fires multiple projectiles which generate and have extra bonuses from Power charges. The projectiles have Culling Strike.
Power Siphon | Skill functions and interactions | The skill doesn't follow typical ranged attack rules; instead of physically firing projectiles out of player's wand in a cone, they are fired from within the player at nearby targets within a circle. If there are no nearby enemies, the character will play a casting animation but no projectiles will be fired. Because of that, it acts more like a spell than an attack. It is impossible to "pre-fire" at enemies if no targets are within targeting range.
Power Siphon | Skill functions and interactions | Power Charges generation: The text ".granting you a power charge if an enemy is killed by, or soon after, the hit. " means enemies killed within 0.5 seconds of taking damage from a Power Siphon's hit will grant a power charge, even if killed by a minion or ally. This duration is inherent to the skill and is not modifiable in any way. [1]
Power Siphon | Skill functions and interactions | Locus Mine Support : When supported by Locus Mine, Power Siphon overrides the aim-at-player behaviour, continuing to fire at each nearby enemy. Because each mine can hit the same enemy, this provides a significant increase in single-target damage.
Power Siphon | Item acquisition | Power Siphon can drop anywhere.

Power Siphon of the Archmage | Description | It is a transfigured version of Power Siphon.
Power Siphon of the Archmage | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version has less damage effectiveness and does not gain any critical strike scaling from Power Charges. Instead, it gains added lightning damage based on your maximum mana.

Prismatic Burst Support | Description | Prismatic Burst Support is a support gem that causes the supported attack skill to release a Prismatic Burst on hit , dealing spell damage of a random chosen element in an area around the enemy. The player's strength , dexterity , and intelligence values determine the likelihood to pick fire, cold, and lightning damage respectively.
Prismatic Burst Support | Skill functions and interactions | Restrictions : Supports attacks, causing them to trigger the Prismatic Burst spell. Cannot support triggered attacks or attacks used by things other than you. Cannot modify the skills of minions.
Prismatic Burst Support | Skill functions and interactions | Prismatic Burst triggers at the location where the enemy was hit by an attack.
Prismatic Burst Support | Skill functions and interactions | Choosing elements : The % chance for Prismatic Burst to choose each specific element is visible in the tooltip for the skill.
Prismatic Burst Support | Skill functions and interactions | Conversion : Due to the Deals 100% less Damage of each unchosen Damage Type modifier, converting the skill's damage (such as with Avatar of Fire ) will prevent unchosen damage types from adding any damage to the chosen damage type. Additionally, Prismatic Burst will be unable to ignite unless the chosen damage type of the hit is Fire. Additionally, Prismatic Burst cannot deal any Physical or Chaos damage, so modifiers that cause you to gain Elemental or non-Chaos Damage as Chaos Damage have no effect; this skill cannot poison enemies.

Puncture of Shanking | Description | Puncture of Shanking is a melee attack skill that applies the Bleed ailment to enemies it hits, causing them to take physical damage over time that increases while the target is moving. Bleed applied with this skill deals more damage than usual.
Puncture of Shanking | Description | It is a transfigured version of Puncture.
Puncture of Shanking | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version is a strike skill and can only be used with claws , daggers , and swords. It has a lower bleed duration, but has greater damage effectiveness, more damage with bleeding, and has increased critical strike chance against bleeding enemies.
Puncture of Shanking | Item acquisition | Puncture of Shanking has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

Purity of Elements | Description | Purity of Elements is an aura skill that grants elemental resistances and immunity to all elemental ailments to the caster and their allies.
Purity of Elements | Skill functions and interactions | See the aura page for more details.
Purity of Elements | Skill functions and interactions | Unlike the Purity of Cold/Fire/Lightning variants, Purity of Elements does not increase the player's maximum elemental resistances. The player character is still capped at 75% elemental resistances unless they have other sources to increase the maximum cap.

Purity of Elements | Monster auras | Auras similar to Purity of Elements can appear on monsters as a monster aura , like the explicit mod "Resists Aura" or other implicit and unique mods from the mod type "MonsterAuraResists". The mod is displayed in monster nameplate as Allies Resist Elemental Damage. The monster also has the same aura visual effect as the player variant.

Purity of Fire | Infobox | Aura , Spell , AoE , Fire Level: (1-20) Reservation: 35% Mana Cooldown Time: 1.20 sec Can Store 1 Use(s) Cast Time: Instant AoE Radius: 36. Requires Level 24. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 24
Purity of Fire | Description | Purity of Fire is an aura skill that grants fire resistance and maximum fire resistance to the caster and nearby allies.
Purity of Fire | Skill functions and interactions | Both the additional fire resistance and additional maximum fire resistance modifiers are affected by increased aura effect. The following x% increases in aura effect are needed to receive a higher amount of max resistances. At level 23, Purity of Fire grants +5% to maximum Fire Resistance , and +4% at lower levels.
Purity of Fire | Skill functions and interactions | For example, if one has a level 20 gem, and wants to boost it to provide +9% maximum fire resistance, one needs to have a total of 125% Increased Aura Effect on it.

Pyroclast Mine | Description | Pyroclast Mine throws a mine that deals damage in an area when detonated, and fires multiple mortar projectiles that deal fire area damage when they land. The skill gains additional projectiles with more consecutive mines detonated. Each mine has an aura that applies Fire Exposure to nearby enemies, which does not stack.

Note that Pyroclast Mine fires # additional Projectiles from Eternal labyrinth also has a legacy mod value of 2.

Quickstep | Description | Quickstep is a blink / movement / travel skill which causes the skill user to quickly move in the target direction. It works similarly to Dash , except it can't pass through terrain or monsters that you can't normally walk on or through.
Quickstep | Description | This skill gem is exclusive to Ruthless mode.
Quickstep | Skill functions and interactions | Description : When the skill is cast, the user teleports in the direction of the cursor. If Attack In Place is held down, the user teleports opposite the direction. The gem by default only has one use before one must wait for the cooldown to use it again. It has a max level of 1, and does not improve its stats in any way if its level is increased.
Quickstep | Gem quality | This skill gains no bonuses from gem quality.
Quickstep | Item acquisition | Quickstep is drop disabled.
Quickstep | Item acquisition | This item is a guaranteed drop from Hillock in Ruthless mode.

Rage Vortex of Berserking | Description | Rage Vortex of Berserking is a sword / axe melee attack skill. If you have at least 10 rage , it creates a ragestorm that follow you and continuously deals damage in an area. The ragestorm constantly drains your rage at an accelerating rate while active and lasts until you run out of rage.
Rage Vortex of Berserking | Description | It is a transfigured version of Rage Vortex.
Rage Vortex of Berserking | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version creates a ragestorm that follows your character. Instead of spending additional rage to increase its damage and radius on use, it starts draining your rage to upkeep the ragestorm once activated. Gem quality increases the base radius instead.
Rage Vortex of Berserking | Item acquisition | Rage Vortex of Berserking has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

Raise Spectre | Description | Raise Spectre is a minion spell skill that summons a defeated enemy as an allied minion.

Raise Spectre | Vendor reward | This item can be bought at the listed NPC vendors , after completing the following quests:
Raise Spectre | Strategy | As players progress through the game, there are several monsters that present interesting choices to supplement summoner and hybrid-summon builds alike. Even for a non-summoner build, a single curse-casting spectre whose AI helps them stay away can be quite useful. In practice, it's not that easy to keep a sole spectre alive without having other bodies around to shield it - this means parties and hybrid/full-summon builds.
Raise Spectre | Popular DPS spectres | The following spectres are some examples of popular DPS spectres due to their damage, AI, and/or interactions with other mechanics:
Raise Spectre | Support spectres | The following are some examples of Spectres that are typically useful due to their buffs or debuffs. Note that their AI, buff/debuff uptime, and other mechanics may affect their usefulness:
Raise Spectre | Curse spectres | These spectres can cast curses. (note: spectres share the player's curse limit ).
Raise Spectre | Curse spectres | These spectres can cast curses and also have an innate +1 additional curse limit:
Raise Spectre | Curse spectres | The innate curse limits stack on top of other curse limits, so having both spectres allows both Enfeeble and Temporal Chains to be inflicted in addition to any other curses already applied.
Raise Spectre | Corpse item spectres // aka Aura spectres | Warning : the aura level does scale very slowly with the level of the gems (rougly divide by 4 see https://www.pathofexile.com/forum/view-thread/3908611 )
Raise Spectre | Corpse item spectres // aka Aura spectres | Raising level will improve your spectre life and damages but mostly does nothing to their aura.
Raise Spectre | Corpse item spectres // aka Aura spectres | For example a perfect frozen canibal // Purity of ice :
Raise Spectre | Corpse item spectres // aka Aura spectres | Rituals and Kingsmarch mappers can reward corpse items that create a Spectral Corpse on the ground. Many of these monsters cannot be found as enemies, and when raised, have several abilities including strong buffs and debuffs. However, these monsters are not added to the spectre bank after being raised. If they die or are unsummoned outside your hideout , the corpse can be permanently lost. Reading Guide:How to avoid losing your spectres before investing in any very expensive ones is recommended.
Raise Spectre | Monster-summoning spectres | Some monsters summon other monsters, without the use of minion skills like Raise Zombie. These monsters behave similar to minions, are affected by auras from the player.
Raise Spectre | Legacy spectres | Embers of the Allflame added monster packs that were not available elsewhere in the game. Currently, there is no way to find these monsters.
Raise Spectre | Finding Spectres in Maps | Some guidance on Spectre corpse availability when raising Spectres in maps :
Raise Spectre | "Spectre bank" | Players who may wish to obtain certain harder-to-obtain spectres from other players can do so by having other players use Desecrate (or alternatively, Corpse Walk from Corpsewalker ) while the desired spectre is in their spectre bank. Note that the spectre owner must be the one to use Desecrate; it is not possible for the other player to desecrate a monster that is not already in their pool, as spectres are not added to the area's monster corpse pool.
Raise Spectre | "Spectre bank" | After changes in version 3.25.0 , it is not possible to share spectres between characters on the same account, as corpses generated by characters are removed upon logging out of that character, where the term "spectre bank" originated from. However, reworks to Spectral Corpse generation and recent spectre memory largely mitigated the downsides of this change.
Raise Spectre | "Spectre bank" | It is still possible to create two accounts and run two instance of the game to share spectres. This requires Steam and standalone client of the game or running the standalone client with a secondary account in addition to the main, as well as alt-tabbing or dual monitors setup.
Raise Spectre | Spectre channel | Global Chat 6666 is a channel dedicated to spectre sharing with other players. Due to game population, this channel may only active in the current temporary trade leagues.

Raise Spectre of Transience | Description | Raise Spectre of Transcience is a minion spell skill that summons a defeated enemy as an allied minion.
Raise Spectre of Transience | Description | It is a transfigured version of Raise Spectre.
Raise Spectre of Transience | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, spectres summoned by this skill are temporary and do not persist between zones. In exchange, the skill has a faster cast time and lower mana cost, increased maximum number of spectres, and gives the spectres greatly increased critical strike chance. Gem quality increases the maximum number of spectres instead.

Raise Zombie | Zombie limit | By default, the player can summon up to six zombies at a given time with a level 20 gem. This limit can be increased with certain passive skills or items.
Raise Zombie | Passive skills | The following passive skills increase the player's zombie limit:
Raise Zombie | Unique items | The following unique items affect the player's zombie limit:
Raise Zombie | Mods | Amulets can have an influence mod The Elder's The Elder's Prefix +1 to maximum number of Raised Zombies to have +1 to maximum number of Raised Zombies. Alternatively, similar mod Subterranean Subterranean Prefix +1 to maximum number of Raised Zombies can be found on non-influenced amulets that drop in specific Delve nodes. Lastly, Undying Flesh Talisman has the implicit +1 to maximum number of Raised Zombies. By using Jorgin 's crafting bench, it is possible to create a talisman with both the implicit and Delve explicit, but it is not guaranteed to convert into a Undying Flesh Talisman.
Raise Zombie | Mods | The Delve mod Subterranean Subterranean Prefix +1 to maximum number of Raised Zombies can also be found on certain body armour drops in Delve. There are other mods ( Upgraded Upgraded Prefix +1 to maximum number of Raised Zombies +1 to maximum number of Skeletons or Upgraded Upgraded Prefix +1 to maximum number of Raised Zombies +1 to maximum number of Skeletons ) on legacy body armour, but they can only be obtained from trading with other players in the permanent leagues. They are in the same mod group "MaximumMinionCount" so +2 body armour is not possible.
Raise Zombie | Mods | Jun, Veiled Master can unveil a veiled mod mod for helmets : Chosen - +1 to maximum number of Raised Zombies , +1 to maximum number of Skeletons, Minions have (8-10)% increased maximum Life (Prefix); the crafted mod equivalent is Upgraded - +1 to maximum number of Raised Zombies , +1 to maximum number of Skeletons (Prefix)
Raise Zombie | Mods | By scourging a pair of rare boots (or unique boots if Blood Crucible skill is allocated), there is a chance to acquire +1 to maximum number of Raised Zombies HellscapeUpsideAdditionalRaisedZombie1_ Scourge Benefit +1 to maximum number of Raised Zombies Scourge mod. The scourge mechanic is not yet added to the core game so these mods are only available in permanent leagues.

Raise Zombie of Falling | Description | Raise Zombie of Falling is a minion spell that causes a zombie to fall from the sky, dealing minion attack damage in an area and killing the zombie.
Raise Zombie of Falling | Description | It is a transfigured version of Raise Zombie.
Raise Zombie of Falling | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version does not require a corpse , and the minion is killed upon hitting the ground after hitting the enemy.
Raise Zombie of Falling | Skill functions and interactions | Falling : Zombies are created mid-air and immediately start falling to the ground. Falling speed is unaffected by minion attack speed or minion movement speed. It is affected by the minion's action speed : effects such as Tailwind and The Flow Untethered will slightly accelerate the descent, and freeze can pause zombies mid-air. [1] While falling, zombies are alive and can be affected by damage and other effects, like consumption by Summon Reaper or Maw of Mischief. Zombies that die or get consumed before hitting the ground will not perform their attack.
Raise Zombie of Falling | Skill functions and interactions | Attack : Zombies attack as soon as they impact the ground, using an instant area melee attack that hits for 2.5x the damage of a regular zombie melee attack. [2] Damage is dealt by the zombie, with the zombie's stats, including from buffs and auras. The attack cannot be repeated with Multistrike Support.
Raise Zombie of Falling | Skill functions and interactions | Death : Zombies die as they attack, and despawn shortly after. They do not count as corpses while dead for the purpose of consumption or nearby corpse stats like Corpsewalker. Dying this way does not cause a Minion Instability explosion, as the zombie dies instantly. It does trigger unconditional on-death effects such as Siegebreaker.
Raise Zombie of Falling | Skill functions and interactions | Minion limit : The maximum number of zombies for this spell is 200 [3] and is separate from other Raise Zombie variants. Because the zombies fall to the ground and die in less than a second, this limit will only affect extremely high cast speed situations. Note that this limit is affected by Mon'tregul's Grasp 's zombie limit penalty.
Raise Zombie of Falling | Item acquisition | Raise Zombie of Falling has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

Raise Zombie of Falling | Alternate skill effects | Raise Zombie of Falling has the following alternate skill effects :

Raise Zombie of Slamming | Description | Raise Zombie of Slamming is a minion spell that consumes a corpse to summon an allied zombie minion that can use a slam attack with a cooldown. Zombies are permanent minions with no duration that follow the user that summoned them.
Raise Zombie of Slamming | Description | It is a transfigured version of Raise Zombie.
Raise Zombie of Slamming | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version does not gain an additional multiplier to minion life as the gem levels. Instead, the zombies have a second skill to use alongside the default auto-attack - Zombie Slam - which has a large Area of Effect and a cooldown.
Raise Zombie of Slamming | Skill functions and interactions | Zombie Slam is queued immediately after the skill is off cooldown and will prioritize it over any other action except moving. Zombies Slam has a base 6-second cooldown. With no further investment into minion cooldown recovery, Zombie Slam has a 3.4-second cooldown at gem level 20.
Raise Zombie of Slamming | Skill functions and interactions | The Slam has the Melee , Slam , Attack , and Area of Effect tags.
Raise Zombie of Slamming | Item acquisition | Raise Zombie of Slamming has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

Base duration is (2.50-3.40) seconds (0-38)% increased Warcry Speed Counts total Power of Enemies and Allies in Range Buff grants Added Attack Damage equal to 4% of the Damage of your Main Hand Weapon per 5 Power, counting a maximum of 30 Power Buff has 100% more Effect on your Minions Exerts the next 5 Melee Attacks you perform Exerted Attacks deal 5% more Damage per affected Ally, to a maximum of 25% Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: (2-40)% increased Cooldown Recovery Rate. Rallying Cry | Description | Rallying Cry is a warcry skill that taunts nearby enemies, grants nearby allies an added damage buff based on the user's main hand weapon and power of nearby enemies and allies, and exerts a number of subsequent attacks to deal more damage based on the number of allies buffed by the warcry.
Rallying Cry | Skill functions and interactions | Power : Normal Monsters have 1 Power, Magic Monsters 2, Rare Monsters 10, Unique Monsters 20, and Players 5
Rallying Cry | Skill functions and interactions | more Damage per affected Ally : This modifier counts the number of allies buffed by the warcry. It does not require allies to stay buffed or stay alive afterward; the damage bonus will apply to exerted attacks regardless. [2] Using Rallying Cry again will record a new number of buffed allies, even if the player had exerts remaining with a greater damage bonus. If the skill cannot apply buffs due to Autoexertion , the number of affected allies will be 0, and exerted attacks will not receive a damage multiplier. [3]
Rallying Cry | Skill functions and interactions | Buff : Multiple Rallying Cry buffs can be granted to allies, but only the strongest one will be active. Each instance has an independent duration. Weapon swapping immediately removes the Rallying Cry buff from allies.
Rallying Cry | Skill functions and interactions | Proxies : Because proxies like totems and mirages use the player's skill, which has the player's stats and not their own, their damage cannot be buffed by Rallying Cry. They do count as affected allies for the exerted attack damage bonus, however.

Reap | Description | Reap is a physical spell that hits with physical damage and applies a strong physical damage over time debuff in a targeted area. If all enemies hit by this skill survive, the player gains a Blood Charge up to a maximum of 5. Blood charges increase this skill's damage and life cost.
Reap | Skill functions and interactions | Casting Reap costs Life instead of Mana.
Reap | Skill functions and interactions | Spell Cascade Support or Awakened Spell Cascade Support are particularly useful to scale the hit damage part of Reap.
Reap | Blood Charges | Blood Charges have no intrinsic effects, has a default maximum of 5, and a default duration of 10 seconds. Gaining a Blood Charge refreshes the stack duration. Non-player objects such as totems have a maximum of 0 Blood Charges, and proxy hits do not grant charges to the player. [1]
Reap | Blood Charges | Blood Charges are shared between multiple Reap skill gems, similar to how Inspiration Charges are shared between multiple Inspiration Support gems. Generic charge duration such as those from Charge Mastery affect Blood Charge duration; skill duration does not affect it, however.
Reap | Blood Charges | Hydrosphere can be hit to generate Blood Charges, since it meets the criteria of surviving enemies. This can be used prior to engaging a boss to maximize damage output. The Hydrosphere will grant Blood Charges even while pulsing despite the hit cooldown.
Reap | Spell Damage Modifiers | The line "Modifiers to Spell Damage apply to this Skill's Damage Over Time effect" specifically applies to DoT part of this this skill, and doesn't affect poison, other ailments, or other additional DoT effect like Decay. [2]

Righteous Fire | Infobox | Spell , AoE , Fire Level: (1-20) Cooldown Time: 0.30 sec Can Store 1 Use(s) Cast Time: Instant. Requires Level 16. Engulfs you in magical fire that rapidly burns you and nearby enemies. Your spell damage is substantially increased while under this effect. The effect ends when you have 1 life remaining. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 16
Righteous Fire | Description | Righteous Fire is a spell that applies a buff to the player, granting more spell damage , but also applying a potent burning effect that scales with the user's maximum life and energy shield to both the user and nearby enemies.
Righteous Fire | Skill functions and interactions | Damage over time effect : While the Righteous Fire buff is active, the player and surrounding enemies will take fire damage over time.
Righteous Fire | Skill functions and interactions | Stopping Righteous Fire : Righteous Fire's buff and burning effect will apply until the player reaches 1 life , at which point the buff is removed. The effect can be ended prematurely by any effect which removes burning, such as flasks with a Removes Burning on use affix. Unsocketing the gem out of its socket will remove the skill effect, but will not stop the burning effect.
Righteous Fire | Skill functions and interactions | More Spell Damage buff : Righteous Fire provides a large "more damage" modifier to spell damage during its effect, which is multiplicative with other damage multipliers. This buff does not affect Righteous Fire itself, as it does not scale with spell damage.
Righteous Fire | Skill functions and interactions | Damage modifiers :
Righteous Fire | Skill functions and interactions | Duration scaling : Linking Righteous Fire with the support gems Lifetap Support or Arcane Surge Support causes Righteous Fire to gain a duration effect, allowing the skill to be supported by Swift Affliction Support and Less Duration Support , which normally would not support it.
Righteous Fire | Skill functions and interactions | Spell Totem Support : When linked with the support gem Spell Totem Support , the burning damage effect is cast and applied by the totem instead, based on its own maximum life and energy shield, not the player's. Righteous Fire's more spell damage buff does nothing in this case.
Righteous Fire | Skill functions and interactions | Inspiration Support : If Righteous Fire is linked with Inspiration Support , as long as the player can gain Inspiration Charges from another source (such as a movement skill linked to another Inspiration Support), then Righteous Fire will benefit from the Elemental damage scaling from Inspiration Support.
Righteous Fire | Skill functions and interactions | Lifetap Support : Similarly, if Righteous Fire is linked with Lifetap Support , as long as the player can gain Lifetap from another source (such as a movement skill linked to another Lifetap Support), then Righteous Fire will benefit from the damage scaling from Lifetap Support.
Righteous Fire | Skill functions and interactions | Ancestral Bond : It is possible to use Righteous Fire with the Ancestral Bond keystone , though the skill will only burn the user, not nearby enemies.
Righteous Fire | Skill functions and interactions | Gluttony of Elements : If Gluttony of Elements is used with Righteous Fire, the skill will restore life over time instead.
Righteous Fire | Skill functions and interactions | Damage stacking : Multiple sources of Righteous Fire (such as from multiple players) do not stack. Instead, the source with the highest damage will take effect on the enemy.
Righteous Fire | Sustaining Righteous Fire | The most straight-forward way of sustaining Righteous Fire's burning damage is combining Fire Resistance with Regeneration. Depending on the player's resistance, less regeneration is needed to break even. The base formula is:
Righteous Fire | Sustaining Righteous Fire | where life , ES and fireRes is the characters maximum life , maximum energy shield and fire resistance. burnLife and burnES is the percent of life and energy shield that Righteous fire burns for and dmgTaken is the sum of all Damage taken stats, such as 10% reduced Damage taken from Damage Over Time from the Soul of Arakaali Pantheon power or #% increased Damage taken from Shock.
Righteous Fire | Sustaining Righteous Fire | The maximum Fire Resistance can be increased by allocating passive skills such as Barbarism , using the Purity of Fire aura and utilizing modifiers on various rare or unique items such as Rise of the Phoenix , up to a cap of 90% maximum Fire Resistance.

Rupture Support | Description | Rupture is a support gem that supports Attack skills, causing them to inflict a stack of Rupture if they deal a critical strike that also applies bleeding. Rupture is a debuff that causes enemies to take more damage from bleeding, but also causes bleeding to expire more quickly. Per its reminder text, Up to 4 Ruptures can be inflicted on a target, lasting 3 seconds each.
Rupture Support | Skill functions and interactions | Rupture dynamically applies to all bleeding debuffs on the target, even those that are not applied by supported skills.
Rupture Support | Skill functions and interactions | Rupture's effect on expiration rate is effectively multiplicative with effects that cause bleeding to deal damage faster , such as Sadism Support. [1]
Rupture Support | Skill functions and interactions | The duration of Rupture is not affected by skill effect duration, as indicated by the lack of Duration gem tag.

Ruthless Support | Infobox | Slam , Support , Melee , Attack , Strike Icon: u Level: (1-20) Cost & Reservation Multiplier: 120%. Supports melee strike or slam skills. Every third time you use a supported attack, that use of the skill will be more powerful. Every third Attack with Supported Melee Attacks deals a Ruthless Blow Ruthless Blows with Supported Skills deal (70-98)% more Melee Damage Ruthless Blows with Supported Skills deal (70-98)% more Damage with Ailments caused by Melee Hits Ruthless Blows with Supported Skills have a base Stun Duration of 0.8 seconds Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Supported Skills deal (0.5-10)% increased Attack Damage. This is a Support Gem. It does not grant a bonus to your character, but to skills in sockets connected to it. Place into an item socket connected to a socket containing the Skill Gem you wish to augment. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 5
Ruthless Support | Description | Ruthless Support is a support gem that grants every third use of a strike or slam attack substantially more melee damage and additional stun duration.
Ruthless Support | Skill functions and interactions | Skills supported by Ruthless Support track the number of times they are used. If multiple skills are supported, each skill has its own independent counter. Skills do not need to hit enemies or complete their animation to increase the Ruthless counter. [1]
Ruthless Support | Skill functions and interactions | Ruthless Blows are dealt every third skill use, and their bonuses apply to that entire skill use, even if it hits multiple times over a duration or by repeating. For example, if an attack skill has 2 repeats (from e.g. Multistrike Support ) and is used 3 times, all strikes from the 3rd use (the 7th, 8th, and 9th animations) will deal Ruthless Blows. [2]
Ruthless Support | Skill functions and interactions | It is possible to reset the Ruthless Blow counter to 0 by losing the active skill it supports, for example by resocketing it, or re-equipping the item it's socketed in. Resocketing Ruthless Support itself does not reset the counter.
Ruthless Support | Skill functions and interactions | Ruthless Blows' damage bonuses do not apply to non-melee hits from melee skills, such as Smite 's area damage or Frost Blades 's projectiles. They can gain additional stun duration.
Ruthless Support | Item acquisition | Ruthless Support can drop anywhere.

Sadism Support | Description | Sadism Support is a support gem which causes damaging ailments you inflict to deal damage faster and have less duration.
Sadism Support | Skill functions and interactions | Sadism Support greatly modifies ailment duration.
Sadism Support | Skill functions and interactions | Damaging Ailments inflicted with Supported Skills deal damage #% faster is additive with other modifiers that cause ailments to deal damage faster or slower.
Sadism Support | Skill functions and interactions | To calculate the new ailment duration after supporting it with a Level 21 Sadism Support, use the following formula:
Sadism Support | Skill functions and interactions | Without any other sources of increased ailment duration, damaging ailments have the following modified base durations:
Sadism Support | Skill functions and interactions | Without other sources of 'deals damage over time faster' the amount of Dot is
Sadism Support | Skill functions and interactions | So DoT damage with sadism over one (now much shorter) period = Base Dot damage * 0.3
Sadism Support | Skill functions and interactions | A crude approximation is: for sadism to be effective you need to be able to apply at the ailment at least 3.34 times during the "normal ailment duration" (see below for special cases and examples)
Sadism Support | Skill functions and interactions | Ignite and Bleeding gain more value from Sadism Support, as they cannot normally stack (and when they do, have a maximum stack amount), causing excessive stacks to be wasted. Modifying its duration to be significantly shorter allows greater efficiency of ailments. However, Sadism will also more likely than not result in large dps losses due to several factors, such as the fact "Damaging Ailments inflicted with Supported Skills deal damage faster" is additive to other sources (making it a poor support to begin with) and the fact it will very likely result in uptime losses, as you will need to continuously use your skills in order to inflict several bleeds or ignite per second. As soon as you are not able to do so, the slight dps increase is immediately lost by the fact you are not doing any damage while moving.
Sadism Support | Skill functions and interactions | Poison does not inherently have this issue as there is no maximum limit to poison stacks. However, several mechanics may benefit from having short-duration poisons so they expire before the next application, such as the Poison Mastery and stacking Low Tolerance which care about non-poisoned enemies, and Viper Strike of the Mamba , which cannot poison enemies that are already poisoned. However, even in this case, you are still required to scale attack speed and get a dramatic damage loss every time you are moving.
Sadism Support | Skill functions and interactions | The less duration downside of Sadism Support can also be circumvented with Alchemist's Mark or Flame Surge , which creates a ground effect that deals damage over time based on ailments on the enemy, but lasts a fixed duration which is unaffected by ailment duration.
Sadism Support | Item acquisition | Sadism Support can drop anywhere.

Sadism Support | Vendor reward | This item can be bought at the listed NPC vendors , after completing the following quests:

Scourge Arrow | Infobox | Attack , Projectile , Channelling , Chaos , Bow Level: (1-20) Cost: (4-5) Mana Attack Speed: 190% of base Attack Damage: (48-65)% of base Effectiveness of Added Damage: (48-65)%. Scourge Arrow | Description | Scourge Arrow is a channelling bow attack skill. Channelling the skill charges up an arrow that creates spore pods as the arrow travels when released. The pods explode to fire thorn arrows around them. The arrow and pods deal physical damage converted to chaos damage.
Scourge Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | Additional arrows (from support gems and items like Death's Harp ) apply to the primary arrow, instead of the thorn arrows. Additional arrows cause spore pods to distribute evenly among each primary arrow, rather than in a line from player. Having more arrows than pods will not cause additional pods to spawn. Therefore at 5 stages Scourge Arrow will spawn 5 pods — if the player has 6 total arrows (5 additional) one of the arrows will not spawn a pod.
Scourge Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | The distance between each spore pod can be increased by releasing the channel while mouse cursor is near the edge of your screen. With 4 additional arrows, the spore pods spawn in a cluster near your mouse cursor.
Scourge Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | When used with Ballista Totem Support , the totem will always fully charge and then shoot.
Scourge Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | Primary arrow collision : If a primary arrow disappears (e.g. because of collision with wall/obstacle) before all pods for that primary arrow are spawned, the rest of the pods will not spawn.
Scourge Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | Point Blank interaction : The thorn arrows are all continuations of the original arrow from the character to the point the pods were placed. Therefore the distance travelled used for Point Blank 's calculation is the total of how far the original arrow travelled to place the pod, plus how far the thorn arrow travelled after being fired from the pod.
Scourge Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | Shotgunning : From a single attack, multiple thorn arrows from different pods can hit the same target, but multiple thorn arrows from the same pod cannot hit the same target.
Scourge Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | Pierce : All parts of Scourge Arrow pierce everything by default, this includes both primary arrow and thorn arrows.
Scourge Arrow | Skill functions and interactions | Return : By default with for example Returning Projectiles only primary arrow will return. In order to also make thorn arrows return, at least 55% total projectile speed is required.
Scourge Arrow | Item acquisition | Scourge Arrow can drop anywhere.
Scourge Arrow | Recipes | Scourge Arrow can be created from the following recipes:
Scourge Arrow | Quest reward | This item is given as a quest reward for the following quests:
Scourge Arrow | Vendor reward | This item can be bought at the listed NPC vendors , after completing the following quests:

Scourge Arrow | Related helmet enchantments | Note that helmet enchantments are no longer obtainable since version 3.23.0 and can only be obtained in permanent leagues as already enchanted items from before this patch.
Scourge Arrow | Alternate skill effects | Scourge Arrow has the following alternate skill effects :

Each arrow fired leaves only one spore pod, and each spore pod fires less thorn arrows. Base attack speed, added damage effectiveness, and mana cost have been adjusted.

Scourge Arrow of Menace | Skill functions and interactions | Arrow collision : It's possible for the primary arrow to disappear (e.g. because of collision with wall/obstacle) before it releases its pod.
Scourge Arrow of Menace | Skill functions and interactions | Point Blank interaction : The thorn arrows are all continuations of the original arrow from the character to the point the pods were placed. Therefore the distance travelled used for Point Blank 's calculation is the total of how far the original arrow travelled to place the pod, plus how far the thorn arrow travelled after being fired from the pod.
Scourge Arrow of Menace | Skill functions and interactions | Shotgunning : From a single attack, multiple thorn arrows from different pods can hit the same target, but multiple thorn arrows from the same pod cannot hit the same target.
Scourge Arrow of Menace | Skill functions and interactions | Pierce : All parts of Scourge Arrow pierce everything by default, this includes both primary arrow and thorn arrows.
Scourge Arrow of Menace | Skill functions and interactions | Return : By default with for example Returning Projectiles only primary arrow will return. In order to also make thorn arrows return, at least 55% total projectile speed is required.

Searing Bond | Infobox | Spell , Totem , Fire Level: (1-20) Cost: (18-51) Mana Cast Time: 1.00 sec. Requires Level 12. Summons a totem that casts a beam of fire at you and each other totem you control, dealing burning damage to enemies caught in the beam. Enemies near either end of a beam also suffer burning damage. +1 to maximum number of Summoned Totems Totem lasts 8 seconds Deals (32.1-3899.6) Base Fire Damage per second Modifiers to Spell Damage apply to this Skill's Damage Over Time effect Summons a Totem which uses this Skill Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: +(0.1-2) to maximum number of Summoned Totems. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 12
Searing Bond | Description | Searing Bond is a spell skill that summons a totem that creates fiery beams between the caster and each other totem under their control. The beams deal fire damage over time to enemies touching it. Totem range is 100.
Searing Bond | Skill Functions and Interactions | Searing Bond totems create beams between themselves and the totem's caster while they are within range of each other, approximately a screen and a half. If the totems are placed too far apart, or the caster moves too far away, the beam will disappear, but will re-appear when re-entering the range. The beams will also fail to appear if there is an obstruction by terrain or non-traversible object (e.g. a large boulder), but will not be blocked by and can clip through elevation changes.
Searing Bond | Skill Functions and Interactions | Searing Bond is considered burning damage and therefore deals fire damage over time. It also scales with modifiers to totem damage and spell damage but does not deal spell damage and therefore cannot be scaled by Unnerve. Searing Bond, the skill used by the totem, does not hit , and cannot penetrate resistances. However, the totems themselves can be made to hit with Flamewood Support , which can benefit from synergistic scaling through fire damage.
Searing Bond | Skill Functions and Interactions | Maximum totems : The +# to maximum number of Summoned Totems inherent to this gem and its quality effect applies locally to the Searing Bond skill gem only. The global totem limit is still restricted by the skill with the maximum number of totems, however. For example, if a Holy Flame Totem and 0 quality Searing Bond is linked to Multiple Totems Support , the maximum totems for each skill are 3 and 4 respectively; 1 Searing Bond will never be replaced no matter how many times Holy Flame Totems are placed. However, if Siege Ballista is linked to Multiple Totems Support instead, Siege Ballista will always have 5-6 max totems and can replace all your active Searing Bonds.
Searing Bond | Item acquisition | Searing Bond can drop anywhere.
Searing Bond | Recipes | Searing Bond can be created from the following recipes:

Second Wind Support | Description | Second Wind Support is a support gem that allows the linked skill with a cooldown to store an additional use and gives it reduced cooldown recovery rate.
Second Wind Support | Skill functions and interactions | Second Wind Support supports skills such as attacks , spells and warcries that have a cooldown.
Second Wind Support | Skill functions and interactions | Instant skills such as Frostblink can be linked to Second Wind Support and will not receive +1 Cooldown Use , but the (24-5)% reduced Cooldown Recovery Rate will apply.
Second Wind Support | Skill functions and interactions | Some pseudo-instant skills, such as Dash and Flame Dash , will also benefit from the +1 Cooldown Use.

Shield Charge | Infobox | Attack , AoE , Movement , Travel , Physical , Melee Level: (1-20) Cost: (6-10) Mana Attack Time: 0.50 sec Critical Strike Chance: 5.00% AoE Radius: 8 / 16. Requires Level 4. Charges at a targeted location or enemy, pushing away enemies in your path and repeatedly dealing off-hand damage in a small area in front of you. You deal damage in a larger area when you reach the target. The further you travel, the more damage you deal, and the greater your chance of stunning enemies. (9-252) to (13-378) Base Off Hand Physical Damage (3-8) to (5-12) Added Physical Damage per 15 Armour or Evasion Rating on Shield 75% increased Stun Threshold reduction on enemies at Maximum charge distance 100% more Damage with Hits at Maximum Charge Distance (90-109)% increased Movement Speed Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: (1-20)% increased Movement Speed. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 4
Shield Charge | Description | Shield Charge is an attack movement travel skill. The user charges at a location with their shield , dealing area damage while moving and on arrival. Enemies in the path of the charge are knocked back. Unlike most attack skills, Shield Charge uses your shield's stats to calculate damage.
Shield Charge | Skill functions and interactions | Shield charge can be used in two ways: a shield charge that moves toward a distant target, or a stationary shield bash against a nearby target.
Shield Charge | Skill functions and interactions | Attack time: Shield Charge gains its base attack time from the gem, not the weapon or the shield. This time is used at the end of a charge: one attack time must pass before the user can perform another attack. Global attack speed modifiers will decrease this delay. The attack time of stationary uses is 30% slower.
Shield Charge | Skill functions and interactions | Movement speed: The speed of the charge is scaled by the user's movement speed, as well as global modifiers to attack speed. Shield Charge gains considerable movement speed from the gem, gem levels, and gem quality.
Shield Charge | Skill functions and interactions | Distance: The maximum charge distance is around 7.5 metres. Damage and reduced enemy stun threshold are proportional to the distance charged, with the full bonus acquired after 6 metres. [1] Note that the in-game average damage tooltip for Shield Charge does not factor in the bonus gained from charge distance.
Shield Charge | Skill functions and interactions | Area of Effect: All hits by Shield Charge deal area damage. All of its damaging areas can be scaled by modifiers to area of effect.
Shield Charge | Skill functions and interactions | Shield: Like other shield skills , damage dealt is based on the equipped shield and global stats. Stats local or specific to the main hand weapon do not apply; shield charge hits will not proc effects that require weapon hits, such as The Poet's Pen , Cospri's Malice , or Mjölner.
Shield Charge | Skill functions and interactions | Repeating : Shield Charge cannot be supported by Multistrike Support. If it gains repeats from another source, only the frontal cone part of the skill will be repeated. [2]
Shield Charge | Item acquisition | Shield Charge can drop anywhere.

Shield Charge | Vendor reward | This item can be bought at the listed NPC vendors , after completing the following quests:

Shield Crush of the Chieftain | Description | Shield Crush of the Chieftain is a melee attack skill. The user swipes their shield, dealing area damage in three waves in front of them as fire damage. Enemies can be hit by two of the waves where they overlap. Shield Crush uses your shield's stats to calculate damage.
Shield Crush of the Chieftain | Description | Shield Crush requires a shield.
Shield Crush of the Chieftain | Description | It is a transfigured version of Shield Crush.
Shield Crush of the Chieftain | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version deals fire damage instead of physical at a greater value and gains added fire damage based on the shield's armour rating (but not evasion rating). It has lower mana cost and a slower attack time. The central wave also has more area of effect, which is further increased by gem quality, but the side waves don't gain added radius per gem level.
Shield Crush of the Chieftain | Item acquisition | Shield Crush of the Chieftain has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

Shock Nova | Infobox | Spell , AoE , Lightning , Nova Level: (1-20) Cost: (12-23) Mana Cast Time: 0.70 sec Critical Strike Chance: 6.00% Effectiveness of Added Damage: (140-190)% AoE Radius: 26 / 14. Requires Level 28. Casts a ring of Lightning around you, followed by a larger Lightning nova. Each effect hits enemies caught in their area with Lightning Damage. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 28
Shock Nova | Description | Shock Nova is a lightning area of effect spell that deals lightning damage in an area around the caster, conjuring an initial ring followed by a larger circular area. Shock Nova has an innate chance to inflict shock and provides increased effectiveness of this ailment.
Shock Nova | Skill functions and interactions | Shock Nova has two damaging components: an initial ring followed by a larger circular area, both dealing the same lightning damage. It is therefore possible for monsters to receive damage from either component, or both. [1]
Shock Nova | Skill functions and interactions | Modifiers to area of effect affect both inner and outer radii of the initial ring. The subsequent damaging area has a larger outer radius than the ring, and all monsters located between the spell's center (the caster by default) up to that radius get hit.
Shock Nova | Skill functions and interactions | Added damage to spells benefits both the ring and the larger nova, giving this skill a 380% effectiveness of added damage per cast if both components hit. For optimal damage, the caster must position themselves at the correct distance away from their target.
Shock Nova | Skill functions and interactions | Shock Nova's Ring always Shocks has no effect when supported by Elemental Focus Support.
Shock Nova | Item acquisition | Shock Nova can drop anywhere.

Shockwave Totem | Description | Shockwave Totem is a spell skill. It summons a totem that releases shockwaves that deal physical damage around it.
Shockwave Totem | Skill Functions and Interactions | The shockwave has spell, AoE, totem and physical tags, so modifiers to any of those will increase its damage.
Shockwave Totem | Skill Functions and Interactions | Due to being a Nova skill, Astral Projector can be used to let totems create shockwaves on top of ranged enemies. Shockwave totems have marginally higher targeting range than other Nova skills supported by Spell Totem Support.
Shockwave Totem | Skill Functions and Interactions | Physical Damage: Because Shockwave Totem deals physical damage it can be boosted by supports and passive skills that are more commonly used to modify attacks, such as the Added Fire Damage support gem or passive skills which grant Physical Damage with no other qualifier, such as Path of the Warrior.
Shockwave Totem | Skill Functions and Interactions | Knockback Distance: As skills cast by the totem are affected by all modifiers affecting the player, the knockback distance is also increased by your passives.

Shrapnel Ballista | Infobox | Attack , Projectile , Totem , Bow Level: (1-20) Cost: (7-13) Mana Attack Speed: 50% of base Attack Damage: (45-52)% of base Effectiveness of Added Damage: (45-52)%. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 5
Shrapnel Ballista | Description | Shrapnel Ballista is a bow attack skill. It summons a ballista totem that fires multiple arrows in a cone. Its damage scales on the player's bow damage.
Shrapnel Ballista | Skill functions and interactions | Ballista: Shrapnel Ballista is part of the ballista sub-class of totems. Ballista totems have an inherent increase to maximum totem limit. They have a much shorter summoning range, spawning much closer to the summoner.
Shrapnel Ballista | Skill functions and interactions | "Shotgunning": Shrapnel Ballista is capable of hitting a single enemy with more than one projectile from a single skill use. As such, this skill can be used to kill multiple targets or can be used to cause massive damage to a single enemy. If supported with Arrow Nova Support , your Shrapnel Ballista will fire a payload arrow on an enemy's location, which upon landing fires all of its projectiles in a nova that can hit the enemy simultaneously (assuming the target does not move out of the way).
Shrapnel Ballista | Skill functions and interactions | Returning Projectiles: Projectiles fired by shrapnel ballista have a limited duration; to ensure projectiles can return to hit the initial target, projectile speed will need to be increased by at least 300%. [1]
Shrapnel Ballista | Item acquisition | Shrapnel Ballista can drop anywhere.
Shrapnel Ballista | Recipes | Shrapnel Ballista can be created from the following recipes:
Shrapnel Ballista | Quest reward | This item is given as a quest reward for the following quests:
Shrapnel Ballista | Vendor reward | This item can be bought at the listed NPC vendors , after completing the following quests:

Its damage scales on the player's bow damage. This skill consumes Steel Shards from Call of Steel to have each totem fire additional arrows.
Shrapnel Ballista of Steel | Description | It is a transfigured version of Shrapnel Ballista.
Shrapnel Ballista of Steel | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version grants the Call of Steel skill, which is used to generate Steel Shards. Totems from this skill consume 1 Steel Shard per attack to fire more than 1 projectile. The skill has higher damage effectiveness and gains an additional chance to impale.
Shrapnel Ballista of Steel | Skill functions and interactions | Additional Projectiles : If you use the skill without any Steel Shards, it will always launch only one projectile, even if you have additional projectiles from other sources.
Shrapnel Ballista of Steel | Skill functions and interactions | Ballista: Shrapnel Ballista is part of the ballista sub-class of totems. Ballista totems have an inherent increase to maximum totem limit. They have a much shorter summoning range, spawning much closer to the summoner.

If supported with Arrow Nova Support , your Shrapnel Ballista will fire a payload arrow on an enemy's location, which upon landing fires all of its projectiles in a nova that can hit the enemy simultaneously (assuming the target does not move out of the way).

Sigil of Power | Description | Sigil of Power is a spell which places a sigil in an area around the caster for a duration. The sigil grants added lightning damage to friendly units. Spending mana while within the sigil empowers it, increasing the buff effect and when full powered, causes enemies within the sigil to deal less damage.
Sigil of Power | Skill functions and interactions | Each skill usage within the area can only increase the number of stages by one, regardless of Mana spent.
Sigil of Power | Skill functions and interactions | Sigil of Power can advance to stage 2 immediately, as being placed at stage 1 doesn't count as "gaining" a stage. Advancing to stage 3 or higher takes a minimum of one second after the previous stage was gained. [1]
Sigil of Power | Skill functions and interactions | Arcane: Sigil of Power cannot be supported by Archmage Support. Stages cannot be gained when converting mana cost to a different resource e.g. energy shield via Eldritch Battery or life via Blood Magic.

Siphoning Trap | Description | Siphoning Trap is a spell that throws a trap that tethers to nearby enemies when activated. Tethered enemies take cold damage over time and the character gains life and mana regeneration for each tether active.
Siphoning Trap | Skill functions and interactions | Cluster Traps Support and Multiple Traps Support do not benefit Siphoning Trap as only one use of the skill is stored at a time. The reduced damage from the support gems is still inherited, however. Despite that, it is possible to have multiple instances of the skill active, as long as they don't tether to the same target. If that happens, the oldest skill instance will be immediately terminated.

Slower Projectiles Support | Infobox | Support , Projectile Icon: J Level: (1-20) Cost & Reservation Multiplier: 120%. Requires Level 31. Supports projectile skills. Supported Skills have (20-30)% less Projectile Speed Supported Skills deal (10-19)% more Projectile Damage Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Supported Skills deal (0.5-10)% increased Projectile Damage. This is a Support Gem. It does not grant a bonus to your character, but to skills in sockets connected to it. Place into an item socket connected to a socket containing the Skill Gem you wish to augment. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 31
Slower Projectiles Support | Description | Slower Projectiles is a support gem. It reduces the speed and increases the damage of supported projectile skills.
Slower Projectiles Support | Skill Functions and Interactions | Slower Projectiles won't affect how far arrow projectiles travel, since arrow projectiles have a fixed travel distance of 120 units. [1] However, it will still affect the travel distance of projectiles from skills such as Freezing Pulse , which have a lifetime rather than a fixed travel distance.
Slower Projectiles Support | Skill Functions and Interactions | Slower Projectiles can support Ball Lightning , which causes the projectiles to deal more total hits/damage because enemies would be in the area of effect for longer before the projectile passes by them. At level 20, Slower Projectiles would result in approximately 9 Ball Lightning hits to an enemy instead of 6, other factors being equal.
Slower Projectiles Support | Item acquisition | Slower Projectiles Support can drop anywhere.

Slower Projectiles Support | Vendor reward | This item can be bought at the listed NPC vendors , after completing the following quests:
Slower Projectiles Support | Mods | The T1 gloves mod can be upgraded to Elevated Shaper's - (23-25)% increased Projectile Damage , Socketed Gems are Supported by Level 25 Slower Projectiles (Prefix) by using a Orb of Dominance. Read the article of the orb for details.
Slower Projectiles Support | Legacy mods | Crucible is not added to the core game, the following mods can only be found in existing legacy items in the permanent leagues.

Snipe | Description | Snipe is a skill gem that supports linked bow skills. It is a channelling bow attack that gains stages while channelling. When released, it triggers a linked non-channelling bow skill for each stage stored and deals more damage per stage. If Snipe is not linked to any applicable skills, it instead fires its own arrow.

Snipe | Skill functions and interactions | As Snipe Support :
Snipe | Skill functions and interactions | Supported skills also have a 50% cost/reservation multiplier and a cooldown time of 0.05 seconds (50 ms).
Snipe | Mana manipulation | By reserving a large portion of mana and having insufficient mana regeneration to channel past the first stage, it is possible to make Snipe repeatedly fire after the first stage, providing a massive bonus to attack speed. [2]

Sniper's Mark | Description | Sniper's Mark is a spell that marks a single target with a curse. The marked target takes increased damage from projectiles and causes projectiles that hit the marked target to split.
Sniper's Mark | Skill functions and interactions | Only one mark (regardless of source) can be active at all times. Increasing the curse limit will allow you to apply additional hexes on top of a mark but does not let you apply additional marks on different targets or apply multiple marks on one target.
Sniper's Mark | Skill functions and interactions | Increased curse effect can increase the amount of splits based on the following formula Total Splits = ( Base splits of Sniper's Mark ( 2 ) × Increase curse effect ) \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}{\text{Total Splits}}=\left({\text{Base splits of Sniper's Mark}}(2)\times {\text{Increase curse effect}}\right) resolving this we can work out the required curse effect for each split Increase curse effect = T o t a l S p l i t s B a s e s p l i t s o f S n i p e r ′ s M a r k \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}{\text{Increase curse effect}}={{TotalSplits} \over {BasesplitsofSniper'sMark}}

Somatic Shell | Infobox | Duration , Physical , Attack , AoE , Projectile Level: (1-20) Cost: (7-10) Mana Attack Damage: (20-50)% of base Effectiveness of Added Damage: (20-50)%. Requires Level 28. Fire a damaging projectile from your wand which aims itself towards an enemy and applies a shell to the first enemy struck. Damage from hits against that target is taken from the shell first. If fully depleted, the shell will explode, dealing area damage and firing a projectile at another target to apply a new shell to it. If multiple projectiles are fired by an exploding shell, only the first one to hit a target can apply a shell. Increases and Reductions to Spell Damage also apply to Attack Damage from this Skill at 150% of their value Base duration is 5.00 seconds Damage from this Skill cannot be taken from Shells 750% more Area Damage Base explosion radius is 1.5 metres Damage from Hits is taken from Shell before target's Life or Energy Shield Shell can take (168-6214) Damage before exploding Shell cannot be reapplied to same target for 0.2 seconds after explosion Maximum (3-6) Somatic Shells Projectiles fired from explosion can apply no more than 1 new Shell Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Base radius is (0.015-0.3) metres. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 28
Somatic Shell | Description | Somatic Shell is a wand attack skill that fires a projectile that automatically targets a nearby enemy, dealing damage and applying a shell to the first enemy hit. While the enemy has a shell, it will take hit damage from the shell's life before the enemy's life or energy shield ; when fully depleted, the shell will explode, dealing significant area damage and firing another projectile towards a random target which applies a new shell to the first enemy hit by the projectile.
Somatic Shell | Skill functions and interactions | It is possible to have multiple shells on different enemies at once; however, a shell will only be created on the first enemy hit by any projectile per skill use or per explosion.
Somatic Shell | Skill functions and interactions | Explosion : The explosion is not considered projectile damage. Damage from the explosion bypasses the damage redirection from shells on enemies.
Somatic Shell | Skill functions and interactions | Additional projectiles : Modifiers to projectile count apply to both the initial fired projectile as well as the projectiles fired when a shell explodes. However, it is new shells are still limited to the first enemy hit per skill use or explosion, regardless of the number of projectiles fired.
