Spark | Infobox | Spell , Projectile , Duration , Lightning Level: (1-20) Cost: (5-21) Mana Cast Time: 0.65 sec Critical Strike Chance: 6.00% Effectiveness of Added Damage: 190% Projectile Speed: 420. Launches unpredictable sparks that move randomly until they hit an enemy or expire. Deals (1-104) to (28-1983) Lightning Damage Base duration is 2.00 seconds Fires (4-8) Projectiles Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Fires +(0.1-2) Projectiles. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 5
Spark | Description | Spark is a lightning spell that launches unpredictable sparks that move randomly until they hit an enemy.
Spark | Immunity to Spark | Spark projectiles travel on the ground and cannot hit flying enemies, including most enemy versions of Summon Raging Spirit (e.g. Shore Map ) and Animate Weapon.
Spark | Immunity to Spark | This does not apply to The Steel Soul and Animated Weapon minions from its arena in Arsenal Map.
Spark | Spark duration and cast speed | Given the current cast speed of 0.65 seconds, and the lifetime of the Spark is 2 seconds, you can get about 18 sparks into play before the initial sparks expire. 2 seconds / 0.65 seconds = 3.07 casts or 18 sparks.
Spark | Spark duration and cast speed | To figure out the required cast speed to get more sparks into play, divide 2 by the number of casts.
Spark | Spark duration and cast speed | 4 casts ( 24 sparks ): 2 / 4 = 0.5 seconds per cast. 5 casts ( 30 sparks ): 2 / 5 = 0.4 seconds per cast.
Spark | Spark duration and cast speed | Note, you may have to decrease these cast times, as this the minimum time; the initial sparks will expire just as the new ones are leaving your position.
Spark | Spark duration and cast speed | To calculate the new desired increase in casting speed, consider that if you increase your speed by 100%, you'll cut the time in half.
Spark | Spark duration and cast speed | NewCastSpeed = OldCastSpeed / ( 1 + IncreasedSpeed ) IncreasedSpeed = OldCastSpeed / NewCastSpeed - 1
Spark | Spark duration and cast speed | As an example, if we wanted to get to 0.5 seconds, this is an % increase of (0.65/.5 - 1)*100% = 30%

Spectral Shield Throw | Description | Spectral Shield Throw is an attack skill performed with a shield. The user throws a single shield projectile forward, which releases secondary shard projectiles in random directions when it hits an enemy or terrain. Unlike most attack skills, Spectral Shield Throw deals offhand damage with a shield.
Spectral Shield Throw | Mechanics and interactions | Spectral Shield Throw has two types of projectiles: shields and shards. Using the skill causes the player to throw one shield projectile forward. Every time it collides with an enemy or terrain, it produces a set of secondary shard projectiles. Note: secondary shard projectiles created by colliding with terrain do not appear to deal damage, likely due to a bug (3.23). [1]
Spectral Shield Throw | Damage modifiers | Spectral Shield Throw gains base damage from the skill gem, and additional added damage depending on stats on the equipped shield. The Added Physical Damage per 15 Armour or Evasion Rating on Shield modifier checks local values on the shield; it is unaffected by #% increased Defences from Equipped Shield.
Spectral Shield Throw | Damage modifiers | The skill does not use local stats on the main hand weapon, and it is not an unarmed attack if the main hand is empty. [2] It only deals offhand damage.

The skill does not inherently chain, but the projectile fires more shards and the shards deal more damage.

Spectral Shield Throw of Trarthus | Description | Spectral Shield Throw of Trarthus is a attack skill. The user tosses their shield at a targeted location that pierces and deals damage to enemies in its path based on the shield's armour or evasion. When it reaches the targeted location, the shield will hover and continue to fire shard projectiles randomly until all shard projectiles are fired, with the firing rate increasing over time.

Spell Echo Support | Description | Spell Echo is a support gem that causes supported spells to repeat.
Spell Echo Support | Mechanics | A repeat is part of the original cast. It does not require a cost or a cooldown , normally has the same critical strike roll, and does not cause when you cast effects to occur a second time. [1]
Spell Echo Support | Mechanics | Spell Echo can only modify non-instant spells cast by a player or minion. It cannot support skills used by proxies or spells that trigger. [2]
Spell Echo Support | Mechanics | Repeats are not reflected in most ingame tooltip information: the time required for both the original cast and the repeat is two times the tooltip value.
Spell Echo Support | Mechanics | For the attack variant of this gem, view Multistrike Support.

Spellslinger | Description | Spellslinger is a skill gem that causes linked spells to be triggered when the user fires a projectile from their equipped wand , and adds the wand's damage to the spell. The linked spell reserves mana while active.
Spellslinger | Description | Despite the fact that this gem can support another skill gem (as Spellslinger Support ), it does not have the Support gem tag, similar to other skill-support hybrid gems.
Spellslinger | Skill functions and interactions | The gem has two functions: As Spellslinger :
Spellslinger | Skill functions and interactions | As Spellslinger Support :
Spellslinger | Skill functions and interactions | Lastly, a mana reservation overrides the supported active skill. The active skill part toggles the mana reservation on/off.
Spellslinger | Skill functions and interactions | The active skill supported by Spellslinger can be triggered when using the basic attack with a wand equipped or while using a wand-based skill with a wand equipped.
Spellslinger | Real Cooldown & Cooldown Threshold | Like other cooldowns, Spellslinger's cooldown only recovers at the start of a 33ms server frame , meaning cooldowns are effectively always rounded up to multiples of 33ms. [1] This causes cooldowns to have specific breakpoints.
Spellslinger | Real Cooldown & Cooldown Threshold | The thresholds in the table below are meant to be upper limits. If you stay below them, you do not need to increase your trigger rate, though it usually is an efficient DPS upgrade. Going above them is something you must not do. If the attack you use to trigger your spells is just slightly too fast and your spells are still on cooldown, you can lose up to almost half your DPS.

Spirit Offering | Description | Spirit Offering is an offering spell that consumes a corpse , granting increased critical strike chance and additional critical strike multiplier to the player's minions. The skill consumes other nearby corpses, increasing the duration for each corpse consumed.
Spirit Offering | Skill functions and interactions | The skill consumes a corpse and up to 4 additional corpses around it. Each additional corpse extends the base duration of the skill by 1 second, making the maximum achievable base duration 9 seconds.
Spirit Offering | Skill functions and interactions | Each player may only have one offering active at any time, unless equipping item such as Leash of Oblation. Casting an offering skill while one is already active will cancel the older one.

Split Arrow of Splitting | Description | Split Arrow of Splitting is a ranged attack skill , firing multiple arrows that split and target multiple enemies.
Split Arrow of Splitting | Description | It is a transfigured version of Split Arrow.
Split Arrow of Splitting | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version fires multiple arrows that each target different enemies instead of firing arrows in a split pattern. The number of targets it splits towards is less than the number of arrows fired by the original skill. It also has lower attack speed, higher mana cost, and higher damage effectiveness at later levels.
Split Arrow of Splitting | Skill functions and interactions | Additional Projectiles : Like all bow attacks which fire more than one projectile simultaneously, a single enemy cannot be hit by more than one projectile per attack. If multiple arrows are on course to impact any given monster , all but one will ignore that monster and travel past to potentially hit enemies behind the first one encountered.

Storm Brand | Description | Storm Brand is a spell which creates a brand on the ground. While attached, the brand sends beams of lightning damage to the attached target and enemies nearby it. This skill may chain.
Storm Brand | Skill functions and interactions | Maximum Active Brands : All brands share a limit of three (3) active brand limit. This can be split between the two brand skills (2-1) or all of one type. Passive skills can increase the number of active brands that are allowed to be active at a time.
Storm Brand | Skill functions and interactions | Maximum Brand Attachment : By default, you may only have one brand of any kind attached to a single target at any time. Taking the Keystone Runebinder allows you to place a second brand on a single target. Like the Active Brand limit these brands may either be split (1-1) or both the same type of brand.
Storm Brand | Skill functions and interactions | Spell Echo : Storm Brand's initial cast can be accelerated and repeated by Spell Echo Support , but its more cast speed multiplier does not effect the activation speed; only increased or reduced cast speed modifiers do.

Storm Burst | Description | Storm Burst is a Spell Channelling skill that creates orbs of energy which deal Physical damage , half of which is converted to Lightning damage , in an Area of effect. The orbs deal damage at an interval of 0.4 seconds over a Duration of 1.2 seconds.
Storm Burst | Skill function and interaction | Channelling Storm Burst creates a cluster of pulsing orbs that follow the mouse pointer. Enemies can be hit by multiple orbs, but the orbs spawn in a spread out pattern resembling buckshot.
Storm Burst | Skill function and interaction | Each orb created has its own duration tracker. Every 0.4 seconds, the orbs will jump to a new location around the cursor and deal Area of Effect damage. This 0.4 seconds interval is independent for each orb.
Storm Burst | Skill function and interaction | When the player stops channelling, all remaining orbs explode, dealing higher damage in a larger area based on duration remaining. The extra damage is not dealt when the orbs jump around.
Storm Burst | Skill function and interaction | Totems channelling Storm Burst will also cause the extra explosion damage if they are destroyed by enemies, if they naturally expire, or get removed due to the player casting new totems somewhere else.

Storm Call | Description | Storm Call is a spell skill which sets a marker at a location. After a short duration , the marker and all other other placed markers are struck by a bolt of lightning which deals damage in an area.
Storm Call | Skill functions and interactions | Duration : Increases and reduction to duration will affect the wait time between placing a marker and the lightning strike. Less Duration Support will therefore greatly reduce the delay between casting the skill and dealing damage.
Storm Call | Skill functions and interactions | Causing lightning strikes at any other markers you've cast : When the duration of any marker expires, lightning will strike it, then all other pending markers will expire in quick succession, each dealing damage at their location.
Storm Call | Skill functions and interactions | Chance for Lightning to Strike the Marker when Cast : Lightning striking the marker due to the quality effect causes a lightning strike to happen at cast time, in addition to the strike that will happen when the duration expires. The extra strike from the quality effect does not cause lightning strikes to happen at any other pending markers.

Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 28
Storm Rain | Description | Storm Rain is a bow attack skill that fires an arrow into the air, which sticks into the ground and deals area damage. Stuck arrows periodically fire a beam to other stuck arrows, dealing area damage between them.
Storm Rain | Skill functions and interactions | Area of Effect : Increases and reductions to AoE will affect both the area of the initial hit and the area spread of the arrows.
Storm Rain | Skill functions and interactions | Duration : Storm Rain has no duration tag and the arrows will stick in the ground until they have pulsed 4 times or until its maximum number of arrows is reached.
Storm Rain | Skill functions and interactions | Projectile : Sources of additional projectiles cause more arrows to be fired, greatly increasing the damage potential of the skill, up to a maximum of 100 arrows stuck in the ground. Both the arrow and their beams will benefit from increases to projectile damage. [1]
Storm Rain | Skill functions and interactions | Point Blank : Damage for both the arrow and their beams will increase based on the distance from where the arrow was fired to where it landed. [2]
Storm Rain | Skill functions and interactions | Hits : Each arrow stuck in the ground can hit an enemy up to 4 times with their beams. [3]
Storm Rain | Item acquisition | Storm Rain can drop anywhere.
Storm Rain | Recipes | Storm Rain can be created from the following recipes:
Storm Rain | Quest reward | This item is given as a quest reward for the following quests:
Storm Rain | Vendor reward | This item can be bought at the listed NPC vendors , after completing the following quests:
Storm Rain | Related helmet enchantments | The following helmet enchantments affect Storm Rain.
Storm Rain | Related helmet enchantments | Note that helmet enchantments are no longer obtainable since version 3.23.0 and can only be obtained in permanent leagues as already enchanted items from before this patch.

Storm Rain of the Conduit | Description | Storm Rain of the Conduit is a bow attack skill that fires an arrow into the air, which sticks into the ground and deals area damage. Stuck arrows periodically fire a beam towards you, dealing area damage between you and the arrow.
Storm Rain of the Conduit | Description | It is a transfigured version of Storm Rain.
Storm Rain of the Conduit | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version causes the arrows to fire a beam towards you instead of other arrows. It has higher damage effectiveness, but has a lower maximum number of arrows you can place on the ground.
Storm Rain of the Conduit | Skill functions and interactions | Traps / Mines : The arrows fired by traps and mines using this skill still count towards the grounded arrow limit. When the skill is triggered, the arrows' beams will arc towards where the trap was sprung/mine was detonated.
Storm Rain of the Conduit | Item acquisition | Storm Rain of the Conduit has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

Storm Rain of the Fence | Description | Storm Rain of the Fence is a bow attack skill that fires an arrow into the air, which sticks into the ground and deals area damage. Stuck arrows periodically fire a beam to other stuck arrows, dealing area damage between them.
Storm Rain of the Fence | Description | It is a transfigured version of Storm Rain.
Storm Rain of the Fence | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version limits the number of arrows you can place on the ground to two and it cannot gain additional projectiles. In exchange, the skill gets higher damage effectiveness, faster beam fire rate, increased number of beams fired per arrow, and reduced enemy stun threshold.

Requires Level 28. Channel to spread runes on the ground in a growing pattern. Deals (36-444) to (107-1332) Lightning Damage Base duration is 12.00 seconds 50% reduced Movement Speed Runes have 30% more Area of Effect per Improvement Runes deal 100% more Damage with Hits and Ailments per Improvement A Rune is Improved for each (5-24) Mana Spent Runes can be improved 3 times Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Runes have +(0.5-10)% more Area of Effect per Improvement (0.5-10)% increased Cast Speed. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 28
Stormbind | Description | Stormbind is a channelling spell that places runes on the targeted ground as the skill is channelled. These runes hinder enemies standing on it. The gem grants a sub-skill, Rune Blast , which improves runes placed by Stormbind as it is channelled, and releasing the channel detonates the rune and surrounding runes to deal lightning damage.
Stormbind | Skill functions and interactions | A level 20 Stormbind that costs 48 mana per channel tick and ticks 10 times per second improves 2 tiles per tick. It would take at least 1.5 seconds to fully improve 9 runes.

These runes hinder enemies standing on it. Summon Carrion Golem | Description | Summon Carrion Golem is a minion spell skill. It summons a Golem that grants added physical damage to your non-Golem minions. The Carrion Golem attacks with melee attacks: a leaping slam, a series of slashes, and a ground slam that sends a cascade of bones spikes. The Carrion Golem gains more damage for each allied non-Golem minion around it, up to a cap of 50% more with 10 nearby non-Golem minions.

Damage from multiple auras does not stack, but each can apply stacks of withered.

Summon Phantasm Support | Description | Summon Phantasm Support is a support gem that causes supported skills to summon a phantasm minion upon killing an enemy or hitting a rare or unique enemy.
Summon Phantasm Support | Skill functions and interactions | Linking Summon Phantasm Support to an active skill causes it to become a skill that summons minions. This does not add the minion gem tag to the skill gem. [1] It does enable supportability by gems compatible with phantasms— projectile spell -casting minions. Compatible stats may also affect non-minion aspects of the skill. Because the phantasms' projectiles pierce all targets, they cannot chain or fork.
Summon Phantasm Support | Skill functions and interactions | The level of Summon Phantasm Support will always determine the level of the Phantasms. [2] Summoned phantasms have a base cast rate of 0.85, 40% Elemental Resistances , and 20% Chaos Resistance.
Summon Phantasm Support | Skill functions and interactions | Multiple skills can be supported by Summon Phantasm Support , but the total number of Phantasms is limited by the highest level support gem.
Summon Phantasm Support | Skill functions and interactions | This support gem can support Summon Phantasm , a related skill. Because the skill itself cannot hit or summon non-phantasm minions, it cannot trigger the support gem's effects, but it does add the Maximum # Summoned Phantasm stat to its default maximum phantasms.
Summon Phantasm Support | Active skill gem | Summon Phantasm Support is a support gem; it does not summon phantasms, it causes the supported skill to summon phantasms. Accordingly, that skill's generic minion bonuses are intended to apply to phantasms. [3] However , potentially due to a bug, [4] this currently only applies to bonuses added by other support gems. Minion bonuses inherent to the supported skill (e.g. Minions deal/have/convert. on Herald of Purity , Absolution , Blink Arrow , Mirror Arrow , Summon Raging Spirit of Enormity ) currently do not apply to phantasms. [5] [6]
Summon Phantasm Support | Active skill gem | While similar effects can be expected to not affect phantasms, functional bonuses have existed in the past (e.g. Minions are Created with maximum Frenzy Charges formerly on an alternate quality of Raise Spectre ), so ingame confirmation is recommended.

Summon Raging Spirit | Description | Summon Raging Spirit (commonly referred to as SRS ) is a minion spell that temporarily summons a flying flaming skull that charges at enemies and attacks them.
Summon Raging Spirit | Item acquisition | Summon Raging Spirit can drop anywhere.
Summon Raging Spirit | Recipes | Summon Raging Spirit can be created from the following recipes:
Summon Raging Spirit | Quest reward | This item is given as a quest reward for the following quests:
Summon Raging Spirit | Vendor reward | This item can be bought at the listed NPC vendors , after completing the following quests:
Summon Raging Spirit | Sustaining Maximum Spirits | The attributes that will improve max spirit count are Cast Speed and Skill Duration. Due to their short duration and very high maximum of 20, sustaining the maximum number of Raging Spirits is challenging without the use of a support gem.
Summon Raging Spirit | Sustaining Maximum Spirits | Unleash Support , In conjunction with some improvements to Skill Duration from the tree, notably Enduring Bond , will allow you to sustain the maximum number of spirits relatively easily, but at the opportunity cost of another damage improving support.
Summon Raging Spirit | Sustaining Maximum Spirits | Between Unleash Support } and Spell Echo Support } The former is generally preferred as it allows the caster to remain mobile, and its damage penalty only applies "when Reoccurring" so immediately stops applying to the created Raging Spirits.
Summon Raging Spirit | Sustaining Maximum Spirits | If we assume: D percent increased Duration C percent increased Cast Speed E percent More Cast Speed \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}{\begin{aligned}D&{\text{ percent increased Duration }}\\C&{\text{ percent increased Cast Speed }}\\E&{\text{ percent More Cast Speed }}\\\end{aligned}}
Summon Raging Spirit | Sustaining Maximum Spirits | And in a scenario where you can continuously cast Summon Raging Spirits without stopping, then the formula for how many spirits are on the screen at a time will be:
Summon Raging Spirit | Sustaining Maximum Spirits | 5.0 ∗ ( 1 + D 100 ) 0.5 ( 1 + C 100 ) ∗ ( 1 + E 100 ) {\color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}5.0*(1+{D \over 100}) \over {0.5 \over {(1+{C \over 100})*(1+{E \over 100})}}} which simplifies to: 10.0 ∗ ( 1 + D 100 ) ∗ ( 1 + C 100 ) ∗ ( 1 + E 100 ) \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}10.0*(1+{D \over 100})*(1+{C \over 100})*(1+{E \over 100})
Summon Raging Spirit | Sustaining Maximum Spirits | And then round up to get the maximum number of spirits reached (with a maximum of 20).
Summon Raging Spirit | Sustaining Maximum Spirits | Example calculation: If you have 45% increased skill duration, 76% increased cast speed, and 70% more cast speed, you would have 10 ∗ 1.45 ∗ 1.76 ∗ 1.70 = 43.384 > 20 \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}10*1.45*1.76*1.70=43.384>20 , which means that if you continuously cast, your spirits will despawn earlier than necessary. This means you can spend several seconds per cycle on other things such as dodging, casting curses, or moving in between spirit castings.
Summon Raging Spirit | Sustaining Maximum Spirits | Formula for Spell Totem Support (30% reduced cast speed, Spell Echo's More Cast Speed doesn't apply): 7.0 ∗ ( 1 + D 100 ) ∗ ( 1 + C 100 ) \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}7.0*(1+{D \over 100})*(1+{C \over 100})
Summon Raging Spirit | Scaling Damage | Due to their relatively high effectiveness for added damage, especially considering their overwhelming numbers, Raging Spirits scale superlatively with sources of "flat" added damage.
Summon Raging Spirit | Scaling Damage | Notable sources of flat added elemental damage include: Added Cold Damage Support , Added Lightning Damage Support , Immolate Support , Wrath and Anger
Summon Raging Spirit | Scaling Damage | Due to the Raging Spirit's fairly low base damage, its remarkably easy to essentially "convert" their damage type to something else by adding enough flat damage of that type.
Summon Raging Spirit | Scaling Damage | A popular strategy using these two points above is to add a huge amount of flat added Chaos damage to the Raging Spirits and then have them inflict Poison which allows for extremely efficient scaling of their damage per second through effects like Wither and the Temporal Chains curse.
Summon Raging Spirit | Scaling Damage | Notable sources of flat added Chaos damage include: Added Chaos Damage Support especially from The Covenant , Chance to Poison Support and the special Envy aura, which can be found at its highest level from either Severed in Sleep or United in Dream.
Summon Raging Spirit | Scaling Damage | When not using Severed in Sleep , Wither chance for the spirits can also found with the Withering Touch Support which also adds a significant amount of Chaos damage but as a percentage of the Spirit's base Physical damage.
Summon Raging Spirit | Scaling Damage | Ghastly Eye Jewels also include mods for flat added damage of each type, as well as chance to poison.

Summon Raging Spirit of Enormity | Infobox | Spell , Minion , Duration , Fire Level: (1-20) Cost: (7-26) Mana Cast Time: 0.80 sec. Requires Level 4. Summons a short-lived flaming skull that rushes at nearby enemies and attacks them rapidly, converting all its physical damage to fire. Enemies will not directly engage these spirits, and can pass through them. Maximum 6 Summoned Raging Spirits Base duration is 5.00 seconds Minions have (50-88)% more Maximum Life Minions' Hits are always Critical Strikes Minions have +(40-78)% to Critical Strike Multiplier Minions cannot Taunt Enemies Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: (0.5-10)% more Minion Movement Speed. Summon Raging Spirit of Enormity | Description | Summon Raging Spirit of Enormity is a minion spell that temporarily summons a flying flaming skull that charges at enemies and attacks them.
Summon Raging Spirit of Enormity | Description | It is a transfigured version of Summon Raging Spirit.
Summon Raging Spirit of Enormity | Changes from default version | This transfigured version gives the minions more maximum life, makes their hits always deal critical strikes , and grants them additional crit multi, but has a greatly reduced maximum minion limit. The gem quality stat gives the Raging Spirits movement speed instead.
Summon Raging Spirit of Enormity | Skill functions and interactions | Minions' Hits are always Critical Strikes : applies to all Raging Spirit hits, including Minion Instability. This stat does not apply to hits from Phantasms summoned by this skill.
Summon Raging Spirit of Enormity | Item acquisition | Summon Raging Spirit of Enormity has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

Summon Reaper | Infobox | Physical , Minion , Spell Level: (1-20) Cost: (15-28) Mana Cooldown Time: 4.00 sec Can Store 1 Use(s) Cast Time: 0.60 sec. Requires Level 28. Summons a powerful Reaper which uses a variety of slashing area attacks. The Reaper's presence weakens your other minions, and it will consume them to temporarily empower and heal itself. Using this skill while the Reaper is already summoned causes it to dash to the targeted location and perform a powerful attack. Maximum 1 Summoned Reaper Minion's Attacks have 50% chance to inflict Bleeding Minion deals (30-87)% more Damage with Bleeding Reaper causes your Non-Reaper Minions to deal (20-29)% less Damage Reaper causes your Non-Reaper Minions to have (20-29)% less Maximum Life Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Minion's Attacks have (1-20)% chance to inflict Bleeding. Summon Reaper | Description | Summon Reaper is a minion spell that summons a Reaper minion that attacks with a variety of slashes and can apply bleeding. Your other minions are weaker while you have a Reaper summoned. The Reaper will consume your targetable minions to heal and empower itself occasionally.
Summon Reaper | Skill functions and interactions | The Reaper has 7 separate abilities it can use. At every idle frame the reaper will decide what to do next based on its own internal logic. The Reaper's abilities are as follows:
Summon Reaper | Skill functions and interactions | The Consume skills kill one allied damageable minion , then recovers 50% of the Reaper's life and applies a buff to the Reaper for a base duration of 6 seconds. The Reaper has 2 versions of this skill. Each version has its own cooldown. The buff grants the following effects: [1] [2]
Summon Reaper | Skill functions and interactions | There are no variations in weighting based on minion type, etc, but the Reaper cannot eat "immortal" minions which can't be damaged, such as skitterbots , as they're invalid targets for the eating action, and cannot eat untargetable minions like Summon Raging Spirit because they can't be targeted. [3] Phantasms and Animate Guardian can be consumed; in the case of Animate Guardian, consuming the Guardian will cause you to be unable to summon the Guardian in that area instance.
Summon Reaper | Skill functions and interactions | The Reaper dual wields and alternates between main hand and off hand for its attacks except for the player-activated dash slash, which is always main hand [4]. This is relevant when the player is using Destructive Link with the Minion Defense Mastery to target the Reaper.
Summon Reaper | Item acquisition | Summon Reaper can drop anywhere.

Summon Reaper of Eviscerating | Infobox | Physical , Minion , Spell Level: (1-20) Cost: (19-35) Mana Cast Time: 1.00 sec. Requires Level 28. Summons a powerful Reaper which passively follows you without attacking enemies. The Reaper's presence weakens your other minions, and it will consume them to temporarily empower and heal itself. Using this skill while the Reaper is already summoned causes it to dash to the targeted location and perform a powerful attack. Cannot be triggered, supported by Spell Echo or Unleash, or used by Totems, Traps, or Mines. Maximum 1 Summoned Reaper Minion's Attacks have 50% chance to inflict Bleeding Minion deals (30-87)% more Damage with Bleeding Reaper causes your Non-Reaper Minions to deal (20-29)% less Damage Reaper causes your Non-Reaper Minions to have (20-29)% less Maximum Life Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Minions have (2-40)% chance to Aggravate Bleeding on targets they Hit with Attacks. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop restricted Drop level: 28 This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.
Summon Reaper of Eviscerating | Description | Summon Reaper of Eviscerating is a minion spell that summons a Reaper minion you can command to have it attack with slashes. Your other minions are weaker while you have a Reaper summoned. The Reaper will consume your targetable minions to heal and empower itself occasionally.
Summon Reaper of Eviscerating | Description | It is a transfigured version of Summon Reaper.
Summon Reaper of Eviscerating | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, the Reaper summoned from this version of the skill does not attack automatically. The skill's dash command ability no longer has a cooldown, and it has a longer cast time and higher mana cost. This skill cannot be used with traps , mines , totems , or supported with Spell Echo Support , Unleash Support , or trigger effects. Quality grants chance to aggravate bleeding.
Summon Reaper of Eviscerating | Item acquisition | Summon Reaper of Eviscerating has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

Summon Reaper of Revenants | Changes from default version | At level 13, your maximum number of summoned reapers is raised to 4. At level 26, this number goes up to 5.

Summon Skitterbots | Infobox | Trap , Mine , Spell , Minion , Cold , Lightning , AoE , Aura Level: (1-20) Reservation: 35% Mana Cooldown Time: 1.00 sec Can Store 1 Use(s) Cast Time: Instant. Requires Level 16. Summon a Chilling Skitterbot and a Shocking Skitterbot, which will trigger your traps and detonate your mines. Mines detonated by Skitterbots will re-arm and can then be detonated again. The Skitterbots grant you more trap and mine damage, and cannot be targeted or damaged. (0-76)% increased Minion Movement Speed Minions have (0-19)% increased Chill Effect Minions have (0-19)% increased Shock Effect Maximum 1 Summoned Skitterbot of each type Chilling Skitterbot's Aura Chills Enemies near it Shocking Skitterbot's Aura Shocks Enemies near it Skitterbots grant 10% more Trap and Mine Damage Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: (2-40)% increased Minion Movement Speed. Summon Skitterbots | Description | Summon Skitterbots is a skill which reserves mana to summon two invulnerable Skitterbot minions. The Skitterbots search for traps and mines you have laid and trigger/detonate some of them if an enemy is within targeting range. Mines detonated by Skitterbots are re-armed to detonate a second time. One Skitterbot has an aura that chills nearby enemies and the other has an aura that shocks nearby enemies. Skitterbots grant more trap and mine damage to the owner.

Summon Stone Golem | Description | Summon Stone Golem is a minion spell skill. It summons a Golem that grants life regeneration and increased defences to the player while active. The Stone Golem can use a rolling movement skill and a taunting AoE ground slam in addition to its melee attacks. It has the largest life pool of all available Golems, but, unlike the others, it does not have any energy shield.

Sunder | Infobox | Attack , AoE , Slam , Melee Level: (1-20) Cost: (8-13) Mana Attack Speed: 75% of base Attack Damage: (273-629)% of base Effectiveness of Added Damage: (273-629)%. Requires Level 12. Slams the ground, creating a slow wave of churning terrain that damages enemies in a sequence of areas in front of you. A number of enemies hit by the wave will release a shockwave, damaging other enemies around them. Using the skill again will stop the previous wave. Requires a Mace, Sceptre, Axe, Staff or Unarmed. Sunder | Description | Sunder is a melee slam attack skill that creates a slow-moving area of effect wave forward. Enemies hit by the wave release circular shock waves that further damage nearby enemies. The wave continues to move past its minimum distance until a new wave is created. Requires an axe , mace , scepter , staff , or unarmed.

Sunder of Earthbreaking | Description | Sunder of Earthbreaking is a melee slam attack skill that creates an area of effect wave forward. Enemies hit by the wave release circular shock waves that further damage nearby enemies. Requires an axe , mace , scepter , staff , or unarmed.
Sunder of Earthbreaking | Description | It is a transfigured version of Sunder.
Sunder of Earthbreaking | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version has a much lower delay for creating damaging areas and you can have more than one wave up at a time. The skill has more area of effect and each wave creates more areas (effectively longer range), but each shockwave hits fewer enemies and it doesn't gain added radius for each subsequent area. It has higher base attack speed, lower mana cost, and less damage effectiveness. Gem quality adds additional areas instead.
Sunder of Earthbreaking | Item acquisition | Sunder of Earthbreaking has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

Sunder of Trarthus | Description | Sunder of Trarthus is a melee slam attack skill that creates a slow-moving wave forward. Enemies hit by the wave release circular shockwaves that further damage nearby enemies. The wave will continue to travel, and can re-aim towards an enemy partway through. Requires an axe , mace , sceptre , staff , or unarmed.
Sunder of Trarthus | Description | It is a Trarthan version of Sunder.
Sunder of Trarthus | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this Trarthan version has less damage and damage effectiveness and its wave damages fewer areas and has a smaller bonus to radius per previous area. Instead, it has a shorter delay between areas, and can re-target towards an enemy in range up to two times, changing the direction of the wave.

Swift Assembly Support | Description | Swift Assembly Support is a support gem which grants the supported trap or mine skill a chance to throw additional traps or mines.
Swift Assembly Support | Skill functions and interactions | Supported Skills throw 0.6 additional Trap or Mine on average with level 20 of the gem (18% + 2x12% + 3x6% = 60%). With Architect's Hand equipped and "5% chance to throw up to 4 additional Traps" Trap Mastery allocated, this increases to 1.04 additional Traps on average with level 20 of the gem (18% + 2x12% + 3x6% + 4x11% = 104%). The gem's advanced description specifies (Throwing Mines takes 10% more time for each additional Mine thrown).

Tectonic Slam | Description | Tectonic Slam is an attack skill that slams the ground in front of the player, dealing area of effect damage in a cone, with the area scaling with your Endurance charges.
Tectonic Slam | Skill functions and interactions | Endurance charge consumption effect : When you have a number of Endurance charges greater than your minimum endurance charges, every third slam will consume one. If your attack repeats, such as by using Multistrike Support , every slam counts towards the counter. [1]
Tectonic Slam | Skill functions and interactions | The conditional modifiers per endurance charge, i.e., fissure branching chance per Endurance Charge and more Area of Effect per Endurance Charge , affect each slam and are independent of whether or not a charge is consumed or can be consumed. [2]

Tectonic Slam of Cataclysm | Description | It is a transfigured version of Tectonic Slam.

The Mirages will benefit from all of the stats on the skill by consuming their own charges.

Tempest Shield | Description | Tempest Shield is a spell that reserves mana to empower your shield, granting spell block chance and immunity to shock. While active, whenever you block, you deal damage to the enemy that hit you with an arc of lightning that chains to nearby enemies.
Tempest Shield | Skill Functions and Interactions | Damage on Blocking: This spell will damage the attacker whenever you successfully block any direct attack or spell.
Tempest Shield | Skill Functions and Interactions | Chaining : The damage on block chains from the attacker to the nearest enemy in a range. The number of times the lightning damage chains can be increased by supporting Tempest Shield with Chain Support , by equipping Mjölner or equipping a helmet with a legacy Tempest Shield chains an additional x times enchantment.
Tempest Shield | Skill Functions and Interactions | Necromantic Aegis has no interaction with Tempest Shield. The added spell block chance and spell lightning damage to enemies on block will still apply to the player.

Temporal Chains | Infobox | Spell , AoE , Duration , Curse , Hex Level: (1-20) Cost: (16-33) Mana Cast Time: 0.50 sec. Requires Level 24. Curses all enemies in an area, lowering their action speed and making other effects on them expire more slowly. Base duration is (5.00-8.80) seconds +(0-1) metre to radius 50% less Effect of Curse against Players Cursed Normal and Magic Enemies have (15-29)% less Action Speed Other effects on Cursed enemies expire 25% slower Cursed Rare and Unique Enemies have (9-18)% less Action Speed Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: (1-20)% increased Curse Duration. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 24
Temporal Chains | Description | Temporal Chains is a spell that curses all targets in an area, reducing their action speed and making all temporary effects on them expire more slowly.
Temporal Chains | Skill functions and interactions | Action speed : Temporal Chains reduces the action speed of the target.
Temporal Chains | Skill functions and interactions | Slower expiration rate of effects : Temporal Chains causes duration effects to expire more slowly, including debuffs and Brands , but not the duration of the curse itself. For the formula, see expiration rate. At the default 25% slower expiry, effects last 33% longer. At the maximum 75% slower expiry, effects last 300% longer.
Temporal Chains | Skill functions and interactions | Area of effect According to last announced value in version 2.0.0 patch notes, the base radius of level 1 Temporal Chains skill is 2.2 metres. A level 20 skill will +1 metre to radius.
Temporal Chains | Skill functions and interactions | Self-curse :
Temporal Chains | Skill functions and interactions | Characters can curse themselves with Temporal Chains using Shackles of the Wretched or Rotblood Promise to extend duration buffs such as Immortal Call , or Boneshatter or Headhunter buff stacks. Due to the inherent 50% less effect of curses on players , the default Temporal Chains gem will only provide 12.5% slower expiry (effects last 14% longer). To reach the 75% slower expiry cap (effects last 300% longer), the player needs a total of 500% increased curse effect (or 520% with Rotblood Promise).
Temporal Chains | Skill functions and interactions | Runegraft of the Warp grants Buffs on you expire 30% slower , reducing the amount of increased curse effect required to reach the cap to 260% (or just 100% with Shaper's Presence).
Temporal Chains | Skill functions and interactions | Temporal Chains' downside (reduced action speed ) can be countered with increased action speed stats, or completely negated by Action Speed cannot be modified to below base value (e.g. Kaom's Roots , Juggernaut 's Unstoppable , or an Animate Guardian with Garb of the Ephemeral ) or Your Action Speed is at least #% of base value (e.g. Trickster's One Step Ahead , Scourge modifiers ).

They all belongs to the same mod domain no.5.

Temporal Rift | Description | Temporal Rift is a skill gem. It reserves mana to apply a buff that grants immunity to Temporal Chains and leaves afterimages in your wake, lingering for up to 4 seconds. Using the skill again will teleport you to the oldest afterimage, as well as reset your Life , Mana and Energy Shield to the values they had when that afterimage was created.
Temporal Rift | Mechanics | As a thematic exception, Temporal Rift is allowed to bypass effects that prevent recovery such as Vaal Pact , or debuffs like Maven's Punishment and Searing Rune. [1]

Thunderstorm | Infobox | Attack , AoE , Duration , Lightning , Bow , Projectile , Trigger Level: (1-20) Cost: (7-10) Mana Attack Damage: (112-125)% of base Effectiveness of Added Damage: (112-125)%. Requires Level 28. Fires an arrow into the air to land at a targeted location. On impact, deals area damage and creates a thunderstorm which blinds enemies within it. Entering the thunderstorm will cause it to gain stages, and leaving it will cause it to dissipate. Leaving the storm while it is at maximum stages will cause it to explode, dealing damage and triggering Thunderburst. You must use this skill yourself, it cannot be used by Totems, Traps, Mines, or other objects that use skills for you. 60% of Physical Damage Converted to Lightning Damage Base duration is 10.00 seconds Fires 1 Arrow Modifiers to number of Projectiles do not apply to this Skill Base radius is (1.8-2.3) metres Maximum 3 Thunderstorms Thunderstorm gains one stage per 0.25 seconds you are in it, up to a maximum of 4 Thunderstorm has 30% more Area of Effect per stage Thunderstorm dissipates if you leave it before reaching maximum Stages, or 1.5 seconds after reaching maximum Stages Explosion deals 300% more Damage Projectiles Pierce all Targets 60% of Physical Damage Converted to Lightning Damage Base duration is 3.00 seconds Fires 5 Projectiles Fires Projectiles in a circle 40% more Area Damage Base radius is 1.5 metres Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Fires +(0.1-2) Projectiles. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 28
Thunderstorm | Description | Thunderstorm is a bow attack that fires an arrow into the air, dealing area damage and creating a thunderstorm where it lands. The thunderstorm blinds enemies, and gains stages when the player enters it, exploding when the player leaves it after reaching maximum stages. Exploding storms deal area damage, and trigger Thunderburst.
Thunderstorm | Description | Thunderburst is a second skill granted by this gem. When triggered, it fires a circle of piercing projectiles from the exploding storm, which deal area damage at the end of their duration.
Thunderstorm | Description | Note: Due to technical limitations, this page's infobox displays gem stats belonging to both Thunderstorm and Thunderburst. These are distinct skills and do not necessarily share the same stats or support gem compatibility.
Thunderstorm | Skill functions and interactions | Thunderstorm gains one stage per 0.25 seconds you are in it, up to a maximum of 4 : By default, Thunderstorm takes 1 second to reach max stages. However, the 0.25 second interval is scaled by global attack speed modifiers [1]. Weapon local attack time does not affect this interval.
Thunderstorm | Skill functions and interactions | Projectile : All hits by Thunderstorm and Thunderburst are projectile damage, including the storm explosion.
Thunderstorm | Skill functions and interactions | Projectile count : Modifiers to projectile count only apply to Thunderburst, not Thunderstorm. Notably, Thunderburst projectiles will not scale with arrow count, even if used with a bow.
Thunderstorm | Skill functions and interactions | By default, Thunderburst projectiles pierce all targets. If pierce is disabled, such as through Kineticism , and the projectile is destroyed before its full duration due to hitting a target, the explosion will not occur. [ confirmation needed ] Thunderburst projectiles can split , and can hit an enemy it hit previously again immediately after splitting.
Thunderstorm | Skill functions and interactions | Thunderburst projectiles move in random directions in a zig-zag motion. Unlike Spark which can hit enemies after a fixed interval, Thunderburst projeciles appear to be only able to hit the same enemy once until they bounce off a wall or obstacle [2].
Thunderstorm | Skill functions and interactions | Proxies : This skill cannot be used by proxies , such as ballistas or Mirage Archer.
Thunderstorm | Skill functions and interactions | Weapon swap : It is possible to weapon swap to a different (even non- bow ) weapon while a Thunderstorm is gaining stages; doing so will not cause the Thunderstorm to disappear. Instead, the Thunderstorm explosion will deal damage and apply effects based on the current weapon, but will not create Thunderburst projectiles if the weapon is not a bow.

Tornado | Infobox | Spell , Duration , Physical , AoE , Orb Level: (1-20) Cost: (15-25) Mana Cast Time: 0.75 sec Critical Strike Chance: 5.00% Effectiveness of Added Damage: (55-95)%. Requires Level 34. Create a Tornado that hinders and repeatedly damages enemies around it. It will move forward for a duration, during which your projectiles can collide with it to deal damage as though it was an enemy. Then it will chase down enemies for a secondary duration, and reflect a portion of the damage it took from your projectiles to them in addition to its own damage. Deals (31-414) to (46-620) Physical Damage Base duration is 1.50 seconds Base secondary duration is 4.00 seconds Deals Damage every 0.25 seconds During initial Duration, can be hit by your Projectiles up to 20 times Reflects 10% of Damage Taken to Enemies Enemies in range are Hindered Tornado has (0-57)% increased Movement Speed Maximum 1 Tornado Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Tornado has (1-20)% increased Movement Speed. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 34
Tornado | Description | Tornado is a spell skill that creates a tornado which repeatedly damages enemies around it. It will move forward for a duration, during which your projectiles can collide with it to deal damage as though it was an enemy. Then it will chase down enemies for a secondary duration, and reflect a portion of the damage it took from projectiles, in addition to its own spell damage.
Tornado | Skill functions and interactions | Durations : During its primary duration , Tornado deals spell damage and can be hit by projectiles. During its secondary duration, Tornado deals both spell damage and reflected damage. Skill effect duration modifiers affect both durations.
Tornado | Skill functions and interactions | Collisions : Tornado can only be hit by projectiles that can collide with enemies, meaning skills like Rolling Magma or Cremation cannot contribute to Tornado's reflected damage. [1] Similar to Hydrosphere , projectiles that collide with Tornado may create their on-collision effects, such as Lightning Arrow 's lightning bolts, or Kinetic Blast 's explosions.
Tornado | Skill functions and interactions | Damage taken : Tornado is considered to have zero resistances , evasion , or damage reduction. It cannot be debuffed , including by auras or ground effects , though stats like resistance penetration can still modify the damage calculation. [2] Tornado is never considered an enemy on Full Life [3] nor an enemy that cannot have Life Leeched from them. [4]
Tornado | Skill functions and interactions | Damage reflection : Reflected damage dealt by Tornado cannot be further scaled by the player's offensive stats, including penetration. [5] It can still be modified by lowering enemies' mitigation through debuffs such as exposure or curses. The damage is no longer considered projectile damage, and cannot benefit from stats like Sniper's Mark 's Cursed enemies take % increased Damage from Projectile Hits.
Tornado | Item acquisition | Tornado can drop anywhere.

Tornado of Elemental Turbulence | Description | Tornado of Elemental Turbulence is a spell skill that creates multiple elemental tornadoes that repeatedly damage enemies around it. It will chase down enemies for a duration.
Tornado of Elemental Turbulence | Description | It is a transfigured version of Tornado.
Tornado of Elemental Turbulence | Changes from default version | Unlike the original skill, this transfigured version does not reflect a portion of the damage from your projectiles that hit it; instead, it only deals spell physical damage converted to elemental damage and Hinders nearby enemies. It allows for more tornadoes to be active simultaneously, and also has lower base damage and damage effectiveness.
Tornado of Elemental Turbulence | Skill functions and interactions | Tornadoes created by this skill cannot be hit.
Tornado of Elemental Turbulence | Skill functions and interactions | Tornado of Elemental Turbulence (ToET) shares a Tornado limit with the base version of Tornado in a fashion similar to Totems that allow for additional to be placed. You may only have 1 Tornado active at a time from the base Tornado skill and it's limit of 1 will override and destroy any existing other Tornadoes that are active (including ones from ToET). If you start by summoning a base tornado, you may summon 2 additional ToET tornadoes. Casting ToET again will replace the first tornado skill summoned with a new ToET version regardless of if it came from the base Tornado skill or ToET.
Tornado of Elemental Turbulence | Skill functions and interactions | Combat Focus jewels prevent specific element Tornadoes from being chosen.
Tornado of Elemental Turbulence | Item acquisition | Tornado of Elemental Turbulence has restrictions on where or how it can drop. This item is a transfigured gem. It can be obtained through the use of the Divine Font in the Lord's Labyrinth.

Toxic Rain | Description | Toxic Rain is a bow attack skill that fires multiple arrows in the air, which rain down on the targeted area. Each arrow deals area damage where it lands, and creates a spore pod for a duration. Spore pods apply a chaos damage over time and movement speed debuff to each nearby enemy, which can stack from multiple pods. Spore pods burst at the end of their duration, dealing area damage.
Toxic Rain | Skill functions and interactions | Toxic Rain has three damaging components: the arrow rain, damage over time from spore pods, and spore pods bursting. All three components deal area damage. The second component deals damage over time, and accordingly does not hit , cannot apply on-hit effects, does not need accuracy , and cannot benefit from with this weapon modifiers. Unlike typical damage over time debuffs, enemies can take damage from multiple spore pods.
Toxic Rain | Skill functions and interactions | Additional projectile modifiers increase the number of spore pods each attack creates, but also the area the arrows are spread over. Additional projectiles primarily increase coverage, not single-target damage against regular size enemies.
Toxic Rain | Skill functions and interactions | Area of effect modifiers affect all of the skill's damaging areas, but also the spread of arrows (and therefore the pods). Increasing area of effect improves coverage, but can also improve spore pod overlap on a single target at specific breakpoints.
Toxic Rain | Optimal Single Target Setup | Due to area of effect breakpoints, optimal single target damage can be achieved by increasing area of effect by at least 37%, but not more than 43%. [1] [2] Potential sources include:

Trap Support | Infobox | Support , Trap Icon: T Level: (1-20) Cost & Reservation Multiplier: 120%. Requires Level 8. Supports spells, or attacks that use bows or wands. Trap Support | Description | Trap Support is a support gem. When linked to a supportable skill gem , using that skill will cause the player to throw a trap instead of attacking or casting. The trap is a proxy that targets the first enemy to enter its trigger radius.
Trap Support | Skill functions and interactions | Traps and Totems : When Trap is linked in combination with the Blastchain Mine Support and Spell Totem Support or Ballista Totem Support gems, the result is a skill that throws a mine. When the mine is detonated, it throws a trap. When the trap is triggered, it summons a totem , which then can cast the linked skill. Players cannot create traps that throw mines, or totems that throw mines or traps.
Trap Support | Skill functions and interactions | Repeating : A spell supported by Trap Support cannot also be supported by Spell Echo Support. The gem combination will not result in a spell trap that repeats its cast. Traps can repeat supportable attack skills with Fatal Flourish , however.

Trauma Support | Description | Trauma Support is a support gem which causes strike attack skills to grant stacks of Trauma on hit which causes attacks to deal added Physical Damage, but also inflicts escalating amounts of physical damage.
Trauma Support | Description | Trauma Support can only be used with Axes, Maces, Sceptres or Staves. Trauma Support cannot support totems or minions. [1]
Trauma Support | Skill functions and interactions | Boneshatter cannot be supported by Trauma Support. Skills that gain bonuses based on your Trauma stacks do not care about the source of that trauma; for example, if you use Boneshatter to generate trauma, skills supported by Trauma Support will still be granted added Physical Damage even if they themselves did not generate the trauma.
Trauma Support | Gaining Trauma | Hitting an enemy with Boneshatter or strike skill (other than Boneshatter) supported by Trauma Support will grant you a Trauma stack, giving more Damage or added Physical Damage respectively. However, you will also take Physical Damage per Trauma stack upon gaining a new one. Only one stack can be obtained per attack, regardless of the number of enemies hit. Repeated attacks from Multistrike Support will also not grant additional Trauma stacks and only inflicts self-damage every 3rd hit. This can be used to reduce the gain and impact of Trauma for builds incapable of tanking high stacks of Trauma.
Trauma Support | Gaining Trauma | Each Trauma stack counts as an individual buff , with each Trauma stack having its own duration. The (now drop-disabled ) Mantra of Flames Jewel therefore grants a damage bonus for each stack of Trauma. Linking Boneshatter or a Trauma skill with More Duration Support / Efficacy Support , cursing yourself/being cursed with Temporal Chains , or gaining skill effect duration elsewhere will increase the duration of each Trauma buff stack, reducing the need for high attack speed to maintain high Trauma.
Trauma Support | Damage taken from Trauma | With a level 20 gem and N current Trauma stacks, you will take (194 ( Boneshatter ) /497 ( Boneshatter of Complex Trauma ) 146 ( Trauma Support ) ) *(N+1) physical damage (before damage mitigation ) upon your next attack.
Trauma Support | Damage taken from Trauma | The damage taken per Trauma stack hits , enabling when-hit effects and Cast when Damage Taken Support. It also allows your armour and/or physical damage reduction to prevent some of the physical damage taken. However, the damage cannot be blocked or evaded via evasion rating/ dodge. "Avoid damage from hits" effects (e.g. Elusive ) do work, however.
Trauma Support | Damage taken from Trauma | Damage conversion modifiers do not affect the damage from Trauma. However, damage taken as modifiers such as #% of Physical Damage from Hits taken as Cold Damage will shift the damage you take, allowing mitigation via elemental resistances. Additionally, it is possible to chill , freeze , or shock yourself this way; it is not possible to self-inflict damaging ailments unless you have a source of All Damage from Hits can Ignite/Poison you such as Mokou's Embrace.
Trauma Support | Damage taken from Trauma | Recoup is a valuable stat to have due to the escalating damage quickly exceeding and overpowering other forms of recovery, whereas Recoup will always recover the same percentage of damage received.
Trauma Support | Item acquisition | Trauma Support can drop anywhere.

Trauma Support | Vendor reward | This item can be bought at the listed NPC vendors , after completing the following quests:
Trinity Support | Infobox | Support Icon: y Level: (1-20) Cost & Reservation Multiplier: 140%. Requires Level 18. Supports skills that hit enemies, causing them to gain bonuses while you have resonance of all three elements, and to grant resonance of elements other than the highest one in a hit. There is a maximum of 50 Resonance for each Element. Cannot support triggered skills, instant skills, orb skills, brands, skills with a reservation, or skills used by totems, traps, or mines. Cannot modify the skills of minions. Hits with Supported Skills for which the highest Damage Type is Elemental cause you to gain 25 Resonance of other Elements per second for 2 seconds Lose 5 Resonance per second of each Element you are not gaining Supported Skills deal 3% more Elemental Damage per 5 of lowest Resonance Supported Skills penetrate (7-16)% Elemental Resistances while each Resonance is at least 25 Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Supported Skills deal (0.5-10)% increased Elemental Damage. This is a Support Gem. It does not grant a bonus to your character, but to skills in sockets connected to it. Place into an item socket connected to a socket containing the Skill Gem you wish to augment. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 18
Trinity Support | Description | Trinity Support is an intelligence and dexterity support gem.
Trinity Support | Skill functions and interactions | When an enemy is hit by a supported skill, and the highest type of damage taken is elemental ( fire , cold , or lightning ), Resonance will be gained of the types that are not the highest. For example, if fire damage is highest, then cold and lightning Resonance are gained. Resonance is indicated by a 3-sector circle at the top left of the screen, with fire (orange) in top right, cold (white) at the bottom, and lightning (blue) in the top left. When all 3 sectors are full and a triangle is visible in the center, Trinity support is functioning at full effectiveness.
Trinity Support | Skill functions and interactions | If two elements are tied for the highest, only the third element will gain Resonance. [1] If all three elements are equal, or the highest damage type is physical and/or chaos , no Resonance will be gained at all. [2] [3] If multiple Trinity supports are used, all supported skills share Resonance. Only hits can generate Resonance. Damage over time (e.g. Ignite ) will not generate Resonance. Minions cannot gain Resonance. The gem's advanced description specifies (Maximum 50 Resonance of each Element). [4] There is currently no way to increase this maximum.
Trinity Support | Skill functions and interactions | Examples :
Trinity Support | Balancing Resonance | Hits do not need to inflict all three elements to gain Resonance. However, it is recommended to use at least two elements with overlapping damage ranges to allow Resonance gain to alternate. If only two sectors remain full, this indicates the skill may have too high of a damage range for a single element (e.g. dealing significantly more fire damage than cold or lightning). Some ways to fix this can include adding more damage of the lower elements, reducing modifiers to the highest element, or changing conversion ratios. Note that flat lightning damage usually has a significantly wider range of minimum and maximum damage; this highly variable damage range can make alternating Resonances easier. Flat lightning damage buffs such as Smite or Sigil of Power can also help achieve an overlap.
Trinity Support | Balancing Resonance | Even when damage ranges are identical, such as equal conversion , keep in mind that each damage type rolls the damage of each hit independently (see receiving damage ), which will result in natural variation between which element is higher. The only exception is in the case of range-less damage (e.g. Archmage Support ) where damage rolls can be exactly the same.
Trinity Support | Balancing Resonance | Since Resonance is shared between all Trinity support gems, two or more skills with different highest damage types can be used to achieve full Resonance, instead of alternating damage types with a single skill.

Unearth | Infobox | Spell , Projectile , AoE , Physical Level: (1-20) Cost: (4-10) Mana Cast Time: 0.30 sec Critical Strike Chance: 6.00% Effectiveness of Added Damage: 140%. Requires Level 10. Fires a projectile that will pierce through enemies to impact the ground at the targeted location, creating a Bone Archer corpse where it lands. Deals (15-607) to (22-911) Physical Damage Projectiles Pierce all Targets Spawned corpses are Level (10-70) Maximum of 10 corpses allowed Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Corpses Spawned have (1-20)% increased Maximum Life. Place into an item socket of the right colour to gain this skill. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 10
Unearth | Description | Unearth is a spell skill that fires multiple corpses in a projectile that pierces and lands to deal physical area of effect damage.
Unearth | Skill functions and interactions | Projectile Behaviours: By default, the projectiles created by this skill travel to the targeted location before creating a corpse and dealing area damage. Projectiles that split , fork , chain or return have a maximum travel distance equal to the original distance between the caster and the targeted location - this includes the distance already traveled, thus if the enemy is near to the targeted location corpses will be created close to the enemy, while if the targeted location is far behind the enemy those projectiles will travel further. Projectiles can only fork or chain if pierce is disabled by an effect such as Snakepit. Due to the ground targeting effect, projectiles will not return after reaching the targeted location, but will return after hitting an impassable obstacle.
Unearth | Skill functions and interactions | Corpse creation: Corpses are only created once a projectile hits the ground at the end of flight, not when it hits a target. Corpses created by Unearth are specifically those of Bone Archers. These monsters can be reanimated with Raise Spectre.
Unearth | Skill functions and interactions | Shotgunning: An enemy that has been hit by a projectile from Unearth cannot be hit by any other projectile created at the same time, including the AoE damage from those projectiles impacting the ground. If no projectile hits a target, the AoE damage from all projectiles ground impacts can hit the same target. The Fledgling can prevent projectiles from hitting a target, allowing this skill to shotgun against nearby targets. [1]
Unearth | Skill functions and interactions | Unearth is currently the fastest non- cooldown non- channelling spell that is not a totem or mine.

Cannot support skills with cooldowns, triggered skills, Brand skills, Vaal skills, channelling skills, skills with a reservation, or skills used by totems, traps, or mines. Cannot modify the skills of minions. Supported Spells gain a Seal every (0.90-0.71) seconds, to a maximum of 3 Seals Supported Spells are Unsealed when cast, and their effects Reoccur for each Seal lost Supported Skills deal (45-36)% less Damage when Reoccurring Additional Effects From 1-20% Quality: Supported Skills deal (0.5-10)% increased Spell Damage. This is a Support Gem. It does not grant a bonus to your character, but to skills in sockets connected to it. Place into an item socket connected to a socket containing the Skill Gem you wish to augment. Right click to remove from a socket. Acquisition Drop level: 38
Unleash Support | Description | Unleash Support is a support gem which causes the supported spell to generate Seals while not being cast. The next time the user casts the spell, the spell will consume the Seals it stored to create additional skill effects in rapid succession.
Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | The gem's advanced description specifies (Spell cast time is 10% longer for each time the Spell reoccurs).
Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | While the spell "reoccurs", your character will stand in place while playing the casting animation. Note that reoccurring effects do not count as separate casts , and will therefore not trigger any on-cast abilities such as Intensify Support stacks. Reoccurring also does not count as repeating.
Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | Due to how this support gem functions, to achieve maximum DPS potential, casting intervals need to be timed to allow for generation for maximum Seals between casts. As increase cast speed is generally also a DPS increase, using Unleash may be detrimental in some scenarios, and the player should ensure these factors have been compared.
Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | The absolute dps increase from the gem can be calculated from 2 factors, the amount of seal casts per second and the damage reduction:
Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | 1 − less damage seal cooldown = ( 1 − less damage ) ∗ seal casts per second = full damage casts \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}{\dfrac {1-{\text{less damage}}}{\text{seal cooldown}}}=(1-{\text{less damage}})*{\text{seal casts per second}}={\text{full damage casts}}
Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | For a level 20 gem this gives you the following properties:
Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | 1 − 0.41 0.71 seconds seal cooldown = ( 1 − 0.41 ) ∗ 1.41 seal casts per second = 0.831 full damage casts each second \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}{\dfrac {1-{\text{0.41}}}{\text{0.71 seconds seal cooldown}}}=(1-0.41)*1.41{\text{ seal casts per second}}=0.831{\text{ full damage casts each second}}
Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | The gems relative damage increase depends on the cast time of the spell it supports:
Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | full damage casts manual cast rate = full damage casts ∗ manual cast time \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}{\dfrac {\text{full damage casts}}{\text{ manual cast rate}}}={\text{full damage casts}}*{\text{manual cast time}}
Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | With a level 20 gem this is
Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | 0.831 manual cast rate \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}{\dfrac {0.831}{\text{ manual cast rate}}}
Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | So if your PoB says your cast rate is 5.34, then unleash support would increase your dps by 15.56%, if you stand in place and constantly cast.
Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | The gem particularly excels in builds where kiting and movement have precedence over constantly casting, or the cast rate for a spell is relatively low.
Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | For a skill with 0.5 cast time, which means 2 casts per second while standing still, the relative dps increase is 41.55%, if you stand in place and constantly cast.
Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | The maximum dps increase, when you are dodging constantly and only cast the spell when it has 3 seals, would be 3 ∗ ( 100 % − damage reduction per seal cast ) = 177 % \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}{3*(100\%-{\text{ damage reduction per seal cast}})=177\%} compared to the same amount of dodging and only casting the same spell once and dodging the same amount of time.
Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | Casting the spell constantly does not seem to interrupt seal generation, instead the support gem seems to add an internal cooldown similar to for example Flame Dash es usage storage, but without the visual indication of a clockwise indicator showing when the next seal will be generated.
Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | The reoccurrences don't cost extra mana. Which means you can get 4 casts at 3 seals, worth 2.77 casts of damage for the mana price of 1.5 casts, because of the 150% mana cost multiplier, which can increases your damage per mana spend by 85%. The mana effectiveness increase is:
Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | casts per second + 0.83 casts per second ∗ 1.5 = 1 + 0.83 ∗ cast time 1.5 \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}{\dfrac {{\text{casts per second}}+0.83}{{\text{casts per second}}*1.5}}={\dfrac {1+0.83*{\text{cast time}}}{1.5}}
Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | The mana effectiveness for any level is:
Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | 1 + 1 − less damage seal cooldown ∗ min(cast time, 3 ∗ seal cooldown ) 1.5 \color [rgb]{0.6392156862745098,0.5529411764705883,0.42745098039215684}{\dfrac {1+{\dfrac {1-{\text{less damage}}}{\text{seal cooldown}}}*{\text{min(cast time, 3}}*{\text{seal cooldown}})}{1.5}}
Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | Getting passive casts from this support gem, that all unleash once you get time to cast could in theory be used to stack the dps of a low number of gems that have no cooldown and that one would normally continuously cast to kill an enemy.
Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | Alternatively, instead of aiming for maximising dps, this support gem can be used with Summon Raging Spirit and other similar skills, which the player can use the stored seal to cast more SRS in one cast. Supported skills deal less damage when reoccuring tag only affects the spell part of SRS which deals no damage. Actual minion damage is not affected by the penalty during reoccuring.
Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | It is currently intended that Unleash cannot support Summon Skeletons , as the skill can potentially have cooldown due to From Dust unique jewel. [1]
Unleash Support | Skill functions and interactions | Critical strikes are rolled once per skill use.

Vaal Absolution | Description | Vaal Absolution is a Minion Vaal spell that causes one of your Sentinels of Absolution to ascend into an apparition of Innocence , causing it to gain new spells, overrides its durations, and improves its damage and damage reduction. Using this skill while an Ascended Sentinel is currently active will refresh its duration.
Vaal Absolution | Skill functions and interactions | The Ascended Sentinel does not count towards your regular Sentinel of Absolution limit and instead has a unique limit of 1 (meaning it's possible to have 3 regular sentinels + 1 ascended sentinel), and innately deals 200% more Damage and takes 70% less Damage. Any buffs and debuffs on your Sentinel before it is Ascended is retained when transformed. The chosen Sentinel's minion duration is reset when ascending, and recasting this spell refreshes its duration.
Vaal Absolution | Skill functions and interactions | Vaal Absolution can only be cast on Sentinels of Absolution created from the base skill granted by that specific gem. [1]

Vaal Arctic Armour | Description | Vaal Arctic Armour is a Vaal spell that encases you in ice, freezing you and preventing any active movement or action, but granting you a massive reduction to damage taken from hits for a duration or until you take a certain number of damaging hits, as well as regenerating mana and energy shield during the effect.
Vaal Arctic Armour | Skill functions and interactions | You count as Frozen while encased in ice, even if the player is Immune to or Unaffected by Freeze. This can enable certain self-freeze mechanics, such as Winterheart.
Vaal Arctic Armour | Skill functions and interactions | You cannot directly move or perform non- instant actions while encased in ice regardless of ailment defences against Freeze, including entering through a portal. This cannot be bypassed. Triggered skills and Frostblink are still usable to move around.
Vaal Arctic Armour | Skill functions and interactions | Vaal Arctic Armour's mana and energy shield regeneration counts as a buff and is affected by Ichimonji and Arctic Armour has #% increased Buff Effect from Eldritch implicit modifiers and The Snowblind Grace. The encased in ice and less damage taken stats are considered a skill effect rather than a buff, and therefore cannot be scaled further by buff effect.
Vaal Arctic Armour | Item acquisition | Vaal Arctic Armour has restrictions on where or how it can drop. Vaal Arctic Armour can drop from the Vaal Vessels found in Vaal side areas , or by corrupting Arctic Armour.

This area is considered chilling ground.
Vaal Cold Snap | Skill functions and interactions | The Damage over Time interacts with spell damage like most DoT skills. It also interacts with multiple sources of cold DoT. Stacking Creeping Frost , Cold Snap , Vortex , or any other source of Cold Damage over Time will work together.
Vaal Cold Snap | Skill functions and interactions | Multiple activation of the skill by other players will stack the burst but not the DoT portion of this skill.

Vaal Discipline | Description | Vaal Discipline is a vaal aura that prevents Energy Shield recharge being delayed by damage for the player and their allies.
Vaal Discipline | Skill Functions and Interactions | Your and nearby allies' Energy Shield Recharges are not delayed by Damage : This stat bypasses the delay to start energy shield recharge and causes energy shield recharge to begin immediately after being interrupted by taking damage. This also means there is effectively no delay from constant damage effects such as damage over time , effectively granting an effect similar to Wicked Ward 's Energy Shield Recharge is not interrupted by Damage during the aura's duration. Taking damage while energy shield is already recharging under this effect will not count as energy shield recharge starting again (e.g. for Ascent From Flesh ).
Vaal Discipline | Skill Functions and Interactions | Using Vaal Discipline will also cause energy shield recharge to start immediately.
Vaal Discipline | Item acquisition | Vaal Discipline has restrictions on where or how it can drop. Vaal Discipline can drop from the Vaal Vessels found in Vaal side areas.
Vaal Discipline | Recipes | Vaal Discipline can be created from the following recipes:
Vaal Discipline | Related helmet enchantments | The following helmet enchantments affect Vaal Discipline.
